data.document_purpose: Active + recently-closed structured work — Tasks and Subtasks following the Taskmaster JSON shape. Provides traceability + observability: Task and Subtask state (pending / in_progress / done / blocked / deferred / cancelled) is the canonical record of session work — no per-session test files or STATUS docs needed. Closed Tasks remain in the file with status `done` + `<info added on …>` journal blocks per PRODUCT inv 13, surfacing what shipped and why. Removal is rare (only for `cancelled` or when an item is reclassified to backlog).

tasks.title: Implement Astro+Starlight docs site + Warp docubot port + decommission /update-docs
tasks.description:
**COMPOUND TASK — four concerns in one delivery:**

1. **IMPLEMENT** the Astro+Starlight docs site per the existing DRAFT spec at `docs/specs/astro-starlight-docs-foundation/` (PRODUCT.md + TECH.md authored S47 — DRAFT, not yet ratified). This is the BASE site implementation: Astro + Starlight integration, content collections, branded site, deploy pipeline.

2. **PORT** Warp's docubot auto-sync approach + integrate six Warp docs skills into `.claude/skills/`. The mechanism: extract ontology + schema + reference docs from code, regenerate docs site on push. NO spec exists yet for the port — Subtask N.1 RESEARCH captures the docubot mechanism + 6 skills inventory; Subtasks N.2 PRODUCT + N.3 TECH extend the existing Astro+Starlight spec foundation with the auto-sync layer.

3. **DECOMMISSION** the bespoke `.claude/skills/update-docs/` skill once the new platform is live (replaced by docubot auto-sync). Removes the per-session manual updating pattern.

Also UNLOCKS the main-track Ontology auto-sync (critical for upcoming schema-related work — the same docubot mechanism extracts the ontology graph from code).

Status `spec_needed` reflects that the base Astro+Starlight DRAFT exists but isn't ratified, AND the docubot+skills extension isn't specced. The {N.1}/{N.2}/{N.3}/{N.4} spec-authoring chain below covers what's missing.

## Acceptance criteria

- Astro+Starlight site stood up per ratified PRODUCT.md + TECH.md (existing DRAFT extended via N.2 + N.3).
- Warp docubot auto-sync pattern ported with research output at `docs/research/warp-docubot-and-docs-skills.md`.
- Six Warp docs skills ported into `.claude/skills/` (skill list deferred to N.4 PLAN.md).
- `.claude/skills/update-docs/` decommissioned (file deleted; consumers redirected to the new platform).
- Main-track Ontology auto-sync unlocked + integrated.

## Dependencies

ID-6 (workflow-orchestration skill body); ID-7 (task-planner agent); ID-8 (implement-subtask skill). All landed in S51.

subtasks.title: RESEARCH — Warp docubot mechanism + six Warp docs skills inventory
subtasks.description: Capture how Warp's docubot extracts + regenerates docs, and which six docs skills they ship. Output to a RESEARCH.md.
subtasks.details:
TO BE POPULATED via Planner at {N.1} dispatch. Out of scope for S51 authoring — body lands when this Task moves to active work.

Expected scope:
- Source: Warp's open docs (search for warp.dev docubot + docs-related skills).
- Output: `docs/research/warp-docubot-and-docs-skills.md`.
- Mechanism extraction: how does docubot detect changes, regenerate, sync?
- Skill inventory: which six skills, their triggers, their outputs.

<info added on 20/05/2026 ~21:55>
RESEARCH complete. Doc landed at `docs/research/docs-site-rebuild-research.md` (716 lines / 7677 words).

Sections delivered (all four mandatory sections populated):
- §1 Warp docs skills inventory — all 6 skills documented with full SKILL.md frontmatter (review-docs-pr, update-changelog, sync-error-docs, missing_docs, check_for_broken_links, docs-seo-audit). Actual location confirmed at `github.com/warpdotdev/docs/.agents/skills/` (not `.warp/skills/` as the S46 assessment guessed). Directory has 24 skills total; the 6 are the assessment-§8 highlights.
- §2 docubot mechanism — triggers, composite action structure (7 steps), envsubst'd prompt template (79 lines verbatim shape), Claude Agent SDK integration choice (per Liam's S46 preference), doc-PR-open flow, Phase 2 composability with the 6 skills.
- §3 KH docs/ IA audit at HEAD `3e4ee5c2` — 16 subdirectories inventoried, 478 live .md files. Cross-referenced with .planning/.archive/ (333 audits + 239 prompts + 319 specs + 75 research archived). 5 canonical front-door candidates identified: reference/, runbooks/, ontology/, product-functionality/, design/. Four-space IA proposal: platform/, reference/, runbooks/, decisions/.
- §4 Delta vs stale specs — 11 valid items preserved, 9 stale items flagged, 6 missing topic areas enumerated (docubot port, 6-skill port, /update-docs decommission, keep-docs-in-sync skill, KH AGENTS.md, hybrid sync contract), 9 PRODUCT invariants needing refresh + 6 new invariants proposed.

OQs surfaced (5 of 5 ceiling used):
- OQ 1: Author KH AGENTS.md inside Task 9 (default A) or carve off as Task 23 (B)? Default A.
- OQ 2: Hybrid sync primary mechanism (build / docubot / schema-driven)? Default: docubot PRIMARY in steady state.
- OQ 3: update-changelog KH equivalent — skip / fresh / defer? Default: defer.
- OQ 4: Four-space IA — ratify or alternative? Default: ratify as proposed.
- OQ 5: /update-docs decommission ordering — Big-Bang / gradual? Default: gradual with hard deadline.

Surprises:
- Actual Warp skills directory is .agents/skills/, not .warp/skills/ (assessment caveat).
- Docubot composite action snapshot at /development/warp/.github/actions/docubot/ predates the GitBook→Astro pivot — still references warpdotdev/gitbook. Internal action likely updated post-pivot.
- The stale TECH.md's first-sync-target `docs/specs/wp6-ontology-harness/` does NOT exist at HEAD `3e4ee5c2` — likely lives on main-track only.

Out-of-scope findings handed to 9.2 Planner: PRODUCT.md authoring, TECH.md authoring, Subtask decomposition, architectural decisions (surfaced as OQs).
</info added on 20/05/2026 ~21:55>

<info added on 2026-05-21T00:08:00.000Z>
**S58 — 9.1 done.** RESEARCH artefact `docs/research/docs-site-rebuild-research.md` (716 lines / 7677 words) landed. 6 Warp docs skills inventoried + docubot mechanism + current KH docs/ IA audit + delta vs stale Astro+Starlight specs.

**Liam ratifications of 5 OQs (3 defaults + 2 overrides):**
- **OQ 1 AGENTS.md location** — Author within ID-9 as `{9.5+}` (default). Six-skill ports depend on it; absorbing scope-bloat preferable to half-functional skill set.
- **OQ 2 Hybrid sync priority** — PR-merge docubot PRIMARY; build-time dev-loop; schema-driven layered (default). PR-flow is production critical path.
- **OQ 3 update-changelog disposition** — **OVERRIDE: skip entirely**, document as Warp-specific (default was "defer"). Cleaner scope; KH doesn't need this category.
- **OQ 4 IA proposal** — **OVERRIDE: five spaces** split `platform/` into `product-functionality/` + `ontology/` (default was four spaces). Better separates user-facing product docs from canonical vocabularies; aligns with existing docs/product-functionality/ + docs/ontology/ directories.
- **OQ 5 decommission ordering** — Gradual: 2-session overlap then delete (default). Verification session 1 = docubot narrative faithfulness; session 2 = CI integration.

**Findings + surprises (for {9.2} Planner awareness, no action here):**
- "Six skills" was actually 24 in `.agents/skills/` — 6 was the assessment-§8 highlight set. Phase-3 expansion could revisit other 18.
- docubot composite action references `warpdotdev/gitbook` (pre-Astro pivot snapshot); KH port targets post-pivot shape.
- AGENTS.md-at-repo-root is Warp's "single source of truth all agents read" convention — leverage point beyond just docs skills.
- Cross-space links in `check_for_broken_links` require ABSOLUTE URLs (relative paths don't traverse Starlight spaces) — architectural constraint for the now-five-space IA.
- `wp6-ontology-harness` first-sync target was already broken at stale-TECH authoring time — {9.2} picks new first-sync from extant targets.

**Inputs to {9.2} PRODUCT (next session):** OQ ratifications above + the 4-section RESEARCH doc + 6-Warp-skills inventory with frontmatter + docubot mechanism cite-paths + KH IA audit (16 docs/ subdirs / 478 .md / archive cross-ref).

Status `in_progress` → `done`.
</info added on 2026-05-21T00:08:00.000Z>

tasks.title: ast-dataflow handover review + worktree closure
tasks.description:
The ast-dataflow track merged its in-flight work to production-readiness during S51 (R-WP-S11-D, merge commit 03392948 + ast-track commits including ffc46cf7). A handover document was authored at `docs/specs/ast-dataflow-tool/HANDOVER.md` (154 lines, commit 16b6a5bb) covering: cross-track merge mechanics, the nine-session arc (kh-ast-S1 → S11), OPS-T1 codemod specs landed, six AST-prefixed backlog items now on production-readiness (since renumbered to ID-N via Task ID-15 migration), three S11 gotchas, carried-forward Liam ratifications, and read-first order for follow-on work.

This Task reviews the handover at session start, validates the 6 follow-on backlog items are correctly captured + sequenced, confirms the ast-dataflow worktree at `/Users/liamj/Documents/development/knowledge-hub-ast-dataflow` can be closed, and runs the worktree teardown.

## Acceptance criteria

- HANDOVER.md read in full; key points (TL;DR + section 2 merge contents + section 5 follow-on items + section 6 read-first order) captured into S52+ working context.
- 6 ast-dataflow follow-on backlog items verified present on production-readiness (post Task ID-15 migration, these will be renumbered from AST-S{N}-O{M} to ID-N format).
- Three S11 gotchas (worktree-isolation CWD drift, freshness-guard merge edge case, concurrent-dev reconciliation pattern) cross-checked against CLAUDE.md — added if not present.
- Worktree teardown: `git worktree remove /Users/liamj/Documents/development/knowledge-hub-ast-dataflow` (with `--force` only if Liam confirms no uncommitted work). Branch `ast-dataflow-tooling` deleted only after confirmation (post-merge tip SHA preserved in HANDOVER.md table for git-archaeology recovery).
- `git worktree list` post-teardown shows three top-level worktrees: main, production-readiness, kh-knowledge-platform.

## Dependencies

None (independent of in-flight WPs); ideally lands early in S52 so context is fresh before any AST-related work picks up.

subtasks.title: Add missing S11 gotchas to CLAUDE.md if not present
subtasks.description: Cross-check the three S11 gotchas from HANDOVER.md against CLAUDE.md and add any missing entries.
subtasks.details:
Three S11 gotchas to check:
1. Worktree-isolation CWD drift (likely already in CLAUDE.md — verify wording).
2. Freshness-guard merge edge case (combined-diff format does not register single-parent last_updated additions on merge commits — this is the bug behind kh-ast-S11 R-WP-S11-D freshness-guard bump 744d9ef1).
3. Concurrent-dev reconciliation pattern (when two worktrees touch the same file).

If any are missing, add to CLAUDE.md "Gotchas" section. Reference HANDOVER.md §6 for exact wording.

<info added on 2026-05-18T21:00:00.000Z>
Cross-check confirmed: NONE of the three S11 gotchas were previously in CLAUDE.md. CLAUDE.md HAD related but distinct gotchas ("Bash CWD drifts into worktree dirs after Read" + "Sub-agent instructions must always use relative paths") covering different failure modes. Added all three S11 gotchas to CLAUDE.md "Worktree isolation rules" sub-section, with specific commit references per HANDOVER.md §8:
1. `isolation: "worktree"` sub-agents can still commit to parent worktree (cites commits 6dbf9da2 + af9fe05c from kh-ast-S11 R-WP-S11-C as evidence; mitigation = prefix `cd $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel) &&` after Read + verify pwd).
2. Reference-doc freshness guard fails on merge commits (combined-diff format; workaround = follow-up single-parent commit per `744d9ef1` precedent; or `[skip-doc-freshness-guard]` body tag).
3. Concurrent commits on production-readiness during cross-track merge (cites b6f7d55f + 80944a09 + 831d9e74; pattern = fresh worktree off production-readiness + reconcile-merge pattern per `docs/runbooks/ast-dataflow-merge-S11.md`).
</info added on 2026-05-18T21:00:00.000Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: CLAUDE.md contains all three gotchas with reference to HANDOVER.md §6.

tasks.title: Bulk ID-N format migration across roadmap + backlog + task-list
tasks.description:
Migrate all legacy-format item IDs across the three task surfaces (roadmap, backlog, task-list) to the canonical ID-N / ID-N.M format per s48-feedback.md B2. Legacy formats in scope: `OPS-*` (operational tech-debt), `AST-S{N}-O{M}` (ast-dataflow follow-ons), `C{N}-T{M}-*` / `C{N}-DT-*` / `C{N}-PA{N}` (design-critique-audit clusters), `R-WP-S{N}-{X}` (cross-track work-package), `RLS-P{N}` (RLS phase items), and others surfaced during inventory. The migration script produces a deterministic mapping (legacy → ID-N) and applies it atomically to all three JSON files + their cross-doc references.

## Acceptance criteria

- Inventory of legacy IDs across all three surfaces (count + format breakdown) surfaced before mapping decisions.
- Mapping table reviewed by Liam (ratification gate) — preserves semantic continuity (e.g. tightly-grouped clusters get sequential ID-N blocks).
- Migration script (`scripts/migrate-legacy-ids-to-id-n.ts`) idempotent, dry-run-able, applies mapping in place.
- All three JSON files validate against their schemas post-migration.
- Cross-doc reference sweep (`docs/`, `__tests__/`, `.claude/skills/`, `.claude/agents/`, `scripts/`, `lib/`) rewrites legacy ID matches to new ID-N.
- Roundtrip tests (`roadmap-roundtrip.test.ts`, etc.) pass post-migration.
- Migration script deleted post-merge per the migrate-roadmap-section-3.ts precedent.

## Dependencies

None (independent of in-flight work — but should land before ID-N is heavily consumed in new agent/skill bodies to avoid mid-stream confusion).

**Required reading:**

**Required output:** `docs/research/cross-surface-schema-alignment.md` (~400-600 lines). Sections:
1. Context — s48 WP1 intent vs what landed
2. Field-level audit across 3 schemas (extending the quick audit done at S56 — find every divergence; identify which were intentional vs accidental drift)
3. What's actually been done (mempalace + git log evidence)

9. Risk register — what breaks if alignment goes wrong (canonical pipeline migration next session is downstream)
10. Open questions for Liam ratification (≤ 5)

**Critical constraints:**
- Worktree first action: `pwd && git branch --show-current && git fetch origin production-readiness && git reset --hard origin/production-readiness && git branch --show-current` (no cd prefix)
- File ownership: ONLY `docs/research/cross-surface-schema-alignment.md` (NEW) + `docs/reference/task-list.json` (status flip 15.6 pending → in_progress + journal block + Task updatedAt + last_updated)

subtasks.title: Phase B — Schema fixes (drift remediation)
subtasks.description: Fix the live schema-data drift surfaced in 15.6 §2.5 + §2.7: (a) `BacklogItem.surfaced` schema is `z.string().min(1)` but 8 items carry `surfaced: null` — relax to nullable OR migrate those items to populate surfaced; (b) `BacklogItemType` enum accepts only `tech_debt` (underscore) but 9 items use `tech-debt` (dash) — pick one canonical form and migrate; (c) `RoadmapPriority` alignment per §2.4 of research doc.
subtasks.details:
Per OQ-2 ratification: backlog migration aligns DOWN to bare-digit ID format. This Subtask follows Phase A apply (15.4) so the schema work lands on already-migrated data.

**Scope (per research doc §2.5 + §2.7 + §6.1):**
1. `BacklogItem.surfaced` — relax schema from `z.string().min(1)` to `z.string().min(1).nullable()` OR audit + populate the 8 null-surfaced items (53, 47, 42, 43, 44, 59, 51, 45) with retrospective provenance. Per OQ-4 ratification we're adding `session_refs[]` + `commit_refs[]` + `cross_doc_links[]` and dropping `surfaced` — so the fix is to DROP the `surfaced` field entirely from schema + data, replacing with the structured provenance fields populated where possible from item history.
2. `BacklogItemType` enum — research doc §2.7 catalogued 9-item `tech-debt` / `tech_debt` drift. Pick canonical form (recommend `tech-debt` — kebab-case matches all sibling enums) + migrate the underscore variants OR vice versa.
3. `RoadmapPriority` alignment per §2.4 — verify roadmap status / priority enums use the shared `work-status.ts` module (S50 WP1 ratified); fix any item-level priority drift.

**File ownership:** `lib/validation/backlog-schema.ts`, `lib/validation/roadmap-schema.ts`, `docs/reference/product-backlog.json` (8 items × surfaced→structured + 9 items × enum harmonisation), `docs/reference/product-roadmap.json` (if any priority drift surfaces). NO touches to task-list.json beyond status flips on this Subtask itself.

**Test gate:** existing schema roundtrip tests + new tests asserting the dropped/added/relaxed fields work; `bun run test` clean.

<info added on 2026-05-21T01:30:00.000Z>

- Slice 1 (§B.1): Dropped `surfaced` field from backlog-schema.ts + all 47 product-backlog.json items. Added `session_refs`, `commit_refs`, `cross_doc_links` (DocLinkSchema) to both schema and all 47 items (empty arrays). 12 new B-INV-1/2 tests added. VALID_ITEM_BASE updated in test fixture. 40 tests pass.

subtasks.details:
Promoted from backlog id=67 during S60 W6 (ID-15.10 Phase E demonstration). Work already shipped at S60 W4 (commits 936cb341 + 5e893f8a).

**Source context (carried from backlog id=67):** Surfaced by WP1 fix-executor (af454d35) + Checker (af30b781) + re-Checker (a7ef9733) on ID-15.7 (S59). **What shipped (commit 936cb341):** Resolved dup id=23 in product-backlog.json (canonical-pipeline-followup defer-rename entry renamed id=23 → id=70). Added .superRefine() on BacklogSchema in lib/validation/backlog-schema.ts rejecting documents with non-unique item ids, emitting clear error message naming the duplicate id(s). Added 6-case test block in __tests__/validation/backlog-schema.test.ts covering single dup, three-item dup, multi-distinct-dup, empty, and single-item acceptance paths.

**Discovery:** Zod refine() with function-as-second-arg does NOT surface custom message (returns generic 'Invalid input'). superRefine with ctx.addIssue() is the correct pattern for dynamic error messages.

Per kh-prod-readiness-S52 WP5 meta-skill evaluation (`docs/plans/phase-0-investigation/meta-skill-evaluation.md`), Option C ratified: author a `docs/reference/skill-routing-map.md` reference doc that lists `Task tilt → recommended skill set` so the Orchestrator (or Liam) can look up which task-specific skills to name in each dispatch brief. Closes the Liam-S51-close question about an Orchestrator meta-skill. Single-Subtask Task — the doc IS the spec; no PRODUCT/TECH chain needed.

## Acceptance criteria

- `docs/reference/skill-routing-map.md` exists with columns: Task tilt | Required skills | Conditional skills | Anti-patterns | Example Tasks.
- 8–10 tilt rows covering: AI, CI, Supabase, Frontend (React), Frontend (Astro), Data-pipeline, Testing (Playwright), Documentation, Security/RLS, Workflow tooling.
- `workflow-orchestration` SKILL.md §4.4 updated: replace 5-tilt example list with map pointer; preserve user-driven framing.
- `task-planner.md` §4.1 (RESEARCH workflow) references the map.
- CLAUDE.md "Key Reference Documents" table gains a row.
- Freshness-coupling registration added so the map updates when skills are added / deleted.
- Map references the meta-skill-evaluation.md Q-META-1/2/3 ratification answers (Liam at session close).

## Dependencies

None — fully self-contained. Slate for a doc-light session.

tasks.title: Worktree-isolation leak hardening (Tiers 1-4 + propagation discipline)
tasks.description:
Per `docs/research/worktree-isolation-synthesis-and-action-plan.md` (synthesis of Track A forensics + Track B Claude docs review at S53 close). Deterministic cause pinned: a sub-agent leaks commits to the parent track branch (typically `production-readiness`) if and only if it issues a bash command that contains `cd /Users/liamj/Documents/development/knowledge-hub*` or `git -C` with that path, or passes that absolute path to Edit/Write `file_path`. Bash shell state does not persist between Bash tool calls — every call runs in harness's default cwd (the worktree). Sub-agents that never `cd` and use only relative paths mechanically cannot leak.

## Acceptance criteria

- Tier 1: brief / spec / skill discipline applied — `task-executor.md` Step 1 no-cd verification gate; `task-planner.md` + `task-checker.md` + `workflow-curator.md` no-cd rule in Operating principles; `start-session/SKILL.md` sub-agent guard + absolute-path purge; `workflow-orchestration/SKILL.md` worktree-isolation discipline rewrite + dispatch-brief composition update; CLAUDE.md gotcha rewrite with deterministic-cause framing.
- Tier 2: `.claude/settings.json` PreToolUse hooks block `Bash` containing `cd /Users/liamj/Documents/development/knowledge-hub*` or `git -C /Users/liamj/Documents/development/knowledge-hub*` — exit code 2 with explicit error message.
- Tier 3: `task-checker.md` first verification step runs dual-log gate (executor worktree vs parent track) and emits `branch-leak` FAIL finding if SHA appears on parent.
- Tier 4: `.claude/settings.json` permissions.allow rules for `Bash(supabase *)`, `Bash(/opt/homebrew/bin/supabase *)`, `Bash(python3 -m pytest *)`, `Bash(python3 -m playwright *)` to reduce sandbox-bypass friction.
- S54 A/B verification dispatch confirms the hooks fire and the briefs don't trigger them (Subtask 19.2).
- Tier 2.2 (added S57 19.3): `.claude/settings.json` PreToolUse hook on `Write|Edit|MultiEdit` matching absolute `/Users/liamj/Documents/development/knowledge-hub*` file_path that is OUTSIDE the agent's CWD — exit code 2 with explicit message. Closes the latent leak class surfaced in S57 WP1 forensic.
- Propagation discipline (added S59 19.4): hooks live in `.claude/settings.json` per branch; sub-agent worktrees inherit MAIN branch state at creation. Pre-dispatch check: `git show origin/main:.claude/settings.json | grep -c "Tier 2.2 hook (ID-19.3)"` must be ≥1. Brief-authoring discipline: relative paths only; never mention sibling worktree absolute paths in dispatch text (primer effect). Full analysis: `docs/research/hook-cwd-prefix-gap-investigation.md`.

## Dependencies

None — defensive hardening, independent of in-flight feature work.

subtasks.title: Tier 2.2 — PreToolUse hook on Write/Edit/MultiEdit absolute KH paths
subtasks.description: Implement the deferred Tier 2.2 hook from `docs/research/worktree-isolation-synthesis-and-action-plan.md` §3. The S57 WP1 forensic (transcript review at `/private/tmp/.../tasks/a220402b7568c4fdc.output` summary) confirmed Tier 2.2 was specified but never shipped — Tier 2.1 (git-commit guard) and Tier 2.3 (Bash cd guard) shipped at S53, Tier 2.2 (Write/Edit absolute-path guard) was missed. Closes the latent leak class that fired silently in WP1 (TECH.md landed in production-readiness from a knowledge-hub/worktrees/agent-acb CWD; agent self-recovered but mechanism had zero coverage).
subtasks.details:
Hook config landed in `.claude/settings.json` as a second PreToolUse matcher block alongside the existing Bash matcher.

**Hook logic:**
1. Extract `tool_input.file_path` and `cwd` from stdin JSON.
2. If `file_path` does NOT start with `/Users/liamj/Documents/development/knowledge-hub` — allow (relative or unrelated path).
3. If `file_path` starts with `$CWD/` or equals `$CWD` — allow (writing within own tree).
4. Otherwise — exit 2 with BLOCKED message naming both paths.

**Coverage:**
- Main session (Orchestrator) in production-readiness writes to production-readiness paths → CWD-prefix match → allow ✓
- Sub-agent in `*/worktrees/agent-X/` writes to its own tree → CWD-prefix match → allow ✓
- Sub-agent in `*/worktrees/agent-X/` writes to production-readiness or another worktree → no CWD-prefix match → block ✓
- Cross-tree absolute-path Read still permitted (the agent needs to read brief-pointed sources). This is the WP1 root-cause primer — but blocking Read would break dispatch briefs that point at production-readiness research docs. Acceptable risk: the Write/Edit block stops the leak from materialising.

**Hook contract verification:**
Per S53 mempalace note: hooks live at next session start (settings watcher does not hot-reload). In-session verification not possible; verification gate is the next worktree dispatch — if Tier 2.2 fires correctly on a probe attempt, the implementation is sound.

**Effort:** ~30min total (probe-design + hook impl + JSON validation + task-list updates + CLAUDE.md gotcha bump). Spike on hook stdin contract (whether Write/Edit PreToolUse receives `tool_input.file_path` the same way Bash receives `tool_input.command`) deferred — high confidence per Claude Code hook docs schema convention; verification at next dispatch.

**Files:**
- `.claude/settings.json` (PreToolUse hooks array — second matcher block added)
- `docs/reference/task-list.json` (this Subtask)
- `CLAUDE.md` (Worktree isolation rules section — append Tier 2.2 note)

<info added on 2026-05-20T23:30:00.000Z>

JSON-validated via `jq -e .`. No in-session probe possible (hook live next session); verification gate = next worktree dispatch.
</info added on 2026-05-20T23:30:00.000Z>

subtasks.title: Hook CWD-prefix gap — Tier 2.2 silent in sandboxed worker sessions
subtasks.description: S58 architectural finding from three independent worker leaks (T-B, T-C, Wave B Executor). Tier 2.2 PreToolUse hook (Write|Edit|MultiEdit) fires correctly in main orchestrator sessions but is silent in sub-Claude worker sessions dispatched via Agent tool isolation:worktree. Workers wrote to sibling-worktree paths (production-readiness) without hook intervention. Sandbox writeAllowOnly does NOT distinguish main repo from `-production-readiness` siblings (both prefix-matched). Three S58 leaks survived only because each one's leaked work was the intended deliverable.
subtasks.details:
TO BE POPULATED at S59+ via Planner. Likely scope: (a) investigate whether hooks load into sandboxed worker sessions at all; (b) if not, extend sandbox writeAllowOnly to be CWD-restrictive per sub-agent dispatch; (c) extend Tier 2.2 regex to block paths whose first non-prefix-matched segment is a sibling-worktree marker (e.g. `-production-readiness/`, `-staging/`); (d) document the gap explicitly in CLAUDE.md gotchas so brief authors avoid absolute-path references in dispatch text (primer effect mitigation).

Provenance: S58 leak evidence in journal blocks for 20.4 (T-B), 9.1 (T-C), 15.4 (Wave B Executor). Each leak surfaced same root cause: brief mentioned absolute production-readiness paths → primer effect on agent's Write tool calls → hook silent + sandbox permissive → write succeeds in wrong tree.

<info added on 2026-05-21 02:10:00>
**S59 W3 — Investigation closure. Root cause REFRAMED: hook propagation lag, NOT silent-in-sandbox.**

First WP3 investigator agent (a949c916) stalled at 600s no-progress watchdog but surfaced the critical finding before stall: sub-agent worktrees created via Agent isolation:worktree live under `/Users/liamj/.../knowledge-hub/.claude/worktrees/agent-XXX/` (under MAIN repo working tree). Claude Code reads project-root settings from sub-agent's CWD → relevant `.claude/settings.json` is the one at the worktree's HEAD, which derives from main branch state at worktree creation time.

Timeline verification (orchestrator-direct): Tier 2.2 hook commit fdaf47c6 landed on production-readiness at S57 close. S58 Wave A workers dispatched BEFORE the Phase 1 (P-R → CII) + Phase 2 (CII → main) alignment merge cascade. Main branch HEAD at S58 Wave A + Wave B dispatch times LACKED the hook. Sub-agent worktrees inherited this hook-less main state → no hook on disk → no protection. Sandbox writeAllowOnly is independently over-permissive (prefix `/Users/liamj/Documents/development/knowledge-hub` matches both main repo AND sibling worktrees). Combined: all 3 S58 leaks unprotected.

S58 W2 probe artefact (`docs/research/wp2-verification-artefacts/s58-tier-2-2-live-fire.md`) conclusion "hook does not fire in sandbox" was incorrect — the probe ran in an environment whose `.claude/settings.json` lacked the hook entirely. Hook absence on disk vs hook present-but-silent are observationally identical to the probe.

Research doc shipped: `docs/research/hook-cwd-prefix-gap-investigation.md` (~250 lines, 6 sections covering re-framed root cause, (a)(b)(c) sub-investigation answers, 3 ranked fixes, verification protocol, 3 OQs).

CLAUDE.md updates landed (in same commit a20ee48c):
  - Hook propagation discipline rule + pre-dispatch check (`git show origin/main:.claude/settings.json | grep -c "Tier 2.2 hook (ID-19.3)"` must be ≥1).
  - Brief-authoring discipline — relative paths only; never mention sibling worktree absolute paths in dispatch text (primer effect).

S58 W2 artefact addendum applied at <follow-up commit SHA>.

Backlog item ID-69 (per-sub-agent sandbox tightening, status=parked, harness-dependent) added at <follow-up commit SHA>.

Liam ratifications at S59 W3 close:
  - OQ-19.4-1 DEFAULT: close 19.4 as documented (no further code change).
  - OQ-19.4-2 DEFAULT: add corrective addendum to S58 W2 artefact.
  - OQ-19.4-3 DEFAULT: track sandbox tightening as backlog ID-69 (status=parked).

Status pending → done.
</info added on 2026-05-21 02:10:00>

tasks.title: Per-Task .md mirror generator + render surface
tasks.description:
Generate human-readable .md mirrors of each Task in task-list.json (one file per Task at docs/reference/tasks/ID-N.md) so Liam can read individual Tasks without loading the whole JSON into context, and render those mirrors in a UI surface that supports edit-back (text + status). Source design at docs/research/per-task-file-mirror-design.md. Cross-project tooling constraint per §7 of the design doc — generator + render surface must NOT live inside knowledge-hub/scripts/ or lib/ since this is workflow tooling, not a KH product. Likely target: ~/.claude/plugins/, ~/.claude/tools/, or a separate dev-tooling repo (decide in 20.4 TECH.md).

## Acceptance criteria

- Per-Task .md mirror file shape ratified (Q1-Q8 in design doc §3 answered by Liam).
- Rendering surface recommendation ratified (Plannotator fork vs kh-knowledge-platform legacy code vs new minimal UI, per 20.2 research).
- Generator script lives in cross-project location per §7.
- Mirror regeneration trigger ratified (manual / Stop hook / CI guard — design doc §2.4 default is B+C).
- UI surface supports view + edit of Task / Subtask text + status, with write-back to task-list.json (canonical) not just the mirror.
- Cross-project location choice does not break the existing parseTaskListWithWarnings ingress (workflow-orchestration's only canonical read path).

subtasks.title: RESEARCH — rendering surface options (Plannotator + kh-knowledge-platform legacy + new minimal)
subtasks.description: Catalogue rendering options for the per-Task .md mirrors. Liam wants view + EDIT (text + status), not just annotate.
subtasks.details:
Per per-task-file-mirror-design.md §8. Two known inputs:

1. **Plannotator** (Liam locally forked). Current capability: annotation on markdown files; takes filename as input. Gap: needs edit-in-place + structured field edit (status enum, priority enum). Research smallest fork delta to add edit mode.

2. **knowledge-hub-knowledge-platform** legacy work at /Users/liamj/Documents/development/knowledge-hub-knowledge-platform/. Built migrations + code rendering state files (backlog, roadmap) to platform UI. Catalogue relevant files, assess reusability as cross-project tooling vs KH-coupled.

Deliverable: docs/research/per-task-render-surface.md covering both inputs + recommendation (build on Plannotator fork, build on legacy, or new minimal UI) + cross-project tooling location implications per §7.

<info added on 2026-05-20T17:00:00.000Z>
What shipped: `docs/research/per-task-render-surface.md` (530 lines, 8 sections). Plannotator v0.19.18 investigated in full — annotation-only, no source write-back; three fork deltas catalogued (edit-in-place ~4-6h, structured field edit ~3-4h, JSON patch write-back ~2-3h, total ~9-13h). kh-knowledge-platform state-docs inventory: 11 files reviewed; all panels are Supabase/KH-auth coupled with read-only design per types/state-docs.ts comment; reusability nil/low. Leading recommendation: Option C (new minimal Bun/React tool, ~10h, clean cross-project packaging from day one). Fallback: Option A (Plannotator fork). Option B (kh-platform reuse) dominated. 7 open questions (OQ-1 dispositive: Option C vs Option A) gate ID-20.3 PRODUCT.md authoring. Cross-project location: ~/.claude/plugins/task-list-mirror/ for both options.
Commit: 68f43b37 (full SHA: 68f43b37).
Spec slice: per-task-file-mirror-design.md §8 (rendering surface research scope).

<info added on 2026-05-20T22:00:00.000Z>
**S57 dispatch begins — scope expanded per Liam ratifications.**

4 dispositive Qs ratified at session start:
1. **Render-surface architecture (was OQ-1):** Option D — fork+rename Plannotator standalone, NO upstream alignment. Repurpose existing UI primitives (Viewer + markdown parser + FrontmatterCard) by stripping annotation-only paths and adding edit-in-place + structured field edit + JSON write-back. ~6-10h estimated.
2. **Ledger scope (was implicit):** ALL 3 ledgers covered — task-list.json + product-roadmap.json + product-backlog.json. Tool dispatches renderer by filename/schema. ~1-2h per additional schema beyond first.
3. **Invocation (was OQ-3):** Plugin AND CLI — one server, two entry points. Plugin slash command inside Claude Code; CLI binary for async/between-session edits. Same `/api/...` endpoint regardless.
4. **Ledger relocation (was OQ-6 adjacent):** NEW Task ID-22 post-Phase A — move ledgers from docs/reference/ to .planning/task-management/. NOT folded into ID-20.

Remaining OQs all default per research docs (Planner empowered to surface override needs):
- File-mirror Q1-Q8: full verbose journal / no ID padding / CI block on drift / spec-doc backlinks / dependency links / cancelled-delete / done-same-shape / Stop hook every edit
- Render-surface OQ-2: direct write-back to JSON (canonical) — NOT to .md mirror
- OQ-4: NO state-machine enforcement (status enum present as dropdown, transitions not enforced)
- OQ-5: full raw `details` field editable as textarea (journal blocks visible but not specially gated)
- OQ-6 reframed: tool lives in its own repo (forked Plannotator rename) installable as plugin
- OQ-7: in-place mutation of pre-journal details content only; new journal blocks remain append-only-by-agents

**Tool name placeholder:** kh-ledger-viewer (Planner to propose final).

**PRODUCT.md scope:** ledger-mirror covering all 3 schemas, view + edit (text + status/priority/etc enum dropdowns) + write-back to canonical JSON, plugin+CLI invocation, regen mirror via Stop hook on JSON edit, cross-project tooling location.
</info added on 2026-05-20T22:00:00.000Z>

subtasks.title: Fork prep + strip + rename + vendor schemas (Slice 1, tool repo)
subtasks.description: Create new GitHub repo `task-view` from local Plannotator v0.19.18 fork. Strip 14 apps + 7 packages. Apply rename map. Vendor 4 KH Zod schemas into packages/schemas/. Standalone foundation Subtask — no other Subtask dependencies.
subtasks.details:
**Repo boundary: NEW external `task-view` GitHub repository (separate from knowledge-hub).** Source the local fork at `/Users/liamj/Documents/development/plannotator/`.

**Implements TECH §1.1-§1.5:**
- §1.1: bootstrap from upstream `backnotprop/plannotator` v0.19.18 (use existing local fork as source); 7 commits per the listed step (rename → strip → vendor → wire), tag `v0.1.0-task-view-prep`; retain dual MIT-OR-Apache-2.0 LICENSE; add AUTHORS.md attribution.
- §1.2 strip ledger: delete apps `apps/codex/`, `apps/copilot/`, `apps/gemini/`, `apps/marketing/`, `apps/portal/`, `apps/paste-service/`, `apps/pi-extension/`, `apps/vscode-extension/`, `apps/opencode-plugin/`, `apps/review/`, `apps/skills/`; rename `apps/hook/` → `apps/server/`; delete packages `packages/editor/`, `packages/review-editor/`, `packages/ai/`, `packages/server/tour/`; trim `packages/ui/` to keep only Viewer + parser + BlockRenderer + ListMarker + InlineMarkdown + TableOfContents + MermaidBlock + GraphvizBlock + CodeBlock + TableBlock + ThemeProvider (delete AnnotationPanel + AnnotationSidebar + AnnotationToolbar + AnnotationCard + CommentPopover + QuickLabelDropdown + EditorAnnotationCard + PinpointOverlay + ImageAnnotator + plan-diff/ + sidebar/ + goal-setup/ + most settings/ tabs + Landing + ModeToggle + Settings non-essential tabs); trim `packages/server/` to keep browser.ts + repo.ts + path-utils.ts + code-nav.ts + config.ts + draft.ts (re-purposed for localStorage) (delete annotate.ts + external-annotations.ts + editor-annotations.ts + claude-review.ts + codex-review.ts + review.ts + agent-jobs.ts + goal-setup.ts + image.ts + share-url.ts + vcs.ts + jj.ts + p4.ts + pr.ts + git.ts); trim `packages/shared/` to storage helpers only.
- §1.3 retained surface: explicit keep list per TECH (Viewer.tsx lines 140-630 minus FrontmatterCard which §4 replaces; parser.ts whole-cloth; slugify.ts for in-page anchors; ThemeProvider disconnected from KH semantic tokens).
- §1.4 rename map: sweep all identifiers `plannotator` → `task-view` per the explicit TECH table (npm pkg name, bin name, slash command, env vars, config dir, server entrypoint, FrontmatterCard → RecordFrontmatterCard, workspace pkg names).
- §1.5 vendor schemas: copy `lib/validation/task-list-schema.ts`, `roadmap-schema.ts`, `backlog-schema.ts`, `work-status.ts` (the 4-file bundle) from KH `lib/validation/` into `packages/schemas/src/`. Replace `@/lib/validation/...` import paths with `@task-view/schemas/...` within the vendored files. Document re-vendoring procedure in `CONTRIBUTING.md` (OQ-T2 ratified default: manual annotated bump).

**PRODUCT invariants covered:** 1 (fork identity + permanent divergence), 3 (zero KH runtime deps + vendored schemas).

**Estimated effort:** ~2-3h (largely mechanical strip + rename; the 25 kLOC → 10-12 kLOC reduction is well-bounded once the keep-list is established).

**Acceptance gate:** New GitHub repo `task-view` exists with no `upstream` remote set; LICENSE retains MIT+Apache dual; strip ledger applied per enumerated apps + packages; rename map applied via sweep (`rg plannotator` returns zero hits in renamed code paths); vendored schemas in `packages/schemas/src/` parse a representative KH `task-list.json` JSON without any `@/lib/validation/` imports; `bun test` in tool repo passes the inherited parser tests (parser.test.ts + InlineMarkdown.test.ts + diagramLanguages.test.ts + shortcuts.test.ts).

subtasks.title: CLI + plugin packaging + browser-close + port retry + --check flag (Slice 5, tool repo)
subtasks.description: CLI binary bin/task-view.js + plugin manifest .claude-plugin/plugin.json + shared-server wiring + --check flag for CI usage + browser-close detection (30s idle) + port retry (5 attempts).
subtasks.details:
**Repo boundary: external `task-view` repo.**

**Implements TECH §6.1-§6.7:**
- §6.1 CLI binary `bin/task-view.js`: per upstream Plannotator `bin/plannotator.js` pattern. Uses `parseArgs` from `node:util`. Positional path argument; flags: `--no-browser` (bool, do not auto-open browser; print URL only), `--port <N>` (string, override random port), `--check` (bool, run one-shot mirror-regen sanity pass + exit non-zero on drift; for CI use per inv 42). When no path positional → call `inferPathFromCwd()` per §2.3. Calls `startTaskViewServer({ path, port })` from `@task-view/server/ledger`. Calls `openBrowser(server.url)` unless `--no-browser`. Prints `Server ready at ${server.url} — close the tab to exit`. Awaits `server.waitForExit()`.
- §6.2 plugin manifest `.claude-plugin/plugin.json`: per Plannotator upstream + Claude plugin convention. Schema: `{ name: "task-view", version, description, commands: [{ name: "/task-view", description, script: "node_modules/.bin/task-view" }] }`. Slash command invocation in any Claude Code session: `/task-view [optional-path-to-ledger.json]`. Launches server, opens browser, prints readiness, waits for browser close per inv 41.
- §6.3 shared server rationale: CLI and plugin both call `startTaskViewServer(...)`. Same Bun process shape. Same routes. Plugin manifest invokes CLI binary; CLI binary calls into `@task-view/server/ledger`. There's no separate "plugin server" — the plugin is a thin manifest pointing at the binary.
- §6.4 `--check` flag for CI: `task-view --check <path>` runs one-shot: read canonical → detectSchema → generate mirrors idempotently → compare against existing mirrors on disk → exit 0 if no drift / non-zero with diff summary if drift. KH CI integration is 20.12 scope.
- §6.5 browser-close detection: server tracks last-request timestamp. If `now - last_request > 30 seconds` AND at least one request has been served (so slow browser start doesn't kill server pre-load), `process.exit(0)`. Threshold is a constant in `packages/server/ledger.ts`, NOT user-configurable per inv 50.
- §6.6 port retry policy: upstream `MAX_RETRIES = 5` carries forward unchanged. On port collision, retry with fresh random port up to 5 times, then exit with "could not bind" per inv 49.
- §6.7 $PATH install convention: documented in README.md. Routes: `bunx task-view` (one-shot, no install); `bun install -g task-view` (global to `~/.bun/install/global/...` + symlink); Claude plugin marketplace `claude plugins install task-view` (installs to `~/.claude/plugins/task-view/`). Per OQ-T1 ratified default, v0.1.0 ships as GitHub Release tarball: `bun install -g github:liam-jons/task-view` works against the tagged release URL. npm + marketplace are FU-2.

**PRODUCT invariants covered:** 41 (plugin slash command launches + opens browser + prints readiness + waits for close), 42 (CLI binary same + `--no-browser` / `--port` / `--check` flags), 43 (no-path invocation scans CWD per §2.3; numbered list / friendly miss), 49 (port retry 5x then "could not bind"), 50 (browser-close detection 30s idle + at-least-one-request gate).

**Estimated effort:** ~1-2h.

**Acceptance gate:** `tests/integration/dual-entry.test.ts` boots via CLI + via plugin invocation; asserts both serve identical routes; `tests/integration/cli-flags.test.ts` each flag behaves (--no-browser skips openBrowser; --port overrides; --check exits 0 on no-drift); `tests/integration/cwd-scan.test.ts` zero/one/multiple `document_name` JSON files in CWD; `tests/integration/port-retry.test.ts` occupy port + assert retry up to 5; `tests/integration/browser-close.test.ts` no requests for 30s after first request + at-least-one-request → server exits 0; `tests/integration/plugin-launch.test.ts` slash command launch flow.

<info added on 2026-05-22T13:51:22.000Z>
**Shipped:** Sub-slices A/B/C/D/E across 5 task-view commits implementing TECH §6.1-§6.7. CLI binary + plugin manifest + browser-close detection + port-retry policy + S64 carryforwards landed; all PLAN §20.11 acceptance tests + 2 S64 close-out items in scope.

**Sub-slice sequencing (5 commits in external task-view repo):**
- **C+D combined (commit 8e81961, +703/-2, 6 files):** `packages/server/ledger.ts` (new factory `startTaskViewServer`) + `packages/server/ledger.test.ts` (10 unit tests) + `packages/server/patch-server.ts` (additive `onRequest` callback in PatchServerOptions) + `packages/server/package.json` (subpath exports for `detect-schema` / `mirror-generator` / `path-resolution` / `ledger` / `patch-server` / `loopback-bind`) + `tests/integration/browser-close.test.ts` (4 tests) + `tests/integration/port-retry.test.ts` (3 tests). C and D share file ownership in `ledger.ts`, landed as one commit per task-executor brief file-ownership rule. `BROWSER_CLOSE_IDLE_MS = 30_000` + `MAX_PORT_RETRIES = 5` module-level constants per inv 49+50 (non-configurable). `onRequest` hook is fail-silent (errors swallowed so a buggy tracker cannot break HTTP layer).
- **A (commit 5dd8f59, +639/-22, 3 files):** `apps/server/index.ts` (full CLI flow: `parseArgs` from `node:util`, positional path, `--no-browser` / `--port` / `--check` / `--help` / `--version` flags, `inferPathFromCwd` via `scanForLedgers`, `runRegenCheck` for `--check` one-shot, `startTaskViewServer` boot + `openBrowser` unless `--no-browser`, signal handlers route to `handle.stop`, `await handle.waitForExit()` block) + `tests/integration/cli-flags.test.ts` (6 tests) + `tests/integration/cwd-scan.test.ts` (3 tests). Existing `bin/task-view.js` Node-shim retained unchanged — it spawns Bun against `apps/server/index.ts` (the CLI logic).
- **B (commit 1a14a71, +373, 3 files):** `.claude-plugin/plugin.json` at repo root (manifest with `/task-view` slash-command `script: "node_modules/.bin/task-view"`) + `tests/integration/plugin-launch.test.ts` (10 tests, manifest shape + bin reachability) + `tests/integration/dual-entry.test.ts` (3 tests, CLI + plugin invocation both serve identical `/api/ledger` routes per §6.3 shared-server). Falls back to `bin/task-view.js` when `node_modules/.bin/task-view` symlink hasn't been created by `bun install` — the binary is the same either way.
- **E1 (commit b5026e7, +329/-33, 3 files):** `packages/ui/record-view/markdown-renderer.tsx` rewires `MarkdownBody` + `DetailsBodyWithJournal` prose segments through `parseMarkdownToBlocks` → `groupBlocks` → `BlockRenderer` (removing the `<pre>` placeholder). `packages/ui/components/InlineMarkdown.tsx` gains a real React component (inherited `trimUrlTail` helper preserved; tokenizer handles bold/italic/code/link/anchor-link) — load-bearing prerequisite the brief didn't anticipate (see Out-of-scope below). `markdown-renderer.test.tsx` grows 7 → 13 tests covering heading rendering, inline emphasis, pipe tables, fenced code, lists, inline links.
- **E2 (commit e996d7f, -3, 1 file):** `packages/ui/record-view/roadmap-section-view.tsx` drops the unused `columnsForColumnSet` sibling re-export at line 267. Grep-confirmed zero external consumers (`columnsForColumnSet` used only at line 139 within the same file). Internal function definition retained.

**PRODUCT inv coverage:** 41 (plugin slash command launches + opens browser + prints readiness + waits for close — viewer flow now end-to-end), 42 (CLI binary + `--no-browser` / `--port` / `--check` flags — each verified by `cli-flags.test.ts`), 43 (no-path invocation scans CWD per §2.3 — zero/one/multiple branches verified by `cwd-scan.test.ts`), 49 (port retry 5x then "could not bind" — `MAX_PORT_RETRIES` constant + retry-and-bind-different-port verified by `port-retry.test.ts`), 50 (browser-close detection 30s idle + at-least-one-request gate — `BROWSER_CLOSE_IDLE_MS = 30_000` constant + behavioural assertions via `_testIdleMs` overrides in `browser-close.test.ts`).

**Acceptance test results — all 6 PLAN §20.11 integration tests PASS:**
- `tests/integration/dual-entry.test.ts` (3) — CLI direct + plugin-script-target both serve `/api/ledger` with structurally identical bodies.
- `tests/integration/cli-flags.test.ts` (6) — each flag verified.
- `tests/integration/cwd-scan.test.ts` (3) — zero/one/multiple `document_name` JSON files.
- `tests/integration/port-retry.test.ts` (3) — retry-and-bind verified; `MAX_PORT_RETRIES = 5` constant asserted.
- `tests/integration/browser-close.test.ts` (4) — at-least-one-request gate + idle exit + continuous-activity refresh.
- `tests/integration/plugin-launch.test.ts` (10) — manifest structure + Claude plugin convention fields + `/task-view` slash-command + bin reachability + `package.json` bin field.

**task-view test baseline:** 687 → 733 (+46 new tests; 0 regressions). Verified via `bun run test` in the task-view repo on commit `e996d7f`.

**S64 carryforwards folded in:**
- **Finding-4 (MarkdownBody CommonMark):** rewired in E1 — `<pre>` placeholder replaced with full `parseMarkdownToBlocks` → `groupBlocks` → `BlockRenderer` pipeline. New tests verify CommonMark + GFM elements render correctly.
- **`columnsForColumnSet` sibling re-export (Checker S64 nit):** dropped in E2.

**Deviations from brief (with rationale):**
1. C+D commit-bundling: ledger.ts naturally co-owns the browser-close + port-retry logic. Splitting them into two commits would have meant either (a) landing a "C" that doesn't yet have retry (so the brief-specified `MAX_RETRIES = 5` constant lives orphaned) or (b) artificially churning the file twice. Combined commit `feat(20.11.cd): ...` keeps git history honest; 5 task-view commits total (C+D, A, B, E1, E2) satisfies the brief's "5+ commits in task-view" requirement.
2. `InlineMarkdown` re-introduction (E1 scope expansion): the brief said "replace `<pre>` block in `MarkdownBody` with the existing `parseMarkdownToBlocks` + `BlockRenderer` pipeline." But BlockRenderer's `InlineMarkdown` named import was unresolved — the 20.6 fork strip removed the upstream annotation-coupled InlineMarkdown, and 20.9 deferred re-implementation. I re-introduced a minimal InlineMarkdown (~120 lines, bold/italic/code/link/anchor-link tokens) as a load-bearing prerequisite. Considered escalating but the brief explicitly said "make sure pipe-formatted tables, code blocks, inline emphasis all render correctly" — that wording presumed the inline path worked. Flagging here for transparency.
3. `apps/server/index.ts` (not `bin/task-view.js`) carries the real CLI logic. The bin script remains a thin Node-shim spawning Bun against `apps/server/index.ts` — this honours the existing repo convention (the placeholder already used this pattern) and avoids requiring `#!/usr/bin/env bun` on systems where `bun` isn't in PATH directly. The brief's TECH §6.1 example used `#!/usr/bin/env bun` directly in `bin/task-view.js` — I deviated to keep the shim pattern that the 20.6 fork-prep landed.

**Out-of-scope observations (for Curator triage):**
1. **InlineMarkdown is a minimal stub.** The component handles inline emphasis tokens (bold / italic / code / link / anchor-link) but does NOT yet wire the upstream annotation surface (image rendering, code-file navigation, GitHub repo URL formatting). The component accepts these props (`imageBaseDir` / `onImageClick` / `onOpenLinkedDoc` / `onOpenCodeFile` / `githubRepo`) so BlockRenderer's call sites compile unchanged. A follow-up Task should re-author the full annotation surface, or the doc comment in InlineMarkdown.tsx should be updated to reflect the deliberate minimalism. Curator should consider backlog item for "InlineMarkdown rich annotation surface re-author".
2. **`apps/server/index.ts` has no direct unit test** — only the spawned-CLI integration tests cover `parseCliArgs` + `inferPathFromCwd` + `runRegenCheck`. The pure helpers could be exported and unit-tested for faster feedback; the integration tests are sufficient for acceptance but ~150ms per spawn is slow if these helpers churn. Backlog candidate.
3. **CWD-scan numbered-list stderr text not verified end-to-end.** The `cwd-scan.test.ts` "multiple matches" test asserts the server boots successfully against `[1]` but doesn't drain stderr while the process is alive (would race with stdout). The numbered-list output path is exercised but its exact text isn't asserted. Considered low-risk — `inferPathFromCwd` is a 30-line helper with clear paths. If the Checker wants tighter coverage, a stderr-text assertion test could be added.

**KH-side commit shape:** one commit on the worktree branch for status flip + this journal block. Task-view's 5 commits are local only — Liam will push manually or fold into 20.13 release-tarball per the S65 prompt.

subtasks.details:
**Repo boundary: THIS REPO (`knowledge-hub`, `production-readiness` branch). NOT the external task-view repo.**

**Implements TECH §3.5 + §6.4 KH-side + PRODUCT inv 5 + 40 initial mirror baseline:**
- §3.5 schema-drift CI workflow `.github/workflows/task-view-vendor-drift.yml`: triggered on PRs touching `lib/validation/{task-list-schema,roadmap-schema,backlog-schema,work-status}.ts`. Fetches tagged `task-view` release vendor bundle (`packages/schemas/src/*.ts` from the GitHub release per OQ-T1) and diffs. On drift: emits warning (does NOT block PR per OQ-T2 ratified default). PR description should reference whether consuming `task-view` needs re-vendor. Schema files form an inputs list in `.github/workflows/ci.yml` if they change.
- §6.4 KH-side wiring: add `task-view --check` step or job to `.github/workflows/ci.yml`. Invocations: `task-view --check docs/reference/task-list.json`, `task-view --check docs/reference/product-roadmap.json`, `task-view --check docs/reference/product-backlog.json`. Step exits non-zero if mirrors drift from canonical → CI fails → developer regenerates mirrors locally. Install task-view in CI via `bun install -g github:liam-jons/task-view@v0.1.0` per OQ-T1.
- PRODUCT inv 5 + 40 initial mirror baseline commit: run `task-view <ledger-path>` once locally against each of the 3 ledgers to generate initial mirror sets at `docs/reference/tasks/ID-N.md` (one per Task, ~13 Tasks today), `docs/reference/roadmap/section-{id}.md` + `docs/reference/roadmap/{id}.md` (per section + per item), `docs/reference/backlog/{id}.md` (per item, ~48 items today). Commit alongside this Subtask's other work so subsequent CI runs see no drift.
- Optional CLAUDE.md `Commands` table entry: add row `task-view <ledger.json>` with description `Open a workflow ledger in the task-view editor (https://github.com/liam-jons/task-view)`. Polish, not blocker.

**PRODUCT invariants covered:** 5 (initial mirrors committed alongside canonical JSON per OQ-B ratified default), 39 (external regen via KH CI guard catches drift), 40 (viewer tolerates mirror absence — this slice provides the initial baseline so subsequent runs are no-ops).

**Estimated effort:** ~1h.

Note: this Subtask touches the same JSON files in scope for ID-15 (`product-backlog.json` etc.) for READ-ONLY mirror generation — no write to canonical JSON. If 20.12 dispatches during ID-15.8+ work, there's no merge conflict on the canonical JSON, only on the new mirror files (which only this Subtask writes).

subtasks.title: Patch-surface extension — record-level CREATE/DELETE + cross-ledger transaction (gates ID-35 4-gap)
subtasks.description:
Extend task-view patch-server surface with record-level primitives (POST record / DELETE record / cross-ledger atomic transaction) gating the 4 ID-35 subcommands (open-task / create-backlog / delete-backlog / promote) that ID-20.8 field-level FieldPatch shape did not ship.

Scope identified by S62E sub-o 2 audit §2 (docs/research/id-35-crossover-audit.md). S65 dep map §3 row 7 confirms ID-20↔ID-35 coupling axis; this Subtask completes the patch-primitive surface ID-35 consumes.

## Acceptance criteria

- Task-view server exposes POST /api/ledger/record (create) + DELETE /api/ledger/record/:recordId + POST /api/ledger/transaction (cross-ledger atomic Promote = remove-from-backlog + add-to-task-list in single transaction).

subtasks.description:

Covers JSON endpoints + on-disk mirror rendering + CLI behaviour tiers of task-view v0.1.0 against KH canonical ledgers + frozen fixtures. ## Acceptance criteria

- All 22 numbered scenarios + 4 edge-case scenarios (1-26 per research doc) executed against task-view v0.1.0 (post-S65 commit `e996d7f` baseline; KH canonical at HEAD).

subtasks.title: Task-view SPA wiring — serve hydrated viewer from GET / against JSON endpoints
subtasks.description:
Close the SPA wiring gap surfaced by S66 WP5 sub-agent during ID-20.16 brief authoring. Patch server currently exposes only JSON endpoints (/api/ledger, /api/ledger/record/:recordId, PATCH, POST /regen); GET / returns no HTML. Vite dev SPA at apps/server/web/index.tsx is still the 20.6 placeholder. SSR record-view components + edit-mode helpers + 29 record-view + 9 edit-mode integration tests exist in packages/ui/ but have no browser-clickable surface.

## Acceptance criteria

- Patch server gains a GET / route that serves the SSR-rendered record-view HTML against the active ledger context (record selected by ?record=ID-N query string per existing path-resolution layer).
- Hydration layer wires the rendered components against the JSON endpoints so edit-mode affordances (pencil click → textarea, enum dropdown, Cmd+Enter save, blocked banner, promotion-ready badge) round-trip via PATCH /api/ledger/record/:recordId.
- Filter URL state for Backlog mode persists in window.location.search per PRODUCT inv 23.
- Zero net-new test failures in task-view repo (baseline 733 pass / 0 fail at S65 close).
- Integration test added covering GET / returns 200 + HTML body containing record-view markup.
- Subtask 20.18 (post-20.17) covers the deferred Scenarios 14-17 + extended SPA-only invariant coverage from the 20.16 manual test plan.
subtasks.details: Surfaced by WP5 sub-agent S66 during ID-20.16 brief authoring. Repo boundary: external task-view repo at /Users/liamj/Documents/development/task-view (branch main). Estimated effort ~3-5h depending on Vite/React 19 SSR hydration approach. Two implementation paths: (A) wire the existing Vite SPA at apps/server/web/index.tsx to fetch /api/ledger on mount + hydrate against the record-view component library; (B) add an Express/Bun route handler in packages/server/ that renders the record-view SSR HTML inline + serves it from GET /. Path B is likely simpler for the loopback-only single-user use case + matches existing patch-server architecture; Path A requires a separate dev-server build step that complicates the bundled CLI distribution. Executor decides based on the post-S65 codebase shape. Liam ratified S66 dispatch shape: open as Subtask 20.17 with deps=[11] alongside 20.16 (both run-able independently against current shipped state; 20.16 covers what exists today, 20.17 adds the missing surface). After 20.17 ships, Subtask 20.18 covers the SPA-only invariant coverage gap.
subtasks.testStrategy: Integration test verifies GET / returns 200 + HTML body with record-view markup; manual smoke verifies pencil click → textarea hydration + Cmd+Enter PATCH round-trip + Backlog filter URL persistence; full task-view repo test suite passes (baseline 733 pass / 0 fail maintained); no net-new lint warnings.

tasks.title: Relocate workflow ledgers from docs/reference/ to .planning/task-management/
tasks.description:
Move the three canonical workflow ledgers — `task-list.json`, `product-roadmap.json`, `product-backlog.json` — out of `docs/reference/` and into `.planning/task-management/`. Rationale (per S57 session-start ratification): workflow ledgers are session/SDLC state, not product reference documentation. The current colocation conflates two concerns. Period-prefix on `.planning/` has zero indexing impact (git tracks normally, GitNexus + cocoindex-code index the directory) — the benefit is purely mental separation, mirroring Taskmaster's `.taskmaster/` convention.

## Acceptance criteria

- Three ledger JSON files moved to `.planning/task-management/{task-list,product-roadmap,product-backlog}.json` (path rewrite + git history preserved via `git mv`).
- ~33 cross-references across `.claude/skills/`, `.claude/agents/`, `docs/`, `lib/docs/tracked-reference-docs.ts`, `__tests__/`, scripts/, etc. updated to point at new paths.
- `parseTaskListWithWarnings`, the canonical ingress in `lib/validation/task-list-schema.ts`, reads from the new location.
- CLAUDE.md `Key Reference Documents` + `Architecture` tables updated.
- ID-20 ledger-mirror tool (from ID-20.3 PRODUCT spec) reads ledgers from the new location — verified by re-running viewer post-relocation.
- Roundtrip tests (`backlog-roundtrip.test.ts`, etc.) pass against new paths.

## Dependencies

Depends on ID-15 (cross-surface schema alignment migration — Phase A id-rewrite + Phases B/C/D). Relocation should land AFTER ID-15 Phase A apply (15.4) to avoid compounding two migrations on the same data file (45 ID rewrites + directory move in one atomic step = atomicity risk + harder rollback per S57 forensic principle). Ideally lands AFTER ID-15 fully closes.
tasks.effort_estimate: ~2-3h (RESEARCH likely skipped; PRODUCT/TECH if any complexity surfaces; implementation = 1 git mv commit + 1 cross-doc sweep commit; ~33 refs to rewrite with word-boundary regex per migrate-roadmap-section-3 precedent).
tasks.priority_note: Mental separation of workflow ledgers from product reference docs; mirrors Taskmaster's `.taskmaster/` convention. Placeholder added at S57 close per Liam ratification at session start. Subtasks (15.1 RESEARCH, 15.2+ spec chain, implementation) populated at S58+ when actually picked up.

tasks.title: Code-intelligence integration into SDLC workflow (gitnexus + ast-dataflow + ccc)
tasks.description:
Integrate the three code-intelligence tools (GitNexus, ast-dataflow, ccc / cocoindex-code) into the Planner / Executor / Checker / Curator dispatch surfaces of the workflow-orchestration skill so sub-agents actually invoke them. Today the 36,955-symbol GitNexus index + 12-query ast-dataflow CLI + ccc semantic search are effectively dead infrastructure for sub-agents because they only read Orchestrator-extracted gotchas, never `.gitnexus/CLAUDE.md` or `.ast-dataflow/CLAUDE.md`. 63/63 tool-integration cells across 9 tool patterns × 7 dispatch surfaces measured MISSING during S61 WP5 audit.

## Acceptance criteria

- Planner brief template updated to invoke `gitnexus_query` + `gitnexus_context` for orientation pre-spec-authoring.
- Executor `implement-subtask` skill Step 6.5 added: pre-commit `gitnexus_detect_changes()` + per-symbol `gitnexus_impact` checks.
- Checker `task-checker.md` gains `scope-containment` audit axis using `gitnexus_detect_changes()` + `gitnexus_context` + `gitnexus_route_map`/`gitnexus_api_impact` for new routes.
- `skill-routing-map.md` adds Refactor/Rename/Type-evolution tilt row naming gitnexus-refactoring + gitnexus-impact-analysis + ast-dataflow + ast-dataflow-rename-sweep + ast-dataflow-call-chain-pin. Skill-routing-map location ratified (current docs/reference/ vs proposed .claude/skills/workflow-orchestration/references/).
- workflow-orchestration SKILL.md adds Code-intelligence baseline section codifying Orchestrator's responsibility to propagate gitnexus/ast-dataflow/ccc mandates into every code-touching dispatch brief.
- write-tech-spec skill gains orientation step prescribing gitnexus_query + gitnexus_context + ccc search + ast-dataflow CLI as the first-line research pattern.
- workflow-orchestration/references/lifecycle-detail.md adds 1-paragraph cite-the-impact-verdict requirement to PRODUCT + TECH Planner sections.
- `.gitnexus/CLAUDE.md` Always Do gains propagation-discipline bullet mirroring ID-19.4 hook propagation rule.
- triage-finding/SKILL.md Step 1 adds gitnexus_context + ast-dataflow callers pre-grep step (caller count = deterministic roadmap-vs-backlog signal).
- commit-commands integration note in implement-subtask Step 4 (codifies current manual gate until tooling automates it).

## Dependencies

None blocking. Could fold into S61 if scoped tightly.

## Constraints

- ALL skill file edits via update-skill / agent-development skills (per Liam directive S60). NO manual Edit on `.claude/skills/*/SKILL.md` or `.claude/agents/*.md`.
- Sub-agents struggle with multi-skill invocations in one brief (3 stalls observed S59 + S60). Each Executor Subtask should be ONE skill invocation. Multi-file changes split across multiple Executors, sequential not parallel where update-skill / agent-development are involved.

tasks.title: session-driver-cmux maintenance — close S61 carryover script gaps
tasks.description:
Close the 4 cmux-script gaps surfaced in S61 parallel-work (worktree-baseline-investigation, workspace:4) carryover #2 so cmux can fully replace Agent-tool isolation:worktree as the canonical parallel-dispatch primitive while two top-level worktrees persist.

Motivating finding: S61 confirmed Anthropic's Agent-tool isolation:worktree places sub-agents under the PRIMARY top-level worktree regardless of orchestrator CWD; S62 W1 empirical re-test (post user-global `baseRef:"head"` flip) re-confirmed STRUCTURAL not session-cache. cmux explicit `git worktree add` from PROJECT_ROOT bypasses this. With these 4 script gaps closed, cmux becomes the production-ready substitute and the parallel-session pattern can eventually collapse to single top-level worktree (Path A, ID-24) without losing parallelism.

Not blocking ID-9 / ID-20 work. Independent maintenance Task. ID-24 (Worktree collapse) remains blocked on ID-9 + ID-20 per Liam ratification (script fixes alone insufficient — substantive carry-forward work still needs to land on main first).

## What shipped (all 4 in one commit)

Commit `25a5fa1b` feat(session-driver-cmux): close S61 carryover script gaps

- launch-worker.sh `--brief <file>` flag — copies brief into worker as `.cmux-brief.md`, auto-sends "Read .cmux-brief.md before any work." after session_start; mirrors OQ-escalation channel shape.
- launch-worker.sh `.worktreeinclude` honour — literal-path copy on `git worktree add`; canonical case `.env.local`.
- stop-worker.sh `--delete-branch` opt-in flag — deletes worker branch post worktree removal; default OFF preserves cherry-pick window.
- wait-for-fleet.sh bash 3.2 portability — flat-string + awk lookup replaces `declare -A` associative arrays; macOS default /bin/bash 3.2.57 verified.

SKILL.md table updated; 2 Known-limitations rows dropped (now closed).

## OQs ratified inline (no spec chain)

- sdc-update OQ #3 (branch-cleanup canonical): opt-in flag on stop-worker (default off, parent passes after cherry-pick confirmed).
- launch-worker brief-delivery: auto-send pointer prompt on session_start (vs orchestrator-composes).
- ID-24 timing: keep blocked on ID-9 + ID-20.

Deferred (out of scope, not blocking): 3 oq-spec PRODUCT-invariant OQs (Q1/Q2/Q3) — ratify when authoring `docs/specs/oq-escalation/TECH.md`.

## Acceptance criteria — all met

- All 4 scripts syntax-PASS on /bin/bash 3.2.57.
- End-to-end live cmux dispatch from production-readiness orchestrator: worker landed in P-R `.claude/worktrees/` (NOT primary), `.cmux-brief.md` copied, `.env.local` copied via `.worktreeinclude`, auto-prompt emitted user_prompt_submit event, `wait-for-fleet.sh --timeout 3` cleanly produced Completed/Pending output + exit 1, `stop-worker.sh --force --delete-branch` removed worktree + branch + events dir.
- SKILL.md reflects new flags + drops closed gaps.

## Dependencies

None (ratification absorbed inline; no spec chain).

subtasks.title: wait-for-fleet.sh — bash 3.2 portability
subtasks.description: Replace `declare -A` associative arrays (AFTER_LINE, COMPLETED) with bash-3.2-portable flat strings (newline-delimited `sid=n` records + awk lookup; space-bordered `" sid1 sid2 "` membership-check string + explicit counter).
subtasks.details: <info added on 2026-05-21 by Orchestrator-direct>Implemented inline (no Executor dispatch — single-file bounded edit). Replaced `declare -A AFTER_LINE` with `AFTER_LINE_DATA` newline-delimited string + `after_line_for()` helper using awk lookup. Replaced `declare -A COMPLETED` with `COMPLETED_LIST` space-bordered string (case-pattern membership) + `COMPLETED_COUNT` integer counter. Trailing timeout-error message uses awk `{$1=$1;print}` whitespace trim for clean display. Verified `/bin/bash --version` shows 3.2.57(1) on macOS Darwin 25.2.0; `/bin/bash -n` passes; live `--timeout 3` test against an active session emitted `Error: timeout after 3s (mode=any)` + `Completed: <none>` + `Pending: <SID>` + exit 1. Shipped in commit 25a5fa1b.</info added on 2026-05-21>
subtasks.testStrategy: /bin/bash -n syntax check + live --timeout 3 against active SID produces Completed/Pending output with exit 1.

subtasks.title: stop-worker.sh — `--delete-branch` opt-in flag
subtasks.description: Add `--delete-branch` flag (default OFF). After worktree removal, deletes the worker branch recorded in meta. Default-off preserves cherry-pick window for the parent orchestrator. Branch name sourced from meta's `branch` field (populated by launch-worker.sh).
subtasks.details: <info added on 2026-05-21 by Orchestrator-direct>Implemented inline. Flag parser refactored from positional `${3:-}` check to a while-loop accepting `--force` and `--delete-branch` in any order. Meta-file load gained `BRANCH_NAME` field. New section after worktree removal: skips if branch name empty (with warning), skips if branch already gone (note), otherwise `git branch -D` with warning on failure. sdc-update OQ #3 ratified: opt-in flag (vs always parent-handled) — centralised one-call cleanup, default-off preserves cherry-pick option. Live test: `stop-worker.sh sdc-fix-verify <SID> --force --delete-branch` removed worktree + branch + events dir; verified `git branch | grep cmux-worker-sdc-fix-verify` returned nothing post-call. Shipped in commit 25a5fa1b.</info added on 2026-05-21>
subtasks.testStrategy: Launch worker, immediately stop with --force --delete-branch; verify worktree gone + branch deleted + events dir gone.

subtasks.title: launch-worker.sh — `--brief <file>` flag with auto-prompt
subtasks.description: Add `--brief <file>` flag that copies a brief file into the worker worktree as `.cmux-brief.md` and auto-sends 'Read .cmux-brief.md before any work.' after session_start. Mirrors OQ-escalation channel shape (file-on-disk + structured pointer prompt). brief_path exposed in result JSON + meta file.
subtasks.details: <info added on 2026-05-21 by Orchestrator-direct>Implemented inline. Flag added to parser between `--branch` and EXTRA_ARGS. Brief existence validated pre-side-effects (early exit if file not found). Copy happens post `git worktree add` with rollback on cp failure. Auto-prompt sent after session_start detection via cmux send + send-key enter — orchestrator's downstream send-prompt.sh calls can assume brief was read. Meta file + result JSON both gain `brief_path` field. Live test: launched with `--brief /tmp/claude-501/sdc-fix-verify/brief.md` (617 bytes); worker worktree contained `.cmux-brief.md` (617 bytes preserved), events.jsonl showed `user_prompt_submit` event consistent with the auto-prompt firing, followed by 6 `pre_tool_use` events indicating the worker actively tooling on the brief steps. Ratified design: auto-send on boot (vs orchestrator-composes). Shipped in commit 25a5fa1b.</info added on 2026-05-21>
subtasks.testStrategy: Launch with --brief, verify .cmux-brief.md exists in worker worktree + brief_path in result JSON + user_prompt_submit event in events.jsonl post session_start.

subtasks.title: launch-worker.sh — `.worktreeinclude` honour
subtasks.description: After `git worktree add`, read `<project-root>/.worktreeinclude` (if exists) as a list of literal file paths (one per line, `#` comments skipped, blanks skipped). Copy each existing source from PROJECT_ROOT into the new worktree, preserving nested-path parent directories. Plain paths only — no glob expansion (kept minimal; extend if patterns needed). Canonical case: `.env.local`.
subtasks.details: <info added on 2026-05-21 by Orchestrator-direct>Implemented inline alongside the --brief flag. Read loop strips CR (for CRLF files), awk-trims whitespace, skips blank + `#`-comment lines. Each path tested with `[ -e ]`; if present, `mkdir -p` the destination parent and `cp -R` source to destination with a warning on failure (non-fatal). Anthropic's `.worktreeinclude` mechanism is internal to `claude --worktree` / Agent-tool isolation / EnterWorktree — cmux's raw `git worktree add` bypasses it, so this is a deliberate mirror of the canonical case (`.env.local`) without claiming full pattern parity. Live test: `.worktreeinclude` at project root contained `.env.local` (1 line); worker worktree post-launch contained `.env.local` (1806 bytes preserved). Shipped in commit 25a5fa1b.</info added on 2026-05-21>
subtasks.testStrategy: Launch worker, verify `.env.local` (or whichever paths .worktreeinclude lists) present in worker worktree.

tasks.title: Orchestrator-of-orchestrators polish — session-driver-cmux + Tier 2.2 hook (S62B fix-pack)
tasks.description:
Address the three polish fix-Tasks surfaced by S62B orchestrator-of-orchestrators e2e verification — see `docs/research/orchestrator-of-orchestrators-e2e-findings.md` §3 for evidence and full per-fix change scope.

All three fixes are quality-of-life polish. None are correctness blockers; none gate Path A migration (per S62B Phase D §4: workflow-primitive verification PASS). They DO reduce friction when the o-of-o pattern is exercised at scale — recommended before next o-of-o dogfooding run (S62C planned).

## What's in scope

FX-1 — `stop-worker.sh` resilience polish (3 sub-issues bundled into one Subtask).
FX-2 — Tier 2.2 `git -C` hook subcommand whitelist (read-only ops should not be blocked).
FX-3 — `launch-worker.sh` collision diagnostic specificity (distinguish branch-collision vs worktree-path-collision).

## Acceptance criteria

- FX-1 (stop-worker.sh): three sub-issues fixed — untracked `.cmux-brief.md` no longer trips dirty-tree exit; cmux workspace close reordered to AFTER dirty-tree check (or partial-failure path explicitly documented); `--delete-branch` falls back to `git worktree list` lookup when meta missing.
- FX-2 (Tier 2.2 git-C hook): subcommand whitelist applied — read-only forms (status, log, show, diff, branch --list, worktree list, rev-parse, ls-files, ls-tree, remote -v, config --get, fetch) permitted from sub-agent context; mutating forms remain blocked.
- FX-3 (launch-worker.sh): pre-`git worktree add` checks emit precise diagnostic per collision shape — "branch already exists" vs "worktree path already exists".

Re-run of S62B Phase B Test #4 + #5 + #8 probes against the fixed scripts must PASS with no NEEDS-WORK on the three covered axes.

## Dependencies

None. Independent of all other Tasks. Safe to dispatch any time.

## Provenance

S62B Phase D finding pack — see `docs/research/orchestrator-of-orchestrators-e2e-findings.md` §3 (FX-1/FX-2/FX-3 detail) and the commit landing this Task.

<info added on 2026-05-21T20:35:00.000Z>
