subtasks.title: FX-1 — stop-worker.sh resilience polish (3 sub-issues)
subtasks.description: Three resilience improvements to `.claude/skills/session-driver-cmux/scripts/stop-worker.sh`. Source of truth: findings doc §3 FX-1.
subtasks.details:
TO BE POPULATED via Executor at {33.1} dispatch. Source of truth: `docs/research/orchestrator-of-orchestrators-e2e-findings.md` §3 FX-1 + §2 #4 side-findings 1/2/3.

Sub-issue 1 — Untracked `.cmux-brief.md` triggers dirty-tree exit-2 even though the brief is a script-managed artefact. Fix: exclude `.cmux-brief.md` from the dirty-tree check OR auto-delete the brief file as part of clean-shutdown.

Sub-issue 2 — cmux workspace is closed BEFORE the dirty-tree check runs. If the check fails (exit 2), the workspace is already gone but the worktree + branch are not removed. Partial-failure state with no recovery path other than manual intervention. Fix: reorder so dirty-tree check runs BEFORE workspace close — OR document that exit 2 is irreversible.

Sub-issue 3 — `--delete-branch` silently no-ops when the meta file is missing (post-failure re-run scenario). Fix: fall back to deriving the branch name from `git worktree list` when meta is absent.

<info added on 2026-05-21T20:35:00.000Z>
**S62C Wave 1 WP1 — FX-1 landed in two commits.**

Commit `cd9c9e9d` (initial WP1) — covers all 3 sub-issues at the script level:
  * Sub-issue 1: `.cmux-brief.md` carved out of dirty-tree check via `grep -Fvx` in the untracked-files component.
  * Sub-issue 2: reordered so dirty-tree check runs BEFORE `cmux close-workspace` — exit 2 now leaves workspace alive (operator can re-attach).
  * Sub-issue 3: WORKTREE_PATH + BRANCH_NAME fallback derivation when meta file absent (path convention + `git worktree list --porcelain` awk lookup).

Commit `7833411a` (follow-up) — WP4 verification surfaced a gap: the script-side carve-out alone wasn't enough because `git worktree remove` itself ALSO refuses dirty trees. Without the brief auto-delete, stop-worker exited 0 cleanly but worktree path persisted post-stop. Fix adds `rm -f ${WORKTREE_PATH}/.cmux-brief.md` immediately before `git worktree remove`.

Verification (per findings doc §6.2):
  * Sub-issue 1 PASS: probe s62c-fx-verify-2 (SID 3bbb244b) clean stop exit 0, worktree path removed, cmux workspace removed, branch retained.
  * Sub-issue 2 PASS: probe s62c-fx-verify-3 (SID 8adc23ad) injected `dirty-test-marker.txt` → exit 2 + workspace:24 STILL ALIVE post-exit (verbatim confirmed via `cmux list-workspaces`).
  * Sub-issue 3 PASS: --force --delete-branch cleanup of s62c-fx-verify-3 worked via meta-derived branch (meta-present canonical path); fallback path edge-noted in §6.2 (both meta + path gone = unresolvable; FX-3 collision tells operator branch name).

Files touched: `.claude/skills/session-driver-cmux/scripts/stop-worker.sh` (rewrite ~80 net lines), `.claude/skills/session-driver-cmux/SKILL.md` (extend `--delete-branch` doc + new Order-of-operations note).
</info added on 2026-05-21T20:35:00.000Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: Three unit-test scenarios: (1) launch + stop with untracked `.cmux-brief.md` only → clean exit 0 (no dirty-tree block); (2) launch + worker writes a real change + stop → block workspace close + exit 2 with NO partial cleanup (worktree + branch + events dir all still present); (3) launch + force-fail mid-stop + re-run with `--force --delete-branch` → branch correctly removed via `git worktree list` fallback. Verify each via direct invocation in a probe-worker dispatch (e.g. S62B-style `s62b-probe-fx1-*`).

subtasks.title: FX-2 — Tier 2.2 git-C hook subcommand whitelist
subtasks.description: Refine `.claude/settings.json` PreToolUse hook (`git -C` absolute-path block) to differentiate read-only vs write subcommands. Source of truth: findings doc §3 FX-2.
subtasks.details:
TO BE POPULATED via Executor at {33.2} dispatch. Source of truth: `docs/research/orchestrator-of-orchestrators-e2e-findings.md` §3 FX-2 + §2 #5.

Current regex: `git[[:space:]]+-C[[:space:]]+/Users/liamj/Documents/development/knowledge-hub` — op-agnostic, blocks all forms (read-only + write equally).

Design: two-phase check inside the hook command — (a) match absolute-path `git -C` call; (b) inspect the subcommand following the path. Whitelist (ALLOW) for read-only: `status`, `log`, `show`, `diff`, `branch --list`, `branch -v`, `worktree list`, `rev-parse`, `ls-files`, `ls-tree`, `remote -v`, `config --get`, `fetch`. Continue to BLOCK all mutating forms: `branch -d/-D`, `worktree add/remove/move`, `push`, `commit`, `merge`, `rebase`, `reset`, `checkout`, `switch`, `stash drop`, `clean`, `gc`, `prune`, `config --set`, etc.

<info added on 2026-05-21T20:35:00.000Z>
**S62C Wave 1 WP2 — FX-2 landed in commit `b6269317`.**

New external hook script `.claude/hooks/git-c-abs-path-subcommand-guard.sh` (~85 L). `.claude/settings.json` PreToolUse Bash[1] command now invokes `bash "$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/.claude/hooks/git-c-abs-path-subcommand-guard.sh"` — resolves via per-worktree toplevel so sub-agent worktrees inheriting branch state can locate it.

Two-phase design per FX-2 spec:
  * Phase 1: detect `git -C <abs-knowledge-hub-path>` shape; no-op if not matched.
  * Phase 2: extract subcommand; route via:
    * Unconditional ALLOW (16 verbs): status, log, show, diff, rev-parse, ls-files, ls-tree, fetch, tag, describe, reflog, blame, grep, shortlog, cat-file, symbolic-ref.
    * Conditional ALLOW for verbs with both read-only + mutating subforms: branch (ALLOW unless -d/-D/-m/-M/-c/-C/--delete/--move/--copy/--set-upstream-to/--unset-upstream); worktree (ALLOW only `list`); remote (BLOCK add/remove/rename/set-url/prune/update/set-head/set-branches); config (ALLOW only --get/--list/--get-all/--get-regexp/--get-urlmatch/-l); stash (ALLOW only list/show).
    * Default BLOCK for everything else.

Verification (per findings doc §6.1):
  * 30 isolated fixtures at authoring time: 13 read-only ALLOW + 14 mutating BLOCK + 3 non-matching ALLOW. All PASS.
  * Probe s62c-fx-verify-1 (SID c0c9fdab) end-to-end from sub-agent context: VERDICT PASS across 6 commands matching expected outcomes.

Files touched: `.claude/hooks/git-c-abs-path-subcommand-guard.sh` (NEW, +85 L), `.claude/settings.json` (replace inline op-agnostic regex with script invocation).
</info added on 2026-05-21T20:35:00.000Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: Re-run S62B Test #5 probe brief (`/tmp/claude-501/s62b-probe-b5.md` style) with the new hook — all 4 read-only abs-path commands (`status --short`, `worktree list`, `log --oneline -3`, `branch --list`) PASS; relative-path command continues to PASS; mutating abs-path command (`branch -D nonexistent`) BLOCKED. Parameterise into a fixture covering 10+ commands across read-only / write splits.

subtasks.title: FX-3 — launch-worker.sh collision diagnostic specificity
subtasks.description: Pre-`git worktree add` checks on `.claude/skills/session-driver-cmux/scripts/launch-worker.sh` to emit a precise collision diagnostic. Source of truth: findings doc §3 FX-3.
subtasks.details:
TO BE POPULATED via Executor at {33.3} dispatch. Source of truth: `docs/research/orchestrator-of-orchestrators-e2e-findings.md` §3 FX-3 + §2 #8 LITE NEEDS-WORK.

Current failure mode: `Error: failed to create worktree at <path>` with exit 1 — operator must inspect to determine whether collision is on branch or worktree path.

Design: pre-`git worktree add` checks — (a) `git rev-parse --verify refs/heads/<branch>` → if exists, error `branch '<name>' already exists; previous worker stopped without --delete-branch? Recover with: git branch -D <name>`; (b) `[ -e <worktree-path> ]` → if exists, error `worktree path '<path>' already exists; recover with: git worktree remove --force <path>`; (c) otherwise, proceed with the original `git worktree add` flow.

<info added on 2026-05-21T20:35:00.000Z>
**S62C Wave 1 WP3 — FX-3 landed in commit `76d9eaeb`.**

Pre-`git worktree add` collision checks distinguish branch-collision vs worktree-path-collision before the opaque `failed to create worktree` error. Each shape emits a precise recovery hint. When both collide, emit BOTH diagnostics.

Design:
  * Reordered WORKTREE_PATH + BASE_SHA + BRANCH_NAME derivation above the collision check block (previously only WORKTREE_PATH derived early).
  * Branch existence: `git rev-parse --verify --quiet refs/heads/branch-name` → if exists, error: branch already exists + recover hint `git branch -D branch-name`.
  * Worktree path existence: `[ -e path ]` → if exists, error: worktree path already exists + recover hint `git worktree remove --force path`.
  * Exit 1 preserved (matches pre-FX-3 behaviour).

Verification (per findings doc §6.3):
  * Isolated logic test: shape 0 (clean) ALLOW; shape 1 (branch only) BRANCH error; shape 2 (path only) PATH error; shape 3 (both) BOTH errors. All PASS, cleanup verified.
  * End-to-end BRANCH-only collision via s62c-fx-verify-2 re-launch after non-delete stop: exit 1 with verbatim BRANCH diagnostic.
  * Order-of-checks note: pre-existing cmux-workspace-name collision (launch-worker.sh:140-145) fires first when cmux is alive, so FX-3 owns the post-cleanup recovery path (intended).

Files touched: `.claude/skills/session-driver-cmux/scripts/launch-worker.sh` (extend pre-flight section, +31 net lines).
</info added on 2026-05-21T20:35:00.000Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: Re-run S62B Test #8 collision probe — verify each collision shape (branch-only / worktree-only / both) emits a precise diagnostic BEFORE `git worktree add` is called. Exit code 1 preserved. Verify via three direct invocations covering the three collision shapes.

tasks.title: Roadmap-backlog consolidation + roadmap rethink (Linear-style)
tasks.description:
Consolidate the current product-roadmap.json content into product-backlog.json (single canonical work list), then rethink the roadmap as a thin capability-themes surface per Linear / Aha! best practice.

Motivation: Per s48-feedback.md line 201 (verbatim: "items currently on the KH Roadmap (which is actually our Backlog - concepts reversed)") the current roadmap is misused as a parking lot for prioritised work, while the backlog is treated as deferred-only. This is the inverted semantic vs standard product management convention. FU-1 (label-reversal content migration) and FU-5 (file-rename completing reversal) were DEFERRED at S52 per surface-migration TECH §Follow-ups; the S60 ID-15.9 label-reversal-residue-audit returned CLEAN but applied triage-finding Branch B target-semantic (strategic/tactical) — not the user-intent Linear semantic.

Approach (per S62 W2 Liam ratification): (1) audit roadmap vs backlog for duplication; (2) remove items rendered redundant by canonical-pipeline implementation (collapse list); (3) move remaining roadmap items → backlog (post-collapse); (4) rethink roadmap to industry best practice (Shape A: ~10-12 capability themes with now/next/later horizons); (5) audit consolidated backlog for further dedup against task-list + propose prioritisation model with optional rank field; (6) extend task-view (ID-20) backlog CRUD model to support priority/rank editing.

## Acceptance criteria

- product-backlog.json grows by ~54 items per RESEARCH §3 migration proposal (post-Liam OQ ratification).
- product-roadmap.json reshaped to Shape A (or alternative per OQ-1) with ~10-12 capability themes.
- triage-finding + update-roadmap-backlog skills rewritten under new semantic.
- Optional rank field added to BacklogSchema; backlog test guards updated.
- task-view (ID-20) ID-20.14 extension surfaces backlog edit + drag-reorder per ID-20.14 PLAN authored in TECH phase.
- Migration sequencing executes in 3 PRs (schema/skill update → bulk content migration → roadmap reshape) per OQ-7.

## Dependencies

ID-15 done (schema baseline). Independent of ID-9 (Astro docs) + ID-20 (task-view) at RESEARCH level; PRODUCT/TECH execution may interlock with ID-20.14 backlog-edit surface.

DONE-S68-W2 — All 13 Subtasks done (PR-A + PR-B + PR-C shipped). 11 capability themes authored; sections[] dropped; schema reshape -144 LOC; 54 net-new backlog entries IDs 87-140; 8 REMOVE_REDUNDANT roadmap items removed; renderer rewritten for theme-traversal; capability_theme back-filled on 21 Tasks. End-of-task simplification + quality-review SKIPPED per S67 W1c precedent (per-Subtask Checker dispatches × 9 already verified; code surface = schema + scripts + tests + data + ledger writes, no behavior-critical logic warranting separate axes).

subtasks.title: Schema additions — rank, capability_theme, RoadmapThemeSchema + union root
subtasks.description: Add three Zod schema fields per TECH §3.1 — BacklogItemSchema.rank (integer nullable optional), TaskSchema.capability_theme (string nullable optional), and a new RoadmapThemeSchema export alongside the existing RoadmapSectionSchema with RoadmapSchema root reshaped to union root via .superRefine() (exactly one of sections[] OR themes[]). All purely additive against live JSON content.
subtasks.details:
Three additive Zod schema changes per TECH §3.1:

lib/validation/backlog-schema.ts (~line 122, between notes and details):
```typescript
/**
 * Within-priority deterministic ordering. Lower integer = higher rank.
 * Default null; pre-existing items omit. Schema does NOT enforce uniqueness
 * or contiguity within tier (PRODUCT inv 3). Curator skill maintains
 * discipline (Subtask 30.5 + P-OQ-3 auto-shift default).
 */
rank: z.number().int().nullable().optional(),
```

lib/validation/task-list-schema.ts (~line 130, after array-fields block, before .strict()):
```typescript
/**
 * Optional back-link to a Roadmap theme (OQ-6 ratification). Authoritative
 * direction is theme.linked_tasks[]; capability_theme is convenience back-link
 * the curator skill maintains in sync. Absent = unaffiliated.
 */
capability_theme: z.string().nullable().optional(),
```

lib/validation/roadmap-schema.ts:
- Add RoadmapThemeSchema export per TECH §3.1 (10 Phase-B fields; .strict()):
  - id (BARE_ID_REGEX), title, description, time_horizon enum 3-value (now/next/later), status enum 3-value (pending/in_progress/done per P-OQ-1 default), linked_tasks string[], linked_backlog string[], session_refs string[], commit_refs string[], cross_doc_links DocLinkSchema[], notes string nullable.
- Reshape RoadmapSchema root: sections[] becomes OPTIONAL; add themes[] OPTIONAL; add .superRefine() enforcing exactly-one-of:
```typescript
.superRefine((doc, ctx) => {
  const hasSections = doc.sections !== undefined;
  const hasThemes = doc.themes !== undefined;
  if (hasSections === hasThemes) {
    ctx.addIssue({ code: 'custom', path: ['sections', 'themes'],
      message: 'Exactly one of sections or themes must be present.' });
  }
});
```
Per T-OQ-4 ratification: stay with superRefine (not discriminatedUnion) to avoid discriminator-field content churn.

GitNexus verification (CLAUDE.md):
- Pre-edit: gitnexus_impact({target: 'BacklogItemSchema', direction: 'upstream'}) + gitnexus_impact({target: 'RoadmapSchema', direction: 'upstream'}).
- Post-edit: gitnexus_detect_changes().

- lib/validation/roadmap-schema.ts

**T-OQ-1 PRODUCT.md amendment diff:**
```
inv 1: 'Today's 55 items grow to 109' → 'Today's 54 items grow to 108'
inv 12 PR-B acceptance gate: 'Backlog item count goes from 55 to 109' → 'Backlog item count goes from 54 to 108'
```
Both amendments carry the reconciliation parenthetical: 'T-OQ-1 reconciled at S67 W1a per Subtask 30.9 — original "55 → 109" reflected a stale count; live baseline measured 54'. Other 55/61 references on lines 16, 80 (roadmap composition factual claim) left unchanged per surgical-amendment scope.

**Design decisions worth flagging:**
1. **Idempotency vs collision boundary.** PLAN.md test (c) says 'one id pre-populated; script reports collision and does NOT write' but PRODUCT inv 15 says idempotent re-runs are no-ops. The reconciliation: classifyFixtureEntry() returns 'absent' / 'migration-skip' / 'collision' based on BOTH id-match AND presence of the canonical 'Migrated from roadmap §' marker in the existing entry's notes. An existing entry with matching id + marker → silent skip (idempotent). An existing entry with matching id + no marker → collision (independently authored entry shadows this id; abort before write). This is the only behaviour consistent with both spec lines.
2. **Synthetic pre-migration baseline in tests.** The live product-backlog.json HEAD has IDs 78-85 populated by independent triage-routed entries (not migration entries). A naive 'copy live JSON into tmpdir' baseline would trigger COLLISION on test (a) before any migration logic runs. Tests filter the live JSON to drop any IDs 78-131, producing a clean pre-migration baseline that mirrors the PR-B starting state PRODUCT inv 12 specifies. This isolates the script's migration behaviour from accidental coupling to the post-spec live data state.
3. **'partial 11.2' interpretation.** The brief / RESEARCH §2.3 line 245 cites '11.2 partially (rename-gated test partitions)'. The collapse-list summary §2.3 lines 238-245 lists 11.2 in the REMOVE_REDUNDANT 8-item set anyway; PRODUCT inv 12 PR-B clause explicitly enumerates '8 REMOVE_REDUNDANT items (3.2, 3.4, 3.6, 7.2, 7.6, 8.1, 11.17, and the rename-gated 11.2 partition)'. Operationally 11.2 is removed entirely (the surviving W-RE/W-RH scope migrates to other Backlog items, not preserved as a residual 11.2 entry). REMOVE_REDUNDANT_ROADMAP_IDS = ['3.2', '3.4', '3.6', '7.2', '7.6', '8.1', '11.17', '11.2'] — 8 ids — matches both the brief's enumeration and the 61→53 expected count delta.
4. **CLI gating via `import.meta.main`.** The script's main() runs only when invoked directly (`bun scripts/migrate-backlog-from-roadmap.ts`); tests import the module without triggering CLI side effects. Same pattern as roadmap-from-json.ts (`if (import.meta.main) { ... }`).
5. **Result-type contract instead of thrown errors.** `runMigration()` returns `MigrationResult = {ok: true, ...} | {ok: false, reason, detail}`. Only readJson() can propagate exceptions (on malformed JSON parse), which matches the project's tryQuery/Result-style convention from lib/supabase/safe — except here the resource is filesystem JSON not Supabase.

**Out-of-scope observations (Curator triage candidates):**
1. **Live backlog IDs 78-85 are independently-authored entries (NOT migration entries).** Per `jq '.items[] | select(.id == "78" or ... "85") | .notes'` against live HEAD, these were added by S64+ triage routing (S252, S64 W1, S65 Wave 2, S66 Wave 2 routes — see Backlog entry notes for provenance). Subtask 30.10 (live execution, Wave 2, gated on Liam ratification) will fail with COLLISION against these IDs unless one of: (a) the existing entries get renumbered to IDs outside 78-131; (b) the migration fixture IDs get shifted to start above the max live id (currently 85); (c) the existing entries get marked with the migration marker (semantically wrong — they're not migration entries). Option (a) seems cleanest — renumber existing 78-85 to ≥132 — but requires triage-finding routing decisions. Routing to Curator: this is exactly the kind of cross-Subtask reconciliation the Curator owns via update-roadmap-backlog Update mode + planning ahead of Subtask 30.10 execution. Surfaced as fyi; not load-bearing for 30.9 itself (the script + tests are correct; 30.10 needs a pre-execution decision).
2. **No similar discovery for IDs 86+.** A scan via `jq '.items | map(.id) | sort_by(tonumber)' docs/reference/product-backlog.json` confirms the live backlog uses IDs 17-85 only — IDs 86-131 are unused and the migration will not collide on those. Only the 78-85 range needs reconciliation.
3. **PARTIAL_OVERLAP cluster member ID-100 not present in pre-migration baseline.** Cluster 'ingest-UI' references IDs 61+62+79+100. After migration, ID-100 lives in the migrated set (it's in the RESEARCH §3.2 payload). The script handles this correctly via `indexById.get(memberId)` (skips missing members in the pre-migration state), and after the merge the lookup succeeds. Verified by test (g): all 4 cluster members carry the adjacency line post-migration. Surfacing this not as a bug but as confirmation that the across-pools cluster-walking design works as intended.
4. **Skill-tool-blocked discipline observed.** The Tier 2.2 hook correctly fired on my initial PRODUCT.md Edit when I used the absolute knowledge-hub-production-readiness sibling-tree path from CWD pointed at the agent worktree (`agent-a5c80852324ad348d` lives under `/Users/liamj/.../knowledge-hub/.claude/worktrees/`). Six-step authoring process per PRODUCT inv 6, 7, 8 + TECH §2 inv 6+7 rows:

1. Read post-PR-B docs/reference/product-roadmap.json (16 sections, 53 items).
2. Read post-PR-B docs/reference/product-backlog.json (108 items including 54 newly-migrated).
3. Read docs/reference/task-list.json (in-flight Tasks).
4. Identify ~10-12 capability themes covering the strategic surface. RESEARCH §4 named candidate themes (Sales Proposals, MCP server, ingestion-UI, taxonomy-and-vocab, intelligence-refinement, etc.) — re-evaluate against post-PR-B state.
5. For each theme: author id (bare-digit '1' onwards under Shape A), title (short UK English capability name), description (multi-paragraph Markdown — why this capability matters; outcome shape), time_horizon (now/next/later per OQ-2), status: 'pending' (P-OQ-1 default), linked_tasks[] (Task IDs contributing), linked_backlog[] (Backlog item IDs contributing), session_refs: ['kh-prod-readiness-S65'], commit_refs as relevant, cross_doc_links if relevant, notes free text.
6. Write document with new schema shape — drop sections[]; populate themes[]. RoadmapSchema union (from 30.6) accepts this in PR-C transitional state.

Theme cardinality discipline per PRODUCT inv 8: soft cap 12. If authoring surfaces 13th theme, consider merging per parseRoadmapWithWarnings warning (from 30.7). Re-evaluate quarterly.

PR-C acceptance gate per PRODUCT inv 12 (all three must hold):
- Theme count ≤ 12.
- Every theme has ≥ 1 linked_tasks[] OR linked_backlog[] entry (no orphan themes).
- Every theme has time_horizon set.

UK English discipline: every theme title + description in UK English (colour, organisation, behaviour, DD/MM/YYYY dates).

Coordination with Subtask 30.5 curator skill: update-roadmap-backlog Create-mode contract from 30.5 IS the contract used here. Author may invoke /update-roadmap-backlog Create once per theme to populate themes[] — verifying rewritten skill's contract works in practice.

tasks.title: Canonical-pipeline → task-list migration approach (Linear-style umbrella/tag model)
tasks.description:
Migrate the canonical-pipeline implementation plan (docs/specs/canonical-pipeline-implementation-plan/PLAN.md, T1-T14) into task-list.json as top-level Tasks with the PLAN doc remaining as spec substrate (Option 2 per S62 W2 Liam ratification). Includes retrospective Task population for T1-T6 + pre-phase work already shipped, and introduces an umbrella/tag model for grouping Tasks under multi-stream initiatives (Linear Initiative analogue).

Motivation: Per s48-feedback.md line 200: "Items on KH backlog and roadmap are essentially 'tasks', which when moved to the task-list, will be decomposed to subtasks." The current canonical-pipeline PLAN.md is rich substrate but lives outside the task-list, breaking traceability/observability of the single canonical work surface. S61 WP7 incorrectly scoped canonical-pipeline as out-of-scope for the production-readiness task-list — user intent is migration into one global task-list.

Approach (per Liam ratification): (1) preserve PLAN.md as spec substrate (auto-syncs to Astro docs site via ID-9); (2) open one Task per Tn with cross_doc_links + slim description; (3) retrospectively encode T1-T6 + pre-phase work as `done` Tasks with provenance journal blocks citing commits + sessions; (4) introduce umbrella/tag model (Option C: separate umbrellas.json document with curated task_ids[] per umbrella + substrate_doc field) for project-plan-as-tag concept.

## Acceptance criteria

- 14 Tasks opened per T1-T14 mapping (Task IDs renumber depending on Liam ratification of WP5 §3 proposed IDs).
- 6 retrospective Tasks (T1-T6) populated with `done` status + `<info added on …>` journal blocks citing commits + main-track session refs.
- docs/reference/umbrellas.json created with at minimum one umbrella `canonical-pipeline-implementation` listing the 14 task_ids + substrate_doc reference to PLAN.md.
- Cross-branch task-list divergence resolved at next merge cycle (stale-merge only per RESEARCH §6).
- PLAN.md retains role as spec substrate; Task descriptions reference PLAN.md sections via cross_doc_links.

## Dependencies

ID-9 (Astro docs) for auto-sync wiring; ID-30 (consolidation) if roadmap-source items also migrate. RESEARCH (31.1) is parallel-eligible with both — done.

**S67 W1c close-out — Wave 1 of ID-31 COMPLETE.** All 11 Subtasks (31.1-31.11) `done`. Subtask 31.7 added canonical-pipeline + 3 stub umbrellas to umbrellas.json; 31.8 added round-trip + cross-doc tests (76/76 PASS); 31.9 surgically extended update-roadmap-backlog skill (Create-mode task-list target + umbrella_id field + Step 8 umbrella membership); 31.10 appended MAX-ID cross-branch discipline to workflow-orchestration SKILL.md; 31.11 smoke gate green. Wave 3 retrospective T1-T6 retro Task opens pending — dispatched separately by future Orchestrator session via update-roadmap-backlog Create mode (task-list target + umbrella_id='canonical-pipeline').

Close-out gates: all per-Subtask Checker verdicts PASS or PASS_WITH_NOTES with promote=true; OOS findings triaged orch-direct (backlog ID-86 added for doc-freshness gap); 4 other findings disposed as no-action (informational/expected per spec). End-of-task code-simplification + quality-review Executor/Checker passes NOT invoked — Task scope was schema + data + tests + skill body (no substantial code surface warranting separate simplification pass).

subtasks.title: Author `lib/validation/umbrellas-schema.ts` + unit test
subtasks.description: Author the Zod schema for `umbrellas.json` per TECH §3.2. Three exports: `UmbrellaStatus` (4-value enum), `UmbrellaEntrySchema`, `UmbrellasSchema` (root). Strict mode throughout.
subtasks.details:
Author `lib/validation/umbrellas-schema.ts` per PLAN §2 Subtask 31.5. Mirror the existing pattern in `lib/validation/{task-list,backlog,roadmap}-schema.ts` — strict-mode Zod objects, no barrel re-export, named-export each schema + each inferred type.

Exports required:
- `UmbrellaStatus = z.enum(['proposed', 'in_progress', 'done', 'archived'])`.
- `UmbrellaEntrySchema` with 6 required fields (`id` kebab-case regex; `title` min(1); `substrate_doc` min(1); `task_ids` array of bare-id regex strings; `status` UmbrellaStatus; `phase` min(1)); .strict().
- `UmbrellasSchema` root with 5 fields (`document_name: z.literal('umbrellas')`; `document_purpose` min(1); `last_updated` with 200-char cap + single-line + single-session-id refine + kh-{prod-readiness|main}-S{N} prefix regex; `related_documents: z.array(z.string())`; `umbrellas: z.array(UmbrellaEntrySchema)`); .strict().

Imports: `import { z } from 'zod'`; `import { BARE_ID_REGEX } from '@/lib/validation/schemas'`. No barrel re-export.

FORWARD-COMPAT CONSTRAINT (LOAD-BEARING per Wave 2 merge): The 6 UmbrellaEntry field names — `id`, `title`, `substrate_doc`, `task_ids`, `status`, `phase` — are LOCKED. ID-9 docubot (Wave 7) consumes verbatim. No renames without surfacing as T-OQ.

Unit test cases in `__tests__/validation/umbrellas-schema.test.ts`: valid root parses; each enum value accepted; invalid id-shape rejected; valid id-shape accepted; empty task_ids[] accepted; invalid task_id rejected; multi-membership accepted; .strict() rejects unknown fields; last_updated discipline (multi-line / multi-session / >200 char rejected, valid kh-prefix accepted).

Verify: `bun run test -- umbrellas-schema` green; `tsc --noEmit` zero new errors.

NO HELPER FILE in this Subtask (lives in 31.6). NO `umbrellas.json` authoring (lives in 31.7).

WAVE 2 STRICT-SEQUENCE: dispatch AFTER ID-30 PR-A schema additions merge (OQ-C2). Pre-dispatch check: `git log --oneline origin/production-readiness -- lib/validation/backlog-schema.ts` must show PR-A's `rank` addition.

<info added on 2026-05-22T15:57:11.000Z>
**Shipped:** `lib/validation/umbrellas-schema.ts` (new module, 161 LOC) authoring 3 named exports — `UmbrellaStatus` (4-value enum: proposed | in_progress | done | archived), `UmbrellaEntrySchema` (6 LOCKED fields: id, title, substrate_doc, task_ids, status, phase — strict mode), `UmbrellasSchema` (root document with 5 fields incl. kh-{track}-S{N} `last_updated` freshness-marker discipline mirroring task-list-schema — strict mode). Plus `__tests__/validation/umbrellas-schema.test.ts` (54 assertions across 9 describe blocks: enum membership + 4 status cases, kebab-case id regex + 10 valid/invalid shape cases, BARE_ID_REGEX task_ids + 9 shape cases, required-field enforcement + strict mode + 11 cases, root-level multi-membership and strict mode + 6 cases, last_updated freshness-marker discipline + 7 cases).
**Commit:** 7f97f0ac (full SHA 7f97f0ac8453e1cfe6aa96f90f18f0f117a40502).
**Files touched:** `lib/validation/umbrellas-schema.ts` (new), `__tests__/validation/umbrellas-schema.test.ts` (new), `docs/reference/task-list.json` (status flip pending → in_progress only).
**Acceptance (per testStrategy):** `bun run test -- umbrellas-schema` → 54/54 pass; `tsc --noEmit` → 34 errors (matches S65 close baseline — zero new errors); ESLint clean. All testStrategy criteria met (4 enum values, kebab-case regex, BARE_ID_REGEX on task_ids, strict-mode rejection, last_updated discipline, multi-membership acceptance).
**Deviations:** (1) Followed canonical TECH §3.2 kebab-case regex `/^[a-z][a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9]$/` rather than the brief's example `/^[a-z][a-z0-9-]*$/` (brief uses 'e.g.' wording — TECH §3.2 is canonical; the strict TECH regex correctly rejects trailing dashes per brief test cases). Min id length is 2 chars under the TECH regex — no single-char ids supported; aligns with realistic kebab-slug usage. (2) Added several additional test cases beyond the brief's minimum list (rejection of leading dash, uppercase letters, internal whitespace, empty id; rejection of ID-* prefix and leading-whitespace task_ids; rejection of empty-string for title / substrate_doc / phase). All additions tighten coverage without contradicting the brief.

subtasks.title: Add round-trip tests for `umbrellas.json` (self-parse + cross-doc with task-list)
subtasks.description: Author two round-trip tests per TECH §3.4: schema self-parse + cross-doc validation of task_ids[] against real Task entries. Orphans warn (don't fail) per P-OQ-2.
subtasks.details:
Author two new test files per TECH §3.4 verbatim.

Test 1: `__tests__/validation/umbrellas-roundtrip.test.ts` — readFileSync `docs/reference/umbrellas.json`, JSON.parse + UmbrellasSchema.parse, expect umbrellas.length > 0.

Test 2: `__tests__/docs/umbrellas-task-list-roundtrip.test.ts` — reads both umbrellas.json AND task-list.json; describe block 'umbrellas ↔ task-list round-trip (PRODUCT inv 9)'.
- it block 1 'every umbrellas[].task_ids[] entry references a real Task': for each umbrella + each task_id, assert realTaskIds.has(id); collect broken into array; expect(broken).toEqual([]). HARD FAIL on broken reference (canary).
- it block 2 'warns (but does not fail) on orphan Tasks (P-OQ-2 default)': build set of assigned ids; for each Task in task-list, if not in set, push to orphans[]; if orphans.length > 0 console.warn; expect(true).toBe(true) — never fail (soft warning only).

Use exact implementations from TECH §3.4 lines 299-353 (copy verbatim, adjust import paths to `@/lib/validation/umbrellas-schema` + `@/lib/validation/task-list-schema`).

The set-membership pattern means tests pass regardless of task-list.json HEAD shape (don't bake in specific Task ids).

Expected steady-state at first run: canonical-pipeline.task_ids[] is empty (per 31.7), so every existing Task is orphan. console.warn fires once with list. Test still passes per P-OQ-2.

subtasks.details:

T-OQ-2 RATIFIED DEFAULT: Extend Create mode (NOT add new OpenTask mode). Add `task-list` as target option. Add shared `umbrella_id` field.

If ID-30 PR-A NOT merged at dispatch time, ESCALATE — do NOT proceed.

Changes:
(a) Create mode — add `task-list` as third target (alongside `product-roadmap` + `product-backlog`). Writes new top-level Task into `task-list.json#/tasks`. All required TaskSchema fields supplied (id MUST equal MAX_ID_ACROSS_BRANCHES + 1; all four nullable fields present with explicit null acceptable).
(b) Shared `umbrella_id: string | null` (default null) field on Create + Promote. When non-null AND destination is a Task: triggers same-commit edit to umbrellas.json appending Task id to named umbrella's task_ids[] (idempotent). When null: no umbrella edit (orphan per P-OQ-2 soft warning).
(c) New skill step '9. Umbrella membership edit (optional)': load umbrellas.json; parse via UmbrellasSchema; find umbrella by id (error+abort if not found); append destination id idempotently; bump last_updated per field-discipline; re-validate via UmbrellasSchema.parse; abort on failure. Commit BOTH task-list.json AND umbrellas.json in single commit (PRODUCT inv 17 commit-coupling).

Document forward-Task open pattern (TECH §6.5): compute fresh resolved id via cross-branch MAX-ID; open Task with spec-chain Subtasks per `spec-driven-implementation`; call `update-roadmap-backlog` Create with `target: 'task-list'` + `umbrella_id: 'canonical-pipeline'` (or applicable); optionally append PLAN.md §4.n backlink per Inv 14.

ID-30 PR-A's `capability_theme` parameter coexists peacefully — combined Promote signature becomes Promote(source_backlog_id, dest_task_id, [capability_theme], [umbrella_id]). Both optional, different fields.

Verify: diff against pre-edit SKILL.md shows only Step 9 + Create-mode target addition + `umbrella_id` field addition (no other rewrites). Manual dry-run with fixture Task open. Skill body length stays within 25-soft-ceiling.

subtasks.title: Scaffold codemod + author defineRoute() wrapper
subtasks.description: Create scripts/codemods/wrap-define-route.ts skeleton (CLI argv parse stub, ts-morph Project init, route enumeration with --scope filter, exit-code 0/1 contract) and author lib/api/define-route.ts — the typed wrapper function that downstream Subtasks rewrite handlers to call.
subtasks.details:
Create new files: scripts/codemods/wrap-define-route.ts (skeleton only — no rewrite logic yet, just CLI stub + route enumeration + exit 0); lib/api/define-route.ts (new wrapper module taking Zod ResponseSchema + async handler, returning Next.js-compatible route export; validates handler return payload against schema or 500 envelope on failure); __tests__/lib/api/define-route.test.ts (wrapper unit tests). Per PRODUCT.md §9 + TECH.md §1, the codemod lives at scripts/codemods/wrap-define-route.ts; per TECH §2.1 ts-morph Project init reads tsconfig.json (keep skipAddingFilesFromTsConfig default false so app/api routes load); per TECH §2.2 route enumeration filters via app/api/.*/route\.ts regex on getFilePath(). NOTE: OQ-1 (HIGH priority) — PRODUCT §9 declares defineRoute() OOS for the codemod itself but TECH and AC-1..AC-10 require it; PLAN folds wrapper authoring into this Subtask as the canonical resolution. Confirm with sub-orchestrator that OQ-1 is resolved as fold-in BEFORE dispatching this Subtask. Use bun run test (NOT bun test) per TECH §8.6. Auth helper pattern unchanged from existing routes (getAuthorisedClient returns {success}; authFailureResponse(auth) for failure).

subtasks.title: Shape classifier
subtasks.description: Implement classifyRoute(sf: SourceFile): RouteShape function per TECH §2.3.
subtasks.details:
Add classifyRoute() function and getExportedMethods() helper to scripts/codemods/wrap-define-route.ts. Create scripts/codemods/types.ts with RouteShape discriminated union (AUTH_PLAIN | PARAM_BODY | BODY_VALIDATED | PARAM | MULTI_PARAM_BODY | MULTI_BODY | MULTI_PARAM | CRON | NAKED_NO_AUTH | MCP with +WRC sub-variants) and NeedsManualReason type (CRON_AUTH_MODEL | NAKED_NO_AUTH | MCP_TRANSPORT | MULTI_METHOD_SCHEMA | WRC_COMPOSITION | NEEDS_SCHEMA). Classifier inspects: imports (getImportDeclarations + getModuleSpecifierValue check for @/lib/auth + getAuthorisedClient/getAuthenticatedClient named imports); path-parameterisation ([ in path); body usage (request.json() or parseBody( in getFullText()); withRequestContext presence (getFullText().includes('withRequestContext')); exported methods (getExportedMethods returns names of FunctionDeclaration + VariableStatement-with-withRequestContext patterns). Per TECH §2.3 example code, classification priority: CRON > MCP > NAKED_NO_AUTH > multi-method > single-method variants.

subtasks.title: ResponseSchema inference Source A (type-drift-baseline.json)
subtasks.description: Implement Source A inference per TECH §3.A — read docs/generated/type-drift-baseline.json, map interface name to route file via R-WP17 heuristic URL matcher, look up ${interfaceName}Schema or ${interfaceName}ZodSchema in lib/validation/schemas.ts. Also authors the 15th fixture (with-baseline-but-no-schema-constant.ts) exercising the fall-back path.
subtasks.details:
Add inferSchema(sf, method, project) function to scripts/codemods/wrap-define-route.ts implementing Source A logic. Extract to scripts/codemods/inference-source-a.ts if baseline-loading + interface-to-route mapping exceeds 50 LOC. Read docs/generated/type-drift-baseline.json (existing 37-interface baseline shipped by R-WP17). For each baseline entry: (1) map interface name → route file via the heuristic URL matcher (re-use logic from lib/ast-dataflow/queries/type-drift-detect.ts — DO NOT modify that file); (2) look up ${interfaceName}Schema or ${interfaceName}ZodSchema in lib/validation/schemas.ts via name-convention; (3) if found, return schema identifier; (4) if not found, return z.unknown() + NEEDS_SCHEMA reason code for codemod-needs-manual.json. Per TECH §3.A trade-off, Zod schema naming conventions are not enforced — fall-back path is expected for some interfaces. AC-5 requires real schema for baseline routes WITH a discoverable Schema constant; placeholder path is acceptable per AC-6 (TODO comment). Authors 15th fixture __tests__/scripts/codemods/fixtures/wrap-define-route/with-baseline-but-no-schema-constant.ts (counterpart to 32.7's with-schema-in-baseline.ts; exercises the z.unknown() + NEEDS_SCHEMA fall-back).

<info added on 2026-05-22T13:43:43.000Z>
**Shipped:** Source A inference for `wrap-define-route` codemod. `inferSchema(sf, method, project, options?)` exported from `scripts/codemods/wrap-define-route.ts`, delegating to `inferSchemaSourceA()` in the new `scripts/codemods/inference-source-a.ts` helper module. Algorithm reads `docs/generated/type-drift-baseline.json`, translates route file path → candidate URL, walks `lib/query/fetchers.ts` for `fetchJson<T>(url)` calls, filters by baseline-interface membership, then looks up `${T}Schema` or `${T}ZodSchema` in `lib/validation/schemas.ts`. Returns the schema identifier on hit; returns `z.unknown()` + `NEEDS_SCHEMA` reason on miss. Authors the 15th fixture (`with-baseline-but-no-schema-constant.ts`) exercising the fall-back path; bumps fixture-count guard 14 → 15 in the corpus harness.

subtasks.title: Handler rewrite — single-method shapes
subtasks.description: Implement rewrite logic per TECH §2.4 for AUTH_PLAIN, PARAM_BODY, BODY_VALIDATED, PARAM single-method shapes (137 MECHANISABLE routes). Includes withRequestContext sub-variant per TECH §8.1 / AC-7.
subtasks.details:
Add rewriteSingleMethod(sf, method, schema) function to scripts/codemods/wrap-define-route.ts; extract to scripts/codemods/rewrite-single-method.ts if exceeds 80 LOC. Targets FunctionDeclaration AST nodes with names in {GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE, HEAD, OPTIONS} ONLY — skips export const maxDuration / dynamic / runtime per TECH §8.3. Rewrites: 'export async function METHOD(req: NextRequest) { body }' → 'export const METHOD = defineRoute(Schema, async (req: NextRequest) => { body });'. Preserves Next.js 15 Promise<params> second argument per TECH §8.2 (78 of 92 parameterised routes use this shape). For withRequestContext sub-variant per TECH §8.1: outer wrapper order MUST be 'export const POST = withRequestContext(defineRoute(Schema, async (req) => { ... }))' — NEVER the reverse (defineRoute outside, withRequestContext inside) because request-context propagation depends on outer-wrap order. AC-7 explicitly asserts this. Also: add the 'import { defineRoute } from "@/lib/api/define-route"' import declaration if not present (use sf.getImportDeclaration check first per TECH §2.4 Step A). Place TODO comment '// TODO(OPS-T1): author ResponseSchema' on preceding line for routes inferring z.unknown() per AC-6.

<info added on 2026-05-22T22:03:07.409Z>

Implements TECH §2.4 Step A (`defineRoute` import-add, idempotent) + Step B (FunctionDeclaration → `export const METHOD = defineRoute(Schema, async (...) => { ... });` or +WRC inner-replace). Preserves Next.js 15 `Promise<params>` second-argument shape per TECH §8.2. AC-6 TODO comment (`// TODO(OPS-T1): author ResponseSchema`) placed on preceding line when inference returns `{ schema: 'z.unknown()', reason: 'NEEDS_SCHEMA' }`.

<info added on 2026-05-22T22:20:52.569Z>
**Shipped:** Subtask 32.11 — multi-method handler-rewrite slice for the OPS-T1 codemod. `rewriteMultiMethod(sf, methods, schemas, route, shape)` added in the new sibling helper `scripts/codemods/rewrite-multi-method.ts` (~130 LOC) and re-exported from `scripts/codemods/wrap-define-route.ts` so the call-site contract stays anchored. Implementation is intentionally thin: composes Subtask 32.10's `rewriteSingleMethod` once per exported method (per the brief's per-method dispatch loop), with a NEEDS_SCHEMA fall-back guarding missing schema entries so a partial map cannot produce an invalid rewrite. Returns one `NeedsManualEntry` per method with reason `MULTI_METHOD_SCHEMA` and a single-element `methods` array naming the affected method — pins the schema slot to a specific (route, method) pair in the dry-run report per TECH §6.2.

**Files touched:**

- Plus four supporting tests (MULTI_BODY wrap, MULTI_PARAM wrap, per-method NEEDS_SCHEMA fall-back TODO placement, import idempotency) — all PASS.

**Test counts:**

**Architectural deviations (empirical findings per Q-EX2 discipline):**
- Extracted to `rewrite-multi-method.ts` (sibling module) per the brief's "extract to scripts/codemods/rewrite-multi-method.ts if needed". The brief's preferred default ("add function to wrap-define-route.ts") would have inflated `wrap-define-route.ts` past the >800 LOC mark; honouring the sibling-extraction precedent set by 32.10 keeps the canonical `wrap-define-route.ts` import target intact via re-export.
- The `rewriteMultiMethod` signature carries `route: string` + `shape: RouteShape` parameters beyond the brief's `(sf, methods, schemas)` shape. Rationale: the function must emit `NeedsManualEntry` records that carry `route` (TECH §6.2 — `NeedsManualEntry.route: string`) and `shape` (TECH §6.2 — `NeedsManualEntry.shape: string`) fields. Without those parameters the helper would have to recompute the route path from `sf.getFilePath()` (which can be a synthetic in-memory path under test) and re-classify the file (duplicating 32.6's `classifyRoute` work). The caller already computes both via `toRepoRelativePosixPath()` + `classifyRoute()` in `wrap-define-route.ts`'s discovery loop, so threading them through avoids the duplication. Brief intent honoured — the per-method dispatch loop is still the load-bearing logic.
- Per-method NEEDS_SCHEMA fall-back: when the caller's `schemas` map omits a method entry, this helper substitutes `{ schema: 'z.unknown()', reason: 'NEEDS_SCHEMA' }` so the rewrite still produces a syntactically valid output (with the AC-6 TODO comment on the affected method's line only — asserted by the supporting test). Forward-compatible with Subtask 32.8's Source A output shape and Subtask 32.9's Source B chain.

This is a wiring concern for 32.14's apply-mode dispatch, not 32.11's scope.
- Subtask 32.13 (idempotency check) can now layer atop both `rewriteSingleMethod` and `rewriteMultiMethod` — `isAlreadyWrapped(sf, method)` should be called per-method inside the multi-method loop's per-method body so a partially-wrapped multi-method route (e.g. GET already wrapped but POST still raw) re-applies correctly to only the unwrapped methods.
- +WRC multi-method fixture coverage: the 32.7 corpus has no dedicated MULTI_*+WRC fixtures (called out in 32.10 close-out OOS F2 and re-confirmed here). The AC-7 outer-wrap invariant is exercised end-to-end by the AUTH_PLAIN+WRC single-method fixture; multi-method +WRC routes share the same code path per-method (via `rewriteSingleMethod`'s +WRC branch) so the inherited coverage is the floor. Surface as a backlog candidate before Subtask 32.16's full-corpus acceptance gate runs.

**Out-of-scope observations (for Curator triage):**

The current helper signature takes `methods: readonly string[]` directly (the caller supplies the array), which decouples the helper from the classifier's enumeration order. subtasks.title: Idempotency check

Implementation: inspect the VariableStatement / FunctionDeclaration matching the method name; check if its initialiser is a CallExpression with identifier 'defineRoute' at the top level (including the withRequestContext-wrapped form: withRequestContext(defineRoute(...))). If wrapped, mark route as SKIPPED, add 'api/items/[id]/route.ts — SKIPPED (already wrapped)' line to dry-run.md, do NOT add entry to codemod-needs-manual.json (skipped routes are not NEEDS-REVIEW or MANUAL). subtasks.title: Apply mode + format pass

subtasks.details: Add applyAll(routes) function and runFormatPass(modifiedFilePaths) helper to scripts/codemods/wrap-define-route.ts. In dry-run mode, sf.save() skipped — only sf.print() vs original text computed for diff preview. In apply mode (--apply flag), iterate over MECHANISABLE + NEEDS-REVIEW routes, invoke sf.save() per file. After save loop completes, run 'bun run format' against the modified file paths (use child_process.spawnSync with the path list as args) OR invoke ts-morph's sf.organizeImports() before save. Per TECH §8.5, the import-ordering pass is needed because ts-morph's addImportDeclaration may insert at a non-Prettier-compatible position. Per AC-8 ('bun run test' passes after apply) and AC-9 ('bun lint' reports 0 errors), the format pass also reduces lint surface.

Both files document the canonical sequence per PRODUCT §7: (1) bun scripts/codemods/wrap-define-route.ts (dry-run); (2) review codemod-dry-run.md; (3) author missing ResponseSchema objects for placeholder routes; (4) bun scripts/codemods/wrap-define-route.ts --apply; (5) bun run ast-dataflow type-drift-detect --pretty (human review); (6) update docs/generated/type-drift-baseline.json to remove closed-gap entries; (7) bun run test + bun lint; (8) bun run ast-dataflow type-drift-detect --ci (must exit 0); (9) raise migration PR. Per PLAN §4 32.16: per-AC mode split — AC-1, AC-2, AC-3, AC-4, AC-5, AC-6, AC-7 use read-only dry-run against the live app/api/ directory (verify post-rewrite text via sf.print() without saving); AC-8, AC-9, AC-10 use apply-against-temp-copy mode (copy app/api/ + lib/validation/schemas.ts + docs/generated/type-drift-baseline.json into tmpdir-scoped fixture corpus, run codemod with --apply against temp copy, then run bun run test + bun lint + type-drift-detect --ci against temp copy; teardown at test exit). Per test-philosophy §5, each it() block titled per its AC ('AC-1: dry-run completes without exception on 193 routes', 'AC-8: bun run test passes after apply against temp corpus', etc.). Per test-philosophy §1, assertions are on observable output (rewritten text, artefact file contents, exit codes), never on ts-morph internal state. Test must not exceed 4-shard quality-test job budget — if temp-copy ACs exceed budget, mark that block as separate integration test under __tests__/integration/. NOTE: OQ-3 (MEDIUM priority) — AC-9 may need reinterpretation as 'no new lint errors' vs absolute 0; confirm with sub-orchestrator before final assertion shape.

subtasks.title: Classifier `getFullText().includes()` → AST refactor (pre-empt 32.16 acceptance gate)
subtasks.description: Replace classifyRoute() body-detection getFullText().includes() substring scan in scripts/codemods/wrap-define-route.ts with ts-morph CallExpression walk filtered by getExpression().getText() match. Restricts detection to executable code only, eliminating the latent JSDoc/comment false-positive risk surfaced by S64 W1 Checker on Subtask 32.7. Extends fixture corpus with JSDoc-poisoned PARAM/MULTI_PARAM regression case. Pre-empts 32.16 acceptance gate per S62E sub-o 1 brief.
subtasks.details:
classifyRoute() currently uses getFullText().includes('request.json()') and getFullText().includes('parseBody(') to classify route body shape. This scans comments and JSDoc as well as executable code, creating a latent false-positive: any route file (fixture or production) that mentions discriminator substrings in JSDoc/inline comments will be misclassified. The 32.7 fixture corpus worked around this by deliberately omitting those substrings from JSDoc bodies on PARAM/MULTI_PARAM fixtures. Fix: replace the substring scan with sf.getDescendantsOfKind(SyntaxKind.CallExpression) filtered by getExpression().getText() match — restricts detection to executable code only, per ts-morph conventions already used in the codemod surface. Extend unit test fixtures with at least one JSDoc-poisoned PARAM/MULTI_PARAM fixture to prevent regression. Detection cost is high if deferred to the 32.16 production-route acceptance gate (137 mechanisable + 40 needs-review routes may carry JSDoc mentioning these substrings). Surfaced: S64 W1 sub-o Checker on Subtask 32.7 (commit b039e1b1). Impl ref commit: 7c6374ed (Subtask 32.6). File: scripts/codemods/wrap-define-route.ts — classifyRoute() body-detection branch.

Source commit refs: b039e1b1 (S64 W1 Checker surfacing), 7c6374ed (Subtask 32.6 impl). Amends 32.16 dependencies [15] → [15, 17] so the AST refactor lands before the acceptance gate.

(Identifier OR PropertyAccessExpression callee for module-style helpers.parseBody(...)). tasks.title: Cocoindex flow scaffolding + Cloud Run sidecar deploy (T8)
tasks.description:
Stand up the cocoindex 1.0.3 pipeline scaffolding for the canonical document → embeddings flow, and the Cloud Run sidecar that hosts the heavy extractor binaries (Docling 1.8 GB + pullmd 3.7 GB Playwright). T8 is the foundational pipeline implementation step in `docs/specs/canonical-pipeline-implementation-plan/PLAN.md` §4.8 — downstream T7 (Phew Q&A first-ingest) depends on T8 standing up the engine boundary. Multi-session arc: S252 authors the full {N.1}–{N.4} spec chain (RESEARCH → PRODUCT → TECH → PLAN) only. Implementation Subtasks {28.5+} dispatch in S253+ after PLAN.md ratifies.

## Acceptance criteria

- Subtask {28.1} RESEARCH.md captures cocoindex 1.0.3 integration constraints (sandbox-disable + walk_dir recursive=True default + layered fn-shape COCO.10), Cloud Run sidecar deploy shape (Docling + pullmd + Playwright sizing), op_id propagation per `02-data-flow.md` §5 / N7, Spike #1 (cocoindex schema-coupling Scenario A) + Spike #2 status, and single-spec vs multi-spec decision.
- Subtask {28.2} PRODUCT.md numbered Behavior invariants covering: cocoindex flow observability (localfs → 6-stage pipeline → `content_items` UPSERT); sidecar deploy contract (Docling + pullmd availability at engine boundary); op_id propagation contract for audit trail; `recordPipelineRun` rollup per `02-data-flow.md` §7.2; ExtractByLlm contract per Q-EX2 (T1 dep); failure-mode behaviour (retry / dead-letter / observability).
- Subtask {28.3} TECH.md one-to-one Proposed changes per PRODUCT invariant: `scripts/cocoindex_pipeline/flow.py` scaffolding; outer-tier `@coco.fn` source-binding adapter; inner-tier extractors consuming `content_text: str` (COCO.10); `ExtractByLlm` + `LiteLLMEmbedder` + `postgres.mount_table_target(managed_by='user')`; Cloud Run sidecar Dockerfile + `cloud-run-deploy.yml`; op_id columns + audit_log trigger; integration test design.
- Subtask {28.4} PLAN.md decomposes TECH into TM-shape implementation Subtasks {28.5+} with load-bearing details + one-line testStrategy + sibling-only deps.

## Dependencies

T1 spec ratification (Q-EX2 ExtractByLlm contract) — RESEARCH may surface T12 dependency shape; flag if so. No hard cross-Task blockers in main-tree task-list at session-start (production-readiness has ID-24/25/26 pending which may also surface during merge cadence).

subtasks.title: RESEARCH — cocoindex 1.0.3 + Cloud Run sidecar + op_id + multi-spec decision
subtasks.description: Investigate the existing cocoindex integration constraints, Cloud Run sidecar deployment shape, op_id propagation rules, and Spike #1/#2 status. Decide whether T8 lands as a single spec or splits.
subtasks.details:

Output: `docs/specs/cocoindex-flow-scaffolding/RESEARCH.md` (slug provisional — RESEARCH ratification may rename if multi-spec split).

Required coverage:
1. **cocoindex 1.0.3 constraints**: sandbox-disable required for PyPI install + Rust-engine LMDB startup (CLAUDE.md gotcha); `localfs.walk_dir(recursive=False)` default — explicit `recursive=True` for nested corpora; layered fn-shape `content_text: str` per COCO.10 (existing `@coco.fn` examples via `bun scripts/ast-dataflow-cli.ts importers '@coco.fn'`).

3. **op_id propagation**: hybrid pattern per `docs/plans/phase-0-investigation/architecture/02-data-flow.md` §5 + N7 — propagation to target columns + audit_log trigger interaction.
4. **Spike #1 (cocoindex schema-coupling Scenario A) + Spike #2 (memo-hit) status**: `docs/plans/phase-0-investigation/0.9-spike-plan.md` §1 + §2.
5. **Single-spec vs multi-spec decision**: does T8 land as one `cocoindex-flow-scaffolding` spec, or split into `cocoindex-flow` + `cloud-run-sidecar` + `extractor-functions`?

Must-reads: PLAN.md §4.8, 02-data-flow.md §3-§5+§7.2, 03-tech-stack.md §6.2 cocoindex + §6.3 LiteLLM, 0.9-decision-graph.md §11.4 op_id ratification, 0.9-spike-plan.md §1+§2.

RESEARCH.md authored at `docs/specs/cocoindex-flow-scaffolding/RESEARCH.md` (new dir + file, ~650 lines, UK English `<!-- Last verified: 21/05/2026 -->`). Worktree: `agent-ad123585d709d0dc6` (base commit 40e5cceb). Branch isolation verified pre-write (NOT leaking to `content-items-investigation` / `main` / `production-readiness`).

**Coverage of 5 required areas (one line each):**
1. cocoindex 1.0.3 constraints — sandbox-disable for install + LMDB startup; walk_dir(recursive=False) default; layered fn-shape COCO.10 (`scripts/ontology-sync/parse-flow.py` is canonical KH stub; `spike/cocoindex_s1/probe_managed_by_user.py` is canonical live-wiring shape with `coco.AppConfig(name=, main_fn=)` + `mount_table_target(managed_by=ManagedBy.USER)`); 4 API drifts confirmed via S2 (sources→connectors, App-class, LMDB, walk_dir default); cocoindex NOT yet in `requirements.txt` (T8 first to add).
2. Cloud Run sidecar — Docling 1.8 GB + pullmd ~3.7 GB Playwright + cocoindex (Rust + LMDB) all exceed Vercel 250 MB; existing baseline `.github/workflows/cloud-run-deploy.yml` + 4 per-tenant manifests in `cloudrun/jobs/` covers 80% of need; gaps: cocoindex+docling+litellm deps, per-tenant Service manifest expansion, container image size budget verification (~5.3 GB target), Service-vs-Job decision (recommend Service per S14 default); runbook actual filename is `cloud-run-phase-1.md` (no `-handover` suffix).
3. op_id propagation — N7 hybrid (cocoindex per-flow op_id + trigger-driven audit_log) RESOLVED-S236; column-level inventory: `content_items.op_id` (T2 combined-PR), `q_a_extractions.op_id` (verify T6 inclusion), `source_documents.op_id` (likely T8 follow-up ALTER per O-Q1); trigger-level: existing `content_history_auto_version` fires unchanged on cocoindex `INSERT ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE`; `audit_log` is platform-level (NOT Q-OQR1-16 scope) — v1 uses `RAISE LOG` per RLS-PATTERN P-5 [DEFERRED-v1.1].

Spike #1 + Spike #2 status — S1 CLOSED-S230 (Scenario A confirmed via `managed_by="user"` mode; verbatim docstring + statediff source); S2 CLOSED-S229 (localfs-only v1 + memo-hit validated: cold 35 / warm 0 / mutated 4 invocations on 35-file canonical corpus); +S14 also closed (single-orchestrator-instance Cloud Run topology). All three relevant spikes CLOSED — no PENDING-SPIKE blocker.
5. Single-spec vs multi-spec decision — **RECOMMEND SINGLE SPEC** at slug `cocoindex-flow-scaffolding`. Reasoning: (a) scope coherence (3 candidate splits tightly mutually coupled), (b) Subtask 25-soft-ceiling headroom (~10-12 subtasks for T8 — well under 25), (c) no genuine independent ratification surface, (d) aligned with existing precedent (rls-pattern / reserved-workspace-seats / intelligence-workspaces all bundle similar coupling-density), (e) spec slug stability (rename is cheap; landing single now is lower-risk). Existing cocoindex-extraction-contract + cocoindex-ledger-api + content-model-invariants specs (T1.1+T1.2+T1.4) already absorb the multi-spec-split candidates; T8 does NOT duplicate.

**Open questions: 8 surfaced (O-Q1 through O-Q8)** — all carry recommended-default ratifiable in Liam pre-flight: source_documents.op_id ALTER home; Service vs Job sidecar shape; pullmd co-location (separate per AGPL boundary); container image budget; LiteLLM dependency pin; LMDB pre-warm; CocoInsight DEFERRED note; T7 source-binding cutover sequencing.

- Area D (recordPipelineRun rollup): 4 invariants (Inv-16..Inv-19) — one pipeline_runs row per invocation (incl. memo-hit polls), per-stage counters observable, all writes via recordPipelineRun() helper per CLAUDE.md gotcha (code-discipline), pipeline_failures table RATIFIED-DO-NOT-BUILD per COCO.7.

subtasks.title: Migration — op_id column propagation + B-tree partial indexes on 4 tables
subtasks.description: DDL migration adding op_id uuid NULL + B-tree partial index on content_items, q_a_extractions, source_documents, pipeline_runs. IF NOT EXISTS-guarded; BLOCKER for op_id-consuming Subtasks 28.8/28.9/28.11.
subtasks.details:
## Dispatch brief — Subtask 28.5 (Migration: op_id column propagation)

**Covers TECH P-change:** P-4 (migration half).
**Covers PRODUCT inv:** Inv-11 (per-row op_id stamping substrate), Inv-12 (op_id round-trip column substrate).
**Sibling deps:** none — first Wave A node, no upstream blockers.

### Goal
Land the schema substrate for cocoindex per-flow op_id stamping. ADD COLUMN-only; no PL/pgSQL functions; no destructive operations. Single migration file under `supabase/migrations/<timestamp>_t8_op_id_propagation.sql`.

### Files to create
- `supabase/migrations/<timestamp>_t8_op_id_propagation.sql` — DDL per TECH §P-4 sketch:
  - `SET search_path = public, extensions;` (top of file)
  - `ALTER TABLE public.content_items ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS op_id uuid NULL;`
  - `CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_content_items_op_id ON public.content_items (op_id) WHERE op_id IS NOT NULL;`
  - Same pattern for `q_a_extractions`, `source_documents`, `pipeline_runs` (4 tables total).
  - `COMMENT ON COLUMN` for all 4 columns naming the spec reference.

### Apply procedure (per TECH §3)
1. Pre-push: `cat supabase/.temp/project-ref` — must match expected project (`turayklvaunphgbgscat` for staging, `rovrymhhffssilaftdwd` for prod). Relink via `supabase link --project-ref <correct>` if drift detected (CLAUDE.md gotcha).
2. Pre-push column-existence verification via `information_schema.columns` query — IF NOT EXISTS no-ops are safe but worth confirming first run is the first time.
3. Apply via `/opt/homebrew/bin/supabase db push` with `dangerouslyDisableSandbox: true` (POSTGRES_PASSWORD env var set).
4. Post-apply: re-run the column query; assert all 4 columns present.
5. Type regen: `/opt/homebrew/bin/supabase gen types typescript --project-id rovrymhhffssilaftdwd --schema public > supabase/types/database.types.ts` and commit.

### CLAUDE.md gotchas
- DDL via CLI only (NOT MCP `apply_migration` / `execute_sql`).
- IF NOT EXISTS guards on every ALTER for idempotency.
- No PL/pgSQL functions in this migration so the anon-EXECUTE-via-PUBLIC pattern is NOT triggered — but if v1.1 follow-up adds e.g. `audit_log_emit(...)` helper, that migration MUST apply: REVOKE ... FROM PUBLIC + REVOKE ... FROM anon + GRANT ... TO authenticated, service_role.
- `.temp/project-ref` drift verification per CLAUDE.md gotcha.
- Index creation uses default (non-CONCURRENTLY) — v1 corpus scale completes in <1 s. If prod scale crosses 1M rows on any of the 4 tables, swap to CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY and split into two migrations.

### Acceptance test shape
- Post-apply: `SELECT column_name FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_schema='public' AND column_name='op_id' AND table_name IN ('content_items','q_a_extractions','source_documents','pipeline_runs');` returns 4 rows.

subtasks.title: Cloud Run sidecar Service deploy infra (4 per-tenant Services + Docling pre-warm)
subtasks.description: 4 per-tenant Cloud Run Service manifests, new cloudbuild-cocoindex.yaml, Docling pre-warm script, requirements.txt pins, .github/workflows/cloud-run-deploy.yml extension. ~5.3 GB image budget (log+warn per T-OQ4). pullmd remains separate per O-Q3 AGPL boundary.
subtasks.details:
## Dispatch brief — Subtask 28.6 (Cloud Run sidecar Service deploy)

**Covers TECH P-change:** P-1.
**Covers PRODUCT inv:** Inv-6, Inv-7, Inv-8, Inv-9, Inv-10.
**Sibling deps:** none — Wave A parallel node alongside 28.5 and 28.7.

### Goal
Add 4 per-tenant Cloud Run **Services** (NOT Jobs per O-Q2 ratification) alongside the existing per-tenant Jobs at `cloudrun/jobs/{prod,staging}-{kpf,phew}.yaml`. Services host cocoindex engine (Rust + LMDB) + Docling (1.8 GB MIT) in one container image. pullmd remains a separately-deployed Service per O-Q3 AGPL boundary — the cocoindex sidecar calls pullmd via HTTP.

### Files to create
- `cloudrun/services/{prod,staging}-{kpf,phew}-cocoindex.yaml` (4 files). Each:
  - `min_instances: 1, max_instances: 1` per S14 single-orchestrator-instance topology.
  - `cpu: 2, memory: 4Gi` (Docling model in memory; ~5.3 GB image needs headroom).
  - `containerConcurrency: 10` (cocoindex single-writer LMDB; in-process concurrency only).
  - `serviceAccountName: {phew,kpf}-pipeline-sa@...` (REUSE existing per-tenant SAs).
  - Secret Manager mounts via `--set-secrets` (reuse existing 11-secret loop in workflow).
  - Env vars: `COCOINDEX_SOURCE_PATH` (empty default per O-Q8), `IMAGE_SHA` (injected by Cloud Build), `PIPELINE_RUN_WEBHOOK_URL` (mounted via Secret Manager — consumed by 28.11), `PULLMD_SERVICE_URL` (mounted; consumed by 28.7's _pullmd_to_markdown call).
- `cloudrun/cloudbuild-cocoindex.yaml` — second cloudbuild config building a new image `kh-cocoindex-pipeline` (separate from existing `kh-pipeline`). Buildpack `gcr.io/buildpacks/python` + extra Docling pre-warm step that downloads layout-heron + docling-models at build time (NOT runtime) per O-Q4 cold-start tolerance.
  - Image-size budget: ~5.3 GB target.
  - **T-OQ4 ratified: log+warn at v1** (NOT fail-build). Emit warning if image >5.3 GB; ratchet to hard-enforce in T13 if bloat becomes load-bearing.
  - Post-build assertion step: `docker image inspect` returns zero `pullmd` binary entries + zero Playwright dependencies (Inv-9 enforcement).
- `cloudrun/cocoindex-prewarm.py` — build-time script: imports Docling and triggers model download (`docling.document_converter.DocumentConverter()` lazy-loads on first call; pre-warm forces fetch into image layer). Runs under `dangerouslyDisableSandbox: true` per CLAUDE.md cocoindex gotcha.
- `cloudrun/services/README.md` — operator-facing doc: Service vs Job split rationale (S14 + O-Q2), per-tenant SA mapping, smoke-verify command (`gcloud run services describe kh-cocoindex-pipeline-{phew,kpf}`), pre-warm-vs-runtime-download trade-off.

### Files to edit
- `.github/workflows/cloud-run-deploy.yml`:
  - Add job step `Build cocoindex image via Cloud Build` (parallel to existing `Build images via Cloud Build`, using `--config=cloudrun/cloudbuild-cocoindex.yaml`).
  - Add step `Deploy cocoindex Services` (parallel to existing `Deploy tenant manifests`, using `gcloud run services replace cloudrun/services/${ENV_PREFIX}-${TENANT}-cocoindex.yaml`).
  - Reuse same WIF auth + deploy SA + Secret Manager mount step — extend secrets loop to cover both Job and Service deploys.
  - Path-trigger guard: add `cloudrun/services/**` to `paths:` allow-list.
- `requirements.txt`:
  - `cocoindex[postgres]==1.0.3` (validated by S1/S2/S14 spikes).
  - `docling>=2.0.0,<3.0.0` (cap minor-version range; pinned exact at first deploy per O-Q4 cold-start tolerance).
  - `litellm>=1.40.0,<2.0.0` (per O-Q5 ratified default; verify Anthropic + OpenAI passthrough works under WIF SA at first deploy).
  - `asyncpg>=0.30.0` (cocoindex transitive; explicit-pin for reproducibility).

### Sidecar version identification (Inv-8 specific wiring)
Cloud Build pipeline injects `IMAGE_SHA` (resolved git commit SHA at build time) as a Cloud Run Service env var; cocoindex sidecar reads `os.environ['IMAGE_SHA']` at boot and passes it through to every `recordPipelineRun()` call (via the webhook from 28.11) in the `result.extractor_version` field. Forensic correlation per Inv-8: query `pipeline_runs.result -> 'extractor_version'` for any successful run; cross-reference the SHA against the Cloud Run deploy log via `gcloud run services describe kh-cocoindex-pipeline-{tenant} --format='value(spec.template.spec.containers[0].env)'`.

### T-OQ3 ratified — 4 per-tenant Services
Per-tenant isolation at the Cloud Run boundary is a load-bearing security property (data-residency + multi-tenant compromise blast-radius). Collapsing to 2 Services (one per env) breaks that. Per-tenant Cloud Run Service cost is small; isolation is worth more.

### LMDB cold-start strategy (O-Q6 ratified)
LMDB ops-DB is ephemeral per container instance — no Cloud Storage persistent volume mount at v1. On cold-start, cocoindex re-fingerprints the source corpus (~7 s for 35-file v1 canonical scale per Spike #2 measurement). Re-evaluate if corpus crosses 10 k files per v1.1 guidance.

### CLAUDE.md gotchas
- Bun fetch hangs on HTTP 204 through sandbox proxy — does not affect deploy infra but is relevant for 28.14 integration tests.
- cocoindex requires `dangerouslyDisableSandbox: true` for pre-warm script execution.

### Acceptance test shape
- **Inv-6:** HTTPS health probe to `https://<service-url>/health` returns 200 OK from Vercel host within Cloud Run warm-start window.
- **Inv-9:** `docker image inspect europe-west2-docker.pkg.dev/<project>/pipeline/kh-cocoindex-pipeline:latest` returns zero pullmd binaries + zero Playwright deps. Post-build assertion step in `cloudbuild-cocoindex.yaml`.
- **Inv-10:** After Cloud Run Service scale-to-zero + cold-start cycle, first PDF ingest completes within 60 s end-to-end (verified by 28.14 integration test).

subtasks.title: Layered fn-shape per-MIME adapters (scripts/cocoindex_pipeline/adapters.py)
subtasks.description: Outer-tier @coco.fn(memo=True) convert_binary_to_markdown(file) + three inner-tier extractors: _docling_to_markdown(bytes), _pullmd_to_markdown(url), _passthrough_markdown(str). Inner-tier signatures take bytes/str — never FileLike — so memoisation key is content-hash.
subtasks.details:
## Dispatch brief — Subtask 28.7 (Layered fn-shape per-MIME adapters)

**Covers TECH P-change:** P-3.
**Covers PRODUCT inv:** Inv-4 (idempotency via content-hash memoisation).
**Sibling deps:** none — Wave A parallel node alongside 28.5 and 28.6.

### Goal
Author the **outer-tier** `@coco.fn(memo=True)` source-binding adapter `convert_binary_to_markdown(file: FileLike) -> str` AND the **inner-tier** per-MIME extractor `@coco.fn(memo=True)` functions consuming `content_text: str` per S9 §7.2 layered fn-shape (COCO.10).

### Why the layering matters
Inner-tier extractors take `bytes` (Docling) or `str` (pullmd URL, passthrough) so memoisation key is **content bytes, not file handles** — metadata-only edits (mtime, owner) do not re-trigger inner work. Outer-tier `FileLike` arg lets cocoindex select the inner-tier by MIME dispatch; memoisation at both tiers means a binary with unchanged content but rewrapped on disk is a memo-hit on the inner tier even if the outer tier's file-handle changed.

### Files to create
- `scripts/cocoindex_pipeline/adapters.py` — module with:
  - `convert_binary_to_markdown(file: FileLike) -> str` — outer-tier `@coco.fn(memo=True)`. Routes by MIME:
    - PDF/DOCX/XLSX → calls `_docling_to_markdown(content_bytes: bytes) -> str`
    - HTML → calls `_pullmd_to_markdown(url: str) -> str`
    - markdown → calls `_passthrough_markdown(content_text: str) -> str`
  - `_docling_to_markdown(content_bytes: bytes) -> str` — inner-tier `@coco.fn(memo=True)`. Imports `docling.document_converter.DocumentConverter` (pre-warmed in image layer per 28.6 P-1).
  - `_pullmd_to_markdown(url: str) -> str` — inner-tier `@coco.fn(memo=True)`. HTTP POST to pullmd Service URL (`PULLMD_SERVICE_URL` env var, mounted via Cloud Run Secret Manager per 28.6).
  - `_passthrough_markdown(content_text: str) -> str` — inner-tier `@coco.fn(memo=True)`. Identity transform; memoisation buys idempotency on content-hash match.

### Pattern reference (mirror this shape)
`scripts/ontology-sync/parse-flow.py` — canonical outer/inner split. Outer tier takes the file argument; inner tier takes the content payload. Inner-tier signatures MUST take `bytes` (Docling) or `str` (pullmd URL, passthrough markdown) — **NEVER** `FileLike` per S9 §7.2 (would break content-hash memoisation).

### CLAUDE.md gotchas
- cocoindex 1.0.3 requires `dangerouslyDisableSandbox: true` for both PyPI install and Rust-engine LMDB startup in dev.
- `localfs.walk_dir` defaults `recursive=False` — but this is consumed by 28.8 (flow.py), not here. Adapters operate on the file argument cocoindex passes in.

### Acceptance test shape (handed off to 28.14)
- **Inv-4** (memo-hit determinism): integration test ingests a file twice without modification; asserts `q_a_extractions` row count for that source is identical pre/post second run AND the second run's `pipeline_runs.items_created` is empty (memo-hit short-circuits all inner-tier work). Mirrors S2 spike Run B (warm-cache = 0 invocations).
- **Inv-5** (nested-corpus recursive support): covered by 28.8's flow.py acceptance test (`localfs.walk_dir(recursive=True)`) — the recursive adapter chain works through any depth.

### Function signature lock
The outer-tier signature `convert_binary_to_markdown(file: FileLike) -> str` is consumed by 28.8 (`flow.py` imports it). If 28.7 and 28.8 are dispatched in parallel Wave A, this signature MUST be locked before 28.8 starts Wave B — orchestrator gate.

<info added on 2026-05-21T12:00:00.000Z>
What shipped: Layered fn-shape per-MIME adapters for cocoindex 1.0.3. Created `scripts/cocoindex_pipeline/__init__.py` (package marker), `scripts/cocoindex_pipeline/adapters.py` (outer-tier `convert_binary_to_markdown(file: FileLike) -> str` dispatcher + three inner-tier converters), and `scripts/tests/test_cocoindex_adapters.py` (26 tests covering all MIME paths, memoisation, shape invariants, and the FileLike API deviation).
Commit: 21be6efe (full SHA: 21be6efeb8d9cfa6afe9bc1a9ef48900855c4c3f — verify with git log).
Spec slice: TECH.md T8 §7.2 (COCO.10 layered fn-shape + memoisation key invariant).
In-flight discoveries:
  - cocoindex 1.0.3 API deviation from brief: `FileLike` exposes `.file_path.path` (PurePath) + async `read()`/`read_text()`, NOT `.mime_type`/`.url`/`.read_bytes()` as assumed. MIME is derived from `.suffix`; HTML url derived from `str(file.file_path.path)`. Layered shape + memoisation key invariant fully preserved per S9 §7.2 (COCO.10).
  - Inner-tier signature lock confirmed for 28.8 handoff: `_docling_to_markdown(content_bytes: bytes)`, `_pullmd_to_markdown(url: str)`, `_passthrough_markdown(content_text: str)` — NONE accept FileLike directly.
  - All 26 tests pass under `python3 -m pytest scripts/tests/test_cocoindex_adapters.py`.
  - Status flipped pending → in_progress at first commit.
</info added on 2026-05-21T12:00:00.000Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: convert_binary_to_markdown signature matches FileLike->str; inner-tier functions take bytes/str (verified via type-annotation grep — never FileLike); memo-hit integration test (28.14) confirms second-ingest is no-op.

subtasks.title: Cocoindex flow.py scaffolding — 6-stage canonical pipeline
subtasks.description: New scripts/cocoindex_pipeline/{__init__.py, flow.py, __main__.py}. Six stages wired flow-scope per 02-data-flow.md §3.1 + Q-EX2 TECH §3.1 verifier B-3 (NOT @coco.fn-wrapped). KH_PIPELINE_APP = coco.AppConfig. localfs.walk_dir(live=True, recursive=True). mount_table_target(managed_by=ManagedBy.USER) for 3 tables.
subtasks.details:
## Dispatch brief — Subtask 28.8 (Cocoindex flow.py 6-stage scaffolding)

**Covers TECH P-change:** P-2.
**Covers PRODUCT inv:** Inv-1 (file-change detection), Inv-2 (end-to-end latency), Inv-3 (six-stage topology observable), Inv-5 (nested-corpus coverage).
**Sibling deps:** [5, 6, 7] — op_id columns must exist (28.5), Cloud Run Service env vars + secrets must be deployable (28.6), `convert_binary_to_markdown` adapter must exist for the flow.py import to resolve (28.7).

### Goal
Author the canonical 6-stage cocoindex flow per `02-data-flow.md` §3.1 + Q-EX2 TECH §3.1 flow-scope pattern. Module exports `app_main()` async function + `KH_PIPELINE_APP = coco.AppConfig(name='kh_pipeline', main_fn=app_main)`. Source-binding via `localfs.walk_dir(live=True, recursive=True)` per CLAUDE.md gotcha + RESEARCH §1.2. Six stages wired flow-scope (NOT `@coco.fn`-wrapped — per Q-EX2 TECH §3.1 verifier B-3 finding).

### Files to create
- `scripts/cocoindex_pipeline/__init__.py` — empty package marker.
- `scripts/cocoindex_pipeline/flow.py` — module per TECH §P-2 sketch (full Python shape is in TECH.md §P-2 — implement verbatim with these key elements):
  - Imports: `cocoindex as coco`, `localfs`, `ColumnDef`, `TableSchema`, `mount_table_target`, `ManagedBy`, `LlmSpec`, `LlmApiType`, `ExtractByLlm`, `LiteLLMEmbedder`, and the adapter from 28.7 (`convert_binary_to_markdown`).
  - Cross-link imports for Q-EX2 (consumed in 28.12, not here): `ClassificationExtraction`, `EntityMentionExtraction`, `QAFormExtraction` from `scripts/cocoindex_pipeline/extraction.py`; instruction prompts from `scripts/cocoindex_pipeline/prompts.py`. These modules are authored by T1.2 — ALREADY LANDED.
  - 3 TableSchema declarations: `CONTENT_ITEMS_SCHEMA`, `Q_A_EXTRACTIONS_SCHEMA`, `SOURCE_DOCUMENTS_SCHEMA`. **OMIT `content_text_hash` from CONTENT_ITEMS_SCHEMA** (GENERATED ALWAYS per CLAUDE.md gotcha). Include `op_id` ColumnDef on all 3.
  - `async def app_main():` body:
    - Read `COCOINDEX_SOURCE_PATH` env var. If unset/empty per O-Q8, log idle-mode message and return (T8 ships empty source-binding; T7 stages files post-T8 stable).
    - Create asyncpg pool via `_build_dsn()`.
    - Stage 1 source walk: `flow['source'] = localfs.walk_dir(source_path, live=True, recursive=True)`.
    - Stage 2 binary conversion: `flow['content_text'] = flow['source'].transform(convert_binary_to_markdown)`.
    - Stages 3 (LLM extraction) — placeholder transforms for `classification`, `q_a_form`, `entity_mentions`. **Real ExtractByLlm wiring lands in 28.12** — at 28.8 the calls can be stubbed or commented-out for the test path to pass module-load.
    - Stage 4 embedding: `flow['embedding'] = flow['content_text'].transform(LiteLLMEmbedder(model='openai/text-embedding-3-large'))`.
    - Stage 5 entity resolution: `flow['resolved_entities'] = flow['entity_mentions'].transform(coco.functions.entity_resolution())`.
    - Stage 6 Postgres UPSERT: 3 `mount_table_target(..., managed_by=ManagedBy.USER)` calls + 3 `flow.bind_target(...)` calls. **op_id stamping ADDED IN 28.9** — at 28.8 the bind_target calls land without the op_id kwarg.
  - `_build_dsn() -> str` helper — mirrors `spike/cocoindex_s1/probe_managed_by_user.py:build_dsn()`. Reads `SUPABASE_URL` + `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` from env (mounted via Cloud Run Secret Manager per 28.6).
  - Module-level constant `KH_PIPELINE_APP = coco.AppConfig(name='kh_pipeline', main_fn=app_main)`.
  - `if __name__ == '__main__':` guard: `asyncio.run(coco.start(KH_PIPELINE_APP))`.
- `scripts/cocoindex_pipeline/__main__.py` — module entrypoint so `python3 -m scripts.cocoindex_pipeline` boots the App (used by Cloud Run Service `GOOGLE_ENTRYPOINT` per 28.6 manifest).

### Latency budgets baked in (PRODUCT Inv-2 + P-OQ4 dual budget)
- 35-file canonical corpus end-to-end ≤ 120 s (S2 cold-cache baseline).
- Per-file p95 ≤ 30 s (protects against pathological inputs).
Both metrics observable via `pipeline_runs.completed_at - pipeline_runs.started_at` after 28.11 webhook lands.

### O-Q8 empty-source idle-mode behaviour
On Service boot, if `COCOINDEX_SOURCE_PATH` is unset OR points at a missing folder, log info and exit `app_main()` cleanly. The Service stays running (Cloud Run expects long-lived process); cocoindex's live-fs-watch arms once source-binding is set. T7 Phew first-ingest cutover sets `COCOINDEX_SOURCE_PATH` and restarts the Service post-T8-stable. **Do NOT raise** — that would crash the Service and trigger Cloud Run restart loop.

### CLAUDE.md gotchas
- `localfs.walk_dir(recursive=True)` — default is False; explicit True for nested corpora per CLAUDE.md cocoindex gotcha + RESEARCH §1.2.
- `content_items.content_text_hash` is GENERATED ALWAYS — omit from TableSchema.
- cocoindex 1.0.3 requires `dangerouslyDisableSandbox: true` for both PyPI install and Rust-engine LMDB startup in dev.
- Embedding vector serialisation: `JSON.stringify(embedding)` for Supabase RPC vector params; cocoindex's `LiteLLMEmbedder` handles this internally.

### Acceptance test shape (delegated to 28.14)
- **Inv-1:** `__tests__/integration/cocoindex/file-change-detection.integration.test.ts`
- **Inv-2:** `__tests__/integration/cocoindex/latency-budget.integration.test.ts`
- **Inv-3:** `__tests__/integration/cocoindex/stage-topology.integration.test.ts`
- **Inv-5:** `__tests__/integration/cocoindex/nested-corpus.integration.test.ts`
