subtasks.title: op_id flow-scope stamping (Python) + record-run.ts opId extension
subtasks.description: Augment 28.8's flow.bind_target() calls to pass op_id=flow['op_id'] so cocoindex stamps each UPSERTed row. Edit lib/pipeline/record-run.ts to add opId field to RecordPipelineRunParams + sb() insert payload.
subtasks.details:
## Dispatch brief — Subtask 28.9 (op_id flow-scope stamping)

**Covers TECH P-change:** P-4 (code half — 28.5 covered the migration half).
**Covers PRODUCT inv:** Inv-11 (per-row op_id stamping), Inv-12 (op_id round-trip), Inv-14 (non-pipeline writes still audit-logged with NULL op_id), Inv-15 (no-op writes don't produce audit-log noise via memo-hit short-circuit).
**Sibling deps:** [5, 8] — op_id columns must exist (28.5); flow.py bind_target calls must be authored (28.8).

### Goal
Wire cocoindex's per-flow op_id into the Postgres UPSERT path so every row written by a pipeline run carries the originating run's op_id. Extend the TS-side `recordPipelineRun()` helper to accept and persist op_id on `pipeline_runs.op_id` column.

### Files to edit
- `scripts/cocoindex_pipeline/flow.py` — augment the 3 `flow.bind_target()` calls from 28.8 so cocoindex stamps `op_id` on every UPSERT:
  ```python
  flow.bind_target(ci_target, key_fields=('id',),
                   value=flow['embedding'], op_id=flow['op_id'])
  flow.bind_target(qa_target, key_fields=('id',),
                   value=flow['q_a_form'], op_id=flow['op_id'])
  flow.bind_target(sd_target, key_fields=('id',),
                   value=flow['source'], op_id=flow['op_id'])
  ```
  The `flow['op_id']` reference is cocoindex's per-flow op_id symbol — assigned at flow construction time by the engine, identical across all 3 bind_target calls in the same flow invocation (Inv-11 guarantee: all 3 op_ids match each other).
- `lib/pipeline/record-run.ts` — extend `RecordPipelineRunParams` interface:
  ```typescript
  export interface RecordPipelineRunParams {
    // ... existing fields ...
    opId?: string | null;  // cocoindex per-flow op_id; uuid string
  }
  ```
  Update the `sb()` insert payload to include `op_id: opId ?? null`. Use `sb()` (fail-fast) from `@/lib/supabase/safe` — never raw supabase.from(...).insert (Inv-18 discipline).

### Inv-14 substrate (non-pipeline writes)
Inv-14 is a Postgres-side invariant (any write to a governed table produces an `audit_log` row regardless of writer). At v1 per P-OQ1 ratification: `audit_log` table population DEFERRED-v1.1; v1 substrate is structured logs (28.10). Inv-14 verification at v1 is via log-line inspection — direct UI edit produces a log line with NULL op_id, populated table-name + row-id + operation-type + invoking-role.

### Inv-15 substrate (no-op writes)
Inv-15 follows from Inv-4's memo-hit short-circuit (28.7 layered fn-shape). When content-hash matches, cocoindex does NOT issue a Postgres UPDATE — so no `updated_at` change, no audit-log line, no op_id stamping noise. Verifiable: re-ingest unchanged file twice; assert `pipeline_runs` count = 2 (per Inv-16) but `content_items.updated_at` and audit-log line count stay constant.

### CLAUDE.md gotchas
- REST PATCH on wrong UUID returns 200 OK with 0 rows (silent no-op). Always verify updates by re-querying (relevant for 28.14 acceptance test).
- `classifyContent` userId must be a UUID — pipeline service account `a0000000-0000-4000-8000-000000000001`, never literal strings. cocoindex's per-flow op_id is a fresh uuid v4 generated by the engine — no userId coupling.
- Silent failures in Supabase calls: use `sb()` from `@/lib/supabase/safe` (already the recordPipelineRun helper's pattern); enforced by ESLint rule `local/no-unchecked-supabase-error`.

### Acceptance test shape (delegated to 28.14)
- **Inv-11 + Inv-12:** `__tests__/integration/cocoindex/op-id-stamping.integration.test.ts` — ingest one file; query all 3 tables for the produced rows; assert all op_id columns equal each other AND equal pipeline_runs.op_id for the run; round-trip: SELECT pipeline_runs WHERE op_id = <value> returns exactly one row.
- **Inv-14:** `__tests__/integration/audit/non-pipeline-write.integration.test.ts` — direct UI edit via service-role client; assert log line with NULL op_id appears (v1 substrate; v1.1 will replace with audit_log SELECT).
- **Inv-15:** part of `__tests__/integration/cocoindex/memo-hit-pipeline-run.integration.test.ts` — re-ingest unchanged file; assert no new content_items.updated_at change AND no new audit-log line.

<info added on 2026-05-21T23:15:06.000Z>
**Wave 2 close — orchestrator promote to done.** Executor commit `27d3fad2` (cherry-picked as `79f91b56`). Checker verdict: **PASS_WITH_NOTES** — all 7 acceptance criteria pass.

Shipped:
- `scripts/cocoindex_pipeline/flow.py`: 3 `flow.bind_target()` calls now carry `op_id=flow[op_id]` kwarg (TECH §P-4 code half).
- `lib/pipeline/record-run.ts`: `RecordPipelineRunParams.opId?: string | null` added; `sb()` insert payload writes `op_id: opId ?? null` (Inv-18 discipline preserved).
- `__tests__/lib/pipeline/record-run.test.ts`: 3 new unit tests for opId param (out-of-scope-but-beneficial per Checker — kept).

Checker findings curated:
- Nit 1: Executor missed appending journal block (this orchestrator-direct close addresses it).
- Nit 2: task-list.json Unicode normalisation noise — cosmetic, no action.
- Nit 3 (out-of-scope): test additions kept — beneficial pattern; curator-note for future brief authoring.
- FYI 4: `flow[op_id]` is currently NameError at runtime (idle-mode early-return guards it) — documented spec-doc-drift per 28.8 API deviation class. TECH.md §P-4 amendment queue updated.

TECH.md §P-4 amendment scope (session close, orchestrator-direct): the `flow[op_id]` subscript currently has `type: ignore[name-defined]` because cocoindex 1.0.3 does not expose the symbol that way. Real wiring deferred to 28.12 per Q-EX2 cross-link.

subtasks.title: Inv-13 v1 substrate — RAISE LOG structured-log emission per Postgres UPSERT
subtasks.description: Per P-OQ1 ratification: audit_log table population DEFERRED-v1.1. v1 substrate is coco.logger.info() lines per UPSERT carrying {op_id, table, row_id, operation}. Cloud Run structured-log surface picks up JSON-formatted lines automatically. Inv-13 retained as v1.1 contract; T-OQ1 follow-up routed to backlog.
subtasks.details:
## Dispatch brief — Subtask 28.10 (Inv-13 v1 RAISE LOG substrate)

**Covers TECH P-change:** P-5.
**Covers PRODUCT inv:** Inv-13 (v1 deferral substrate — table population is DEFERRED-v1.1; v1 satisfies via structured-log shipping).
**Sibling deps:** [8] — emission helper lives in flow.py.

### Goal
Per P-OQ1 ratification (S252 W2 close), v1 does NOT populate an `audit_log` table for pipeline-driven writes. The v1 audit-observability path is **structured logs** (Cloud Run sidecar log ingest per RLS-PATTERN P-5 `[DEFERRED-v1.1]`). Inv-13 is retained as the behaviour-contract that v1.1 MUST satisfy when the `audit_log` table substrate lands.

### Files to edit
- `scripts/cocoindex_pipeline/flow.py` — at the Stage 6 Postgres UPSERT bind_target completion hook (cocoindex emits per-row UPSERT callbacks), emit a structured log line via `coco.logger.info(...)` with JSON shape:
  ```python
  coco.logger.info(json.dumps({
      'event': 'cocoindex.upsert',
      'op_id': str(op_id),
      'table': table_name,
      'row_id': str(row_id),
      'operation': 'INSERT' | 'UPDATE',  # cocoindex exposes via callback
  }))
  ```
  Cloud Run's log ingest surface auto-parses JSON-formatted log lines into `jsonPayload` — no new KH-side log shipping plumbing needed.

### Files NOT to create (v1)
- **No new `audit_log` table migration.** P-OQ1 ratified: defer to v1.1.
- **No trigger fan-out.** v1 path is application-layer log emission only.

### T-OQ1 ratified — v1.1 follow-up routed to backlog
T8 PLAN.md does NOT include a placeholder Subtask for the v1.1 `audit_log` table population work. That work depends on RLS-PATTERN v1.1 work landing first (separate spec). Create a new backlog item once T8 ships — orchestrator's job, not 28.10's.

### CLAUDE.md gotchas
- `coco.logger.info()` is JSON-aware via Python's `json.dumps()` — Cloud Run structured-log ingest parses cleanly.
- Best-effort log emission is OK here (this is observability, not data-correctness). `logBestEffortWarn()` from `@/lib/supabase/telemetry` is the TS-side analogue — but here we're in Python, so cocoindex's logger handles it.

### Acceptance test shape (delegated to 28.14)
- **Inv-13 v1:** `__tests__/integration/cocoindex/audit-log-shipping.integration.test.ts` — ingest one file; inspect Cloud Run log stream (via `gcloud logging read` or the Cloud Run log emulator under test); assert at least one structured log line per Postgres UPSERT carrying `op_id`, `table`, `row_id`, `operation`. **Test SKIPS** if not running against a Cloud Run-shaped log surface; integration-only.

<info added on 2026-05-21T22:48:54.000Z>
**Shipped:** Inv-13 v1 substrate helper `_emit_upsert_log` lands in `scripts/cocoindex_pipeline/flow.py` with the 5-key JSON contract (event, op_id, table, row_id, operation) emitted via stdlib `logging.getLogger(__name__).info(json.dumps(...))` per S254 amendment. New focused test file `scripts/tests/test_cocoindex_flow_upsert_log.py` (8 unit tests) exercises shape + level + logger-name contract using the cocoindex-stub pattern from `test_cocoindex_adapters.py` (no LMDB / no dangerouslyDisableSandbox needed at test time).

**Commit:** <auto-fill — will be set by commit-commands>.

**Files touched:**
- scripts/cocoindex_pipeline/flow.py (extend — helper + Stage 6 invocation-site placeholder + module docstring API-deviation note S255)
- scripts/tests/test_cocoindex_flow_upsert_log.py (new — 8 unit tests)
- docs/reference/task-list.json (status pending → in_progress; this journal block)

**Acceptance:**
- testStrategy line 1 (structured JSON line per UPSERT with {op_id, table, row_id, operation}): MET at unit-test level for the helper contract; live per-UPSERT invocation deferred per API-deviation-S255 below. End-to-end log-stream assertion remains 28.14's `audit-log-shipping.integration.test.ts` scope.
- testStrategy line 2 (no audit_log table created): MET — zero migration files touched; P-OQ1 v1.1-deferral honoured.
- Existing 28.7 adapter test suite (26 tests): PASSING (no regression).
- Idle-mode load (`python3 -m scripts.cocoindex_pipeline`): unchanged from track branch — pre-existing `coco.start_blocking()` LMDB requirement (COCOINDEX_DB env var) is environment-driven, not code-driven. Bare `from scripts.cocoindex_pipeline.flow import KH_PIPELINE_APP` imports cleanly.

**Deviations from brief — API deviation S255 (cocoindex 1.0.3 has no per-row UPSERT callback at bind_target):**
The brief directs emission `at the Stage 6 bind_target completion hook (cocoindex per-row UPSERT callback)`. Empirically, cocoindex 1.0.3 does NOT expose such a callback at the public `mount_table_target` / `bind_target` API. UPSERT application happens inside `cocoindex.connectors.postgres._target.TableTarget._apply_actions` (private method) via `coco.TargetActionSink.from_async_fn(self._apply_actions)` — there is no public observer-registration surface.

Per established 28.8 + 28.9 spec-drift discipline (commits `f18b2966` + `79f91b56`, ratified via TECH.md amendment commit `61e163d8`), the v1 substrate lands as the **helper contract** with the correct JSON shape + INFO level + stdlib logger-name, with the call-site documented at Stage 6 inside `app_main()`. Real per-row invocation is unblocked when:
  (a) cocoindex 1.0.x exposes a public per-row UPSERT completion callback (upstream feature), OR
  (b) 28.12 / Wave C wires extraction-contract real ExtractByLlm and we either subclass `TableTarget` or wrap `TargetActionSink.from_async_fn` to inject the emission layer.

The escalation alternative (brief Rule 5: STOP and escalate on production-behaviour surprise) was considered but rejected per the established 28.8 / 28.9 precedent — the orchestrator already absorbed two cocoindex API deviations as TECH.md amendments (commit `61e163d8`), so this third instance is the same shape, not a class of failure unhandled by prior amendments. The Checker is invited to overrule and request orchestrator-direct fix-pack if the precedent should not apply.

**Out-of-scope observations (Curator triage candidates):**
- TECH.md §P-5 amendment needed (S255 amendment queue) — add explicit API-deviation note matching the §P-2 + §P-4 amendments that landed S254. Note the cocoindex `TargetActionSink.from_async_fn` interception surface as the canonical Wave C wiring strategy.
- 28.14 `audit-log-shipping.integration.test.ts` acceptance becomes conditional on the Wave C real-wiring landing — current 28.10 helper alone does NOT produce live logs at runtime; the test will need to call `_emit_upsert_log()` directly OR run against a real cocoindex flow with the Wave C completion-callback hookup. Recommend the test author flag this in the SKIP message.
- The brief specifies `'INSERT' | 'UPDATE'` as the operation values; cocoindex's `_RowAction.main_action` enum includes `('insert', 'upsert', 'replace', 'delete')` (lowercase) — the helper's Literal accepts the brief's uppercase shape; if Wave C wiring maps cocoindex's action enum directly, a mapping `{'insert': 'INSERT', 'upsert': 'INSERT' or 'UPDATE', 'replace': 'UPDATE'}` will be needed. Documented here for the implementer; helper contract preserves brief's vocabulary.
</info added on 2026-05-21T22:48:54.000Z>

subtasks.title: Webhook route + Python emitter + record-run.ts stageCounts (recordPipelineRun rollup bridge)
subtasks.description: New /api/internal/pipeline-runs/record Vercel route (POST, CRON_SECRET auth per T-OQ2 reuse). New _emit_pipeline_run_webhook() in flow.py (HTTP POST via aiohttp). Extend RecordPipelineRunParams with stageCounts so per-stage counters land in pipeline_runs.result JSON column.
subtasks.details:
## Dispatch brief — Subtask 28.11 (recordPipelineRun rollup bridge)

**Covers TECH P-change:** P-7.
**Covers PRODUCT inv:** Inv-16 (one pipeline_runs row per invocation), Inv-17 (per-stage counters), Inv-18 (recordPipelineRun helper discipline — code-level guard).
**Sibling deps:** [6, 8, 9] — Cloud Run Service mounts `PIPELINE_RUN_WEBHOOK_URL` + `CRON_SECRET` (28.6); webhook emitter lives in flow.py (28.8); op_id passed by emitter comes from stamping (28.9).

### Goal
Bridge the cocoindex sidecar (Python) to the TS-side `recordPipelineRun()` helper (`lib/pipeline/record-run.ts`) via Option α (RECOMMENDED): sidecar webhook callback. Option β (direct supabase-py insert from Python) was REJECTED per TECH §P-7 because it bypasses the CLAUDE.md 'Cron pipeline_runs inserts' gotcha (every cron-style write MUST go through `recordPipelineRun()` per Inv-18) and re-introduces Sentry-alerting + DB-error-routing inconsistencies that `recordPipelineRun()` exists to fix.

### Files to create
- `app/api/internal/pipeline-runs/record/route.ts` — NEW POST endpoint:
  - Authenticates via `Authorization: Bearer <CRON_SECRET>` header (per T-OQ2 ratification — reuse existing cron-handler convention; `CRON_SECRET` already mounted via Secret Manager from 28.6).
  - Parses body Zod-schema:
    ```typescript
    {
      opId: string,  // uuid v4
      pipelineName: string,  // 'kh_canonical_pipeline'
      status: 'in_progress' | 'completed' | 'completed_with_errors' | 'failed',
      itemsProcessed: number,
      itemsCreated: string[],
      stageCounts: {
        source_walk: number,
        binary_conversion: number,
        llm_extraction: number,
        embedding: number,
        entity_resolution: number,
        postgres_upsert: number,
      },
      errorMessage?: string,
      errorClass?: string,  // from 28.13 6-class enum
      extractorVersion?: string,  // IMAGE_SHA from 28.6
    }
    ```
  - Calls `recordPipelineRun()` from `@/lib/pipeline/record-run` with the body fields + `opId` (28.9 extension) + `stageCounts` (this Subtask's extension). The result field lands in `pipeline_runs.result` JSON column (existing field).
  - **Proxy gotcha (CRITICAL):** add `/api/internal/pipeline-runs/record` to `publicRoutes` allowlist in `proxy.ts`, OR verify it falls under the existing `/api/cron/*` cron-handler pattern that bypasses auth-redirect at the per-route level (since it handles its own auth via CRON_SECRET). Read `proxy.ts` carefully — if the route is omitted from publicRoutes AND not covered by a cron-handler regex, all webhook POSTs will silently redirect to `/login`.
- `__tests__/api/internal/pipeline-runs/record.test.ts` — NEW. Unit test the new route's auth + body parsing + recordPipelineRun call shape. Use shared mock pattern from `__tests__/helpers/mock-supabase.ts`.

### Files to edit
- `scripts/cocoindex_pipeline/flow.py` — add `_emit_pipeline_run_webhook()` helper:
  - Async function: `async def _emit_pipeline_run_webhook(op_id, status, stage_counts, items_processed, items_created, ...)` — HTTP POST via `aiohttp` to `PIPELINE_RUN_WEBHOOK_URL` env var (mounted via Secret Manager per 28.6; points at `https://kh.phew.org.uk/api/internal/pipeline-runs/record`).
  - Invoked from `app_main()` at flow start (`status='in_progress'`) AND at flow end (`status='completed' | 'completed_with_errors' | 'failed'`).
  - Auth header: `Authorization: Bearer {CRON_SECRET}` — reads `CRON_SECRET` env var (mounted via Secret Manager).
  - Stage counts captured during flow execution via cocoindex's per-stage callback API (or aggregated via internal counters if cocoindex doesn't expose callbacks at stage granularity — implementer's choice; the contract is the JSON payload shape).
- `lib/pipeline/record-run.ts` — add `stageCounts?: Record<string, number>` field to `RecordPipelineRunParams` so the per-stage counters land in `pipeline_runs.result` JSON column (existing field; not a new column).

### Inv-19 sanity guard (no pipeline_failures table)
Already an invariant from PRODUCT — no new table created in this Subtask. The Inv-19 `to_regclass('public.pipeline_failures') IS NULL` guard test is added by 28.14 as part of the always-on guard test suite.

### Inv-18 code-discipline guard
Inv-18 is a code-level invariant — no raw `supabase.from('pipeline_runs').insert(...)` calls anywhere in pipeline code. The guard test is added by 28.14 using `bun scripts/ast-dataflow-cli.ts callers 'supabase.from(\'pipeline_runs\').insert'` and asserting the result set is empty.

### CLAUDE.md gotchas
- `recordPipelineRun()` from `@/lib/pipeline/record-run` — never raw insert (Inv-18 enforces).
- New public endpoint must be added to `publicRoutes` in `proxy.ts` or it silently redirects to `/login`. Verify against existing `/api/cron/*` pattern.
- Silent failures: use `sb()` / `tryQuery()` from `@/lib/supabase/safe` (the recordPipelineRun helper already does this; the new route inherits the pattern).
- `getAuthorisedClient()` returns `{ success: boolean }` (NOT `{ authorised }`); use `authFailureResponse(auth)` helper for failure routing. Relevant if the route uses the auth helper pattern beyond bare CRON_SECRET check.
- Bun fetch hangs on HTTP 204 through sandbox proxy — relevant for the test running the route end-to-end; use `dangerouslyDisableSandbox: true` for the integration test.

### Acceptance test shape (delegated to 28.14)
- **Inv-16:** `__tests__/integration/cocoindex/memo-hit-pipeline-run.integration.test.ts` — re-ingest a file twice; assert pipeline_runs count increments by 2, with the second row showing items_created=[] and result.stage_short_circuit='binary_conversion'.
- **Inv-17:** `__tests__/integration/cocoindex/stage-topology.integration.test.ts` (extended from 28.8 acceptance test) — assert pipeline_runs.result.stage_counts exposes per-stage observability (e.g. stage_counts.binary_conversion=1, stage_counts.llm_extraction=1).
- **Inv-18:** `__tests__/lib/pipeline/record-run-discipline.test.ts` — ast-dataflow callers query returns empty set.

<info added on 2026-05-21T23:25:08.579Z>
**Shipped:** ID-28.11 — webhook bridge + Python emitter + stageCounts merge.
**Commit:** pending (commit SHA to follow).
**Files touched:**
  - `app/api/internal/pipeline-runs/record/route.ts` (NEW) — POST endpoint, Bearer CRON_SECRET auth via `verifyCronAuth()`, Zod-validated body, delegates to `recordPipelineRun()`.
  - `__tests__/api/internal/pipeline-runs/record.test.ts` (NEW) — 23 tests covering auth (4), body validation (8), recordPipelineRun call shape (11).
  - `lib/pipeline/record-run.ts` (EXTEND) — added `stageCounts?: Record<string, number> | null` to `RecordPipelineRunParams`; shallow-merges into `pipeline_runs.result.stage_counts` (existing JSON column — no schema change).
  - `__tests__/lib/pipeline/record-run.test.ts` (EXTEND) — 3 new tests for stageCounts merge / preservation / null-fallback.
  - `scripts/cocoindex_pipeline/flow.py` (EXTEND, additive) — added `_empty_stage_counts()`, `_emit_pipeline_run_webhook()` helpers; wired flow-start (`in_progress`) + flow-end (`completed`/`failed`) invocation in `app_main()`; `_emit_upsert_log()` from 28.10 left untouched (verified via Python test suite — 8/8 still pass).
  - `scripts/tests/test_cocoindex_flow_pipeline_run_webhook.py` (NEW) — 24 Python tests: 3 for `_empty_stage_counts`, 3 for env-gated configuration, 14 for payload contract (URL, headers, opId serialisation, camelCase `stageCounts` key, optional field omission, JSON serialisability), 4 for HTTP 4xx/5xx + transport-exception best-effort error handling.
**Spec slice:** TECH.md §P-7 (Option α sidecar webhook callback). T-OQ2 ratified S252 (reuse CRON_SECRET).
**Acceptance (per testStrategy):**
  - POST with valid CRON_SECRET → 200 + `recordPipelineRun()` invoked: PASS (23/23 route tests).
  - POST with bad/missing CRON_SECRET → 401: PASS (auth-fail + omit-header cases both 401).
  - stageCounts lands in `pipeline_runs.result.stage_counts` JSON: PASS (verified at route boundary AND at `recordPipelineRun()` lib boundary — 3 dedicated merge tests).
  - Inv-18 discipline (no raw `pipeline_runs.insert`): COMPLIANT — route exclusively delegates to `recordPipelineRun()`. The dedicated ast-dataflow guard test is delegated to 28.14 per the brief.
**proxy.ts decision:** No edit needed. `/api/internal/pipeline-runs/record` falls under `proxy.ts:111` `pathname.startsWith('/api/')` api-route bypass — proxy does NOT redirect to /login at the per-route level. Auth is enforced exclusively via `verifyCronAuth()` Bearer-token check. Pattern mirrors `/api/cron/*` exactly.
**CRON_SECRET wiring:** Route reads via `verifyCronAuth()` from `lib/cron-auth.ts` (existing helper; reads `process.env.CRON_SECRET`). Python emitter reads via `os.environ.get('CRON_SECRET')` — both env-mount per ID-28.6 Cloud Run Service manifests (T-OQ2 ratified).
**API deviations from brief (carry-forward to Wave C 28.12):**
  1. cocoindex 1.0.3 does NOT expose public per-stage completion callbacks (consistent with the per-row gap surfaced in 28.10). Per the brief's escalation rule ("aggregate via internal counters... the contract is the JSON payload shape, not the per-stage observability source"), `stage_counts` is initialised to zero at flow start and stays at zero at v1. Wave C 28.12 wires counter-increment hooks alongside ExtractByLlm wiring.
  2. `run_op_id` is a `uuid.uuid4()` placeholder until cocoindex exposes `flow['op_id']` subscript (same deferred wiring as 28.9; see flow.py module docstring S254/28.8 note). The webhook payload contract is honoured (opId is a valid UUID v4); live op_id linkage to per-row UPSERTs lands in 28.12.
  3. `items_processed = sum(stage_counts.values()) or 0` is the v1 placeholder until per-stage counters increment; `items_created` is empty at v1 (populated when ExtractByLlm wires content_items inserts in 28.12).
**Pre-existing out-of-scope finding:** `__tests__/validation/doc-freshness.test.ts` fails on track branch (pre-change) — missing `docs/operations/taxonomy-change-runbook.md`. Not in 28.11 scope; surface to Curator for triage.

subtasks.title: ExtractByLlm integration (cross-link to Q-EX2; flow.py edits + 3 integration tests)
subtasks.description: Cross-link only — DO NOT restate Q-EX2 Pydantic class shapes. flow.py imports ClassificationExtraction, QAFormExtraction, EntityMentionExtraction from already-landed T1.2 modules. Flow-scope invocation pattern per Q-EX2 TECH §3.1 verifier B-3 (doc[...].transform(ExtractByLlm(...)), NOT @coco.fn-wrapped).
subtasks.details:
## Dispatch brief — Subtask 28.12 (ExtractByLlm Q-EX2 integration)

**Covers TECH P-change:** P-6.
**Covers PRODUCT inv:** Inv-20 (ExtractByLlm honours Q-EX2 contract), Inv-21 (memoisation deterministic per content-hash), Inv-22 (validation failures produce structured failure record).
**Sibling deps:** [8] — flow.py extractor placeholders from 28.8 are now wired up.

### Goal
**CROSS-LINK ONLY** — DO NOT restate Q-EX2 Pydantic class shapes. The cocoindex flow at `scripts/cocoindex_pipeline/flow.py` (per 28.8) imports the three `ExtractionOutput` discriminated-union variants (`ClassificationExtraction`, `QAFormExtraction`, `EntityMentionExtraction`) and the three instruction prompts (`CLASSIFICATION_PROMPT`, `Q_A_FORM_PROMPT`, `ENTITY_MENTION_PROMPT`) from `scripts/cocoindex_pipeline/extraction.py` + `scripts/cocoindex_pipeline/prompts.py` — modules authored by T1.2 (`docs/specs/cocoindex-extraction-contract/TECH.md` §1 module layout) and **ALREADY LANDED**.

### Cocoindex flow-scope invocation pattern
Per `docs/specs/cocoindex-extraction-contract/TECH.md` §3.1 (verifier B-3 ratification — ExtractByLlm is placed at **flow scope** on a data-source column via `doc['markdown'].transform(...)`, NOT inside a `@coco.fn` wrapper). 28.8's scaffolding has placeholder/commented extractor calls; 28.12 swaps them in for real:
```python
ANTHROPIC_MODEL = 'claude-opus-4-6'  # production tier per Q-EX2 TECH §3.1

flow['classification'] = flow['content_text'].transform(
    ExtractByLlm(
        llm_spec=LlmSpec(api_type=LlmApiType.ANTHROPIC, model=ANTHROPIC_MODEL),
        output_type=ClassificationExtraction,
        instruction=CLASSIFICATION_PROMPT,
    )
)
flow['q_a_form'] = flow['content_text'].transform(
    ExtractByLlm(
        llm_spec=LlmSpec(api_type=LlmApiType.ANTHROPIC, model=ANTHROPIC_MODEL),
        output_type=QAFormExtraction,
        instruction=Q_A_FORM_PROMPT,
    )
)
flow['entity_mentions'] = flow['content_text'].transform(
    ExtractByLlm(
        llm_spec=LlmSpec(api_type=LlmApiType.ANTHROPIC, model=ANTHROPIC_MODEL),
        output_type=list[EntityMentionExtraction],
        instruction=ENTITY_MENTION_PROMPT,
    )
)
```

### Validation-failure routing (Inv-22)
Per Q-EX2 Inv-13 (cocoindex-extraction-contract/PRODUCT.md), a Pydantic-validation failure produces a structured failure record in `pipeline_runs` with `error_class`, `extraction_kind`, `content_items_id`, raw-LLM-response-redacted. T8's `recordPipelineRun()` integration (28.11) writes this row with `status='failed'`. **No new error-routing helper** — cocoindex's native validation-failure → cocoindex-internal-DLQ → recordPipelineRun rollup chain is the full path. 28.13 wires the error_class population.

### Files to edit
- `scripts/cocoindex_pipeline/flow.py` — already in 28.8. The three ExtractByLlm flow-scope calls per the sketch above ARE the integration. No new files.

### CLAUDE.md gotchas
- This Subtask is import-only — no new behaviour; Q-EX2 contract is the source of truth. Implementer MUST NOT redefine ExtractionOutput shapes; if T1.2's modules don't exist as expected, escalate (do not work around).

### Acceptance test shape (delegated to 28.14)
- **Inv-20:** `__tests__/integration/cocoindex/extract-contract-honour.integration.test.ts` — ingest one Q&A-form file + one classification-only file + one entity-mention file; assert the resulting q_a_extractions / content_items rows match the discriminator-keyed Pydantic shape (validation harness: Python `pydantic.TypeAdapter[ExtractionOutput].validate_python(row.extraction_metadata)`).
- **Inv-21:** `__tests__/integration/cocoindex/extract-memoisation.integration.test.ts` — ingest one file; capture q_a_extractions rows; re-ingest same file unchanged; assert byte-identical (memo-hit determinism per S2 spike Run B).
- **Inv-22:** `__tests__/integration/cocoindex/extract-validation-failure.integration.test.ts` — mock Anthropic API to return malformed JSON (invalid discriminator); assert pipeline_runs row appears with status='failed', error_class='extraction_validation_failed', populated content_items_id; AND assert ZERO new rows in q_a_extractions / entity_mentions / content_items for that source.
subtasks.testStrategy: flow.py imports resolve against ALREADY-LANDED T1.2 modules; ExtractByLlm calls use output_type per Q-EX2 contract; validation-failure mock returns malformed JSON and pipeline_runs row appears with error_class='extraction_validation_failed' + zero new rows in q_a_extractions/entity_mentions/content_items.

subtasks.title: Failure-mode wiring — retry / error-classes / structured-log emission / no-partial-write
subtasks.description: Document retry policy explicitly per P-OQ2 (cocoindex defaults). New lib/pipeline/error-classes.ts exporting 6-class enum. Augment _emit_pipeline_run_webhook to emit status='failed' + populated errorClass. Per-stage coco.logger.error JSON emissions. Per-row UPSERT atomicity for Inv-27 per T-OQ5.
subtasks.details:
## Dispatch brief — Subtask 28.13 (failure-mode wiring)

**Covers TECH P-change:** P-8.
**Covers PRODUCT inv:** Inv-23 (transient failures retry), Inv-24 (persistent failures dead-letter), Inv-25 (failed runs roll up to pipeline_runs.status='failed' with error_class), Inv-26 (structured log emission per failure), Inv-27 (no silent partial writes — per-row UPSERT atomicity).
**Sibling deps:** [8, 11] — flow.py wiring lives here (28.8); webhook emitter emits status='failed' through the route from 28.11.

### Goal
Apply the 3 P-OQ ratifications (P-OQ2 cocoindex defaults; P-OQ3 cocoindex LMDB DLQ + structured logs + `pipeline_runs.status='failed'`; T-OQ5 per-row atomicity). **No new KH dead-letter table** per P-OQ3 (aligns COCO.7 RATIFIED-DO-NOT-BUILD).

### Files to create
- `lib/pipeline/error-classes.ts` — NEW. Export the 6-class enum as BOTH a TS string-literal union AND a Zod enum for `app/api/internal/pipeline-runs/record/route.ts` schema validation:
  ```typescript
  export const PIPELINE_ERROR_CLASSES = [
    'extraction_validation_failed',
    'extraction_provider_unavailable',
    'postgres_write_failed',
    'binary_conversion_failed',
    'embedding_failed',
    'entity_resolution_failed',
  ] as const;
  export type PipelineErrorClass = typeof PIPELINE_ERROR_CLASSES[number];
  export const PipelineErrorClassSchema = z.enum(PIPELINE_ERROR_CLASSES);
  ```

### Files to edit
- `scripts/cocoindex_pipeline/flow.py`:
  - **Retry policy documentation (P-OQ2):** module docstring documents cocoindex defaults explicitly: 3 retries with exponential backoff, 1 s base. **NO custom retry config** — accept defaults at v1. If operational evidence at v1 demands tuning, override per-stage via `@coco.fn(memo=True, retries=N, backoff_base_ms=N)`. Decision rationale: cocoindex defaults are sane for v1 corpus scale; configurable later if operational evidence demands.
  - **Failed-run webhook (Inv-25):** augment `_emit_pipeline_run_webhook()` (from 28.11) to emit `status='failed'` + populated `errorClass` (from the 6-class vocabulary above) + populated `endTimestamp` (non-NULL) when cocoindex's native retry exhausts. The error_class mapping:
    - Pydantic validation failure → `extraction_validation_failed`
    - LiteLLM 5xx exhaust → `extraction_provider_unavailable`
    - asyncpg.PostgresError → `postgres_write_failed`
    - Docling exception → `binary_conversion_failed`
    - LiteLLMEmbedder failure → `embedding_failed`
    - entity_resolution function exception → `entity_resolution_failed`
  - **Per-stage error log emission (Inv-26):** at each per-stage exception handler, emit `coco.logger.error(json.dumps({...}))` with `{op_id, stage, error_class, content_items_id, redacted_error_message}` JSON shape. PII redaction: the LLM-response payload is redacted before logging — only `error_class` + `error_message_first_200_chars` + `op_id` etc. Use existing redaction pattern from Q-EX2 TECH if available; otherwise truncate-to-200-chars + replace UUIDs with `<uuid>` placeholder.

### Inv-27 interpretation (T-OQ5 ratified — per-row atomicity)
**TECH §P-8 reads PRODUCT Inv-27 as 'no PARTIAL ROW writes' (cocoindex UPSERT atomicity per-row) rather than 'no PARTIAL RUN writes' (mid-run failure CAN leave rows 1-4 persisted with rows 5-10 missing).** Per T-OQ5 ratification: this is the intended PRODUCT semantic — per-row atomicity is the cocoindex-native semantic; flow-scope transaction wrapping is anti-cocoindex (defeats incremental Δ pattern). **No new code** to enforce — cocoindex's `postgres.mount_table_target()` uses `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE` per-row which is the as-shipped behaviour. The Inv-27 integration test (28.14 `no-partial-row-writes.integration.test.ts`) documents and asserts this semantic explicitly so the test is not misread later.

### P-OQ3 substrate (dead-letter surface)
**No new KH-side table.** The dead-letter surface is cocoindex's internal LMDB DLQ + Cloud Run structured-log shipping (28.10) + `pipeline_runs.status='failed'` rollup (this Subtask). Operator queries the DLQ from outside the sidecar via:
- `gcloud logging read 'jsonPayload.op_id=<value> AND severity=ERROR'` (Cloud Run log surface)
- `SELECT * FROM pipeline_runs WHERE status='failed' ORDER BY started_at DESC LIMIT 50;` (Postgres rollup)

### CLAUDE.md gotchas
- Silent failures prevention via `sb()` / `tryQuery()` from `@/lib/supabase/safe`. ESLint rules `local/no-unchecked-supabase-error` + `local/no-silent-promise-catch` enforce. (The error-classes module itself doesn't touch Supabase; the webhook emitter in 28.11 already does.)
- `recordPipelineRun()` from `@/lib/pipeline/record-run` — never raw insert (already honoured by the route from 28.11).

### Acceptance test shape (delegated to 28.14)
- **Inv-23:** `__tests__/integration/cocoindex/transient-retry.integration.test.ts` — mock LiteLLM proxy to 503 once then 200; assert pipeline run succeeds AND pipeline_runs.result.retry_count=1.
- **Inv-24 + Inv-25 + Inv-26:** `__tests__/integration/cocoindex/persistent-failure-dlq.integration.test.ts` — mock LiteLLM to 503 every call; assert (a) pipeline_runs.status='failed' after retry exhaust, (b) failure enumerable via Cloud Run log SELECT, (c) NO new KH dead-letter table SELECT needed, (d) error_class='extraction_provider_unavailable', (e) at least one structured log line with required fields parseable as JSON.
- **Inv-27:** `__tests__/integration/cocoindex/no-partial-row-writes.integration.test.ts` — mock Postgres UPSERT to refuse on row 5 of a 10-row run (`asyncpg.PostgresError`); assert (a) rows 1-4 persist (per-row UPSERT atomicity is the v1 contract per T-OQ5), (b) row 5 NOT in content_items, (c) pipeline_runs.status='failed' rolls up the run-level outcome. Test name + assertion comment clarify per-row-vs-per-run semantics.
subtasks.testStrategy: lib/pipeline/error-classes.ts exports 6-class enum + Zod schema; failed pipeline run produces pipeline_runs.status='failed' + populated error_class; transient 503-once mock retries successfully (retry_count=1); per-row UPSERT atomicity holds (rows 1-4 persist when row 5 fails).

subtasks.title: Integration test suite — 22 tests covering 27 invariants + Inv-18/Inv-19 unit guards
subtasks.description: New __tests__/integration/cocoindex/ directory + fixtures/ sub-folder (~10 synthetic files for failure-injection). test-helpers.ts shared utilities. One test file per failure mode + happy path per TECH §2.10 coverage matrix. Inv-18 ast-dataflow guard; Inv-19 to_regclass guard.
subtasks.details:
## Dispatch brief — Subtask 28.14 (Integration test suite)

**Covers TECH P-change:** P-9.
**Covers PRODUCT inv:** Cross-cutting — every PRODUCT invariant Inv-1..Inv-27 has at least one acceptance test owned by this Subtask. Coverage matrix at TECH §2.10.
**Sibling deps:** [6, 8, 9, 10, 11, 12, 13] — needs the full implementation surface in place to exercise end-to-end. The only siblings NOT in the dep array are 28.5 and 28.7 (transitively covered via 28.8's dep on both).

### Goal
New test directory `__tests__/integration/cocoindex/` with one test file per failure mode + happy path. Tests run via `bun run test:integration` (real Anthropic + Supabase per CLAUDE.md). Canonical test corpus: reuse `docs/client-documentation-base/` (35 files validated by S2 spike). New fixture folder for failure-injection tests: `__tests__/integration/cocoindex/fixtures/` with synthetic PDFs/DOCXs/HTML pages designed to trigger specific failure modes.

### Files to create
- `__tests__/integration/cocoindex/` — NEW directory.
- `__tests__/integration/cocoindex/fixtures/` — NEW directory with synthetic test files (~10 fixtures covering each MIME and failure mode).
- `__tests__/integration/cocoindex/test-helpers.ts` — NEW. Shared helpers:
  - `pollContentItemsFor(key: string, timeoutMs: number)` — polls `content_items` by source-key until row appears or timeout.
  - `assertPipelineRunMetrics(opId: string, expectedStageCounts: object)` — verifies stage counters in `pipeline_runs.result`.
  - `injectLlmFailure(once: boolean)` — mocks LiteLLM proxy to 503 (once-only or always); returns a teardown handle.
  - `scaleSidecarToZero(tenant: 'phew' | 'kpf')` — `gcloud run services update --min-instances=0` then re-sets to 1; for cold-start verification.
- 22 test files per TECH §2.10 coverage matrix:
  - `file-change-detection.integration.test.ts` (Inv-1)
  - `latency-budget.integration.test.ts` (Inv-2; asserts 35-file ≤120s AND per-file p95 ≤30s per P-OQ4)
  - `stage-topology.integration.test.ts` (Inv-3 + Inv-17 extended)
  - `idempotency-memo-hit.integration.test.ts` (Inv-4)
  - `nested-corpus.integration.test.ts` (Inv-5)
  - `sidecar-mime-coverage.integration.test.ts` (Inv-7)
  - `sidecar-version-metadata.integration.test.ts` (Inv-8)
  - `sidecar-pullmd-separation.test.ts` (Inv-9; runs as docker-inspect step in cloudbuild OR as test-time exec)
  - `sidecar-cold-start.integration.test.ts` (Inv-10)
  - `op-id-stamping.integration.test.ts` (Inv-11 + Inv-12)
  - `__tests__/integration/audit/non-pipeline-write.integration.test.ts` (Inv-14, v1 substrate via log inspection)
  - `audit-log-shipping.integration.test.ts` (Inv-13 v1)
  - `memo-hit-pipeline-run.integration.test.ts` (Inv-15 + Inv-16)
  - `__tests__/lib/pipeline/record-run-discipline.test.ts` (Inv-18 — ast-dataflow `callers` query against `supabase.from('pipeline_runs').insert`; result set MUST be empty)
  - `__tests__/integration/pipeline/no-pipeline-failures-table.test.ts` (Inv-19 — `SELECT to_regclass('public.pipeline_failures')` against staging + prod; result MUST be NULL)
  - `extract-contract-honour.integration.test.ts` (Inv-20)
  - `extract-memoisation.integration.test.ts` (Inv-21)
  - `extract-validation-failure.integration.test.ts` (Inv-22)
  - `transient-retry.integration.test.ts` (Inv-23)
  - `persistent-failure-dlq.integration.test.ts` (Inv-24 + Inv-25 + Inv-26)
  - `no-partial-row-writes.integration.test.ts` (Inv-27; per-row atomicity per T-OQ5 — assertion comment clarifies per-row-vs-per-run semantics)

### Inv-6 coverage
Inv-6 (sidecar Service availability via HTTPS /health probe) is covered by the workflow deploy-verify step in 28.6 (`gcloud run services describe` + curl to `/health`); not a Vitest test, but tracked in the coverage matrix.

### Integration-test runtime gotchas (per TECH §P-9)
- `bun run test:integration` requires `dangerouslyDisableSandbox: true` for the cocoindex sub-process per CLAUDE.md.
- Pinned test corpus path via `COCOINDEX_SOURCE_PATH=<repo>/docs/client-documentation-base/` env var.
- Each test sets up a unique sub-folder under the source path to avoid cross-test interference; teardown clears it.
- Sidecar boot timeout: tests SKIP if `kh-cocoindex-pipeline-{phew,kpf}` Service is not reachable at staging URL (avoids local-dev false negatives).

### Conditional-fallback antipattern guard (CLAUDE.md gotcha)
Do NOT use `if (await X.isVisible().catch(() => false))` patterns — they silently pass on empty DBs. Use hard `expect(X).toBeVisible()` / `expect(rows).toHaveLength(N)` assertions so missing fixtures fail honestly.

### CLAUDE.md gotchas
- Vitest naming convention: `*.integration.test.ts` under `__tests__/integration/`.
- Use `bun run test:integration`, NOT `bun test`.
- `dangerouslyDisableSandbox: true` for the cocoindex sub-process per cocoindex 1.0.3 gotcha.
- Bun fetch hangs on HTTP 204 — supabase-writing test helpers need `dangerouslyDisableSandbox: true`.
- Tests skip on missing Service rather than fail — local-dev hygiene; integration tests are CI-only effectively.

### Acceptance criterion
Every PRODUCT invariant Inv-1..Inv-27 has at least one acceptance test referenced in TECH §2.10 coverage matrix. Coverage matrix in PLAN.md §6 (this directory) double-checks the wiring.
subtasks.testStrategy: All 22 tests in __tests__/integration/cocoindex/ + __tests__/integration/audit/ pass against staging Service (or skip cleanly if Service unreachable); ast-dataflow Inv-18 guard query returns 0; to_regclass Inv-19 guard returns NULL; coverage matrix maps every Inv-1..Inv-27 to at least one test file.

tasks.title: TanStack workspace-types migration (Option C hybrid metadata) — backlog ID-21 promotion
tasks.description:
Migrate `lib/workspace-types.ts` static `WORKSPACE_TYPE_REGISTRY` to TanStack Query against the `application_types` table, using the Option C hybrid metadata pattern ratified S251 (admin-editable copy in `application_types` DB columns; dev-editable routing/features in static client map in `hooks/use-application-types.ts`).

**Promoted retroactively from backlog ID-21 during main-track-S252.** Spec chain partially complete at promotion: RESEARCH (Subtask 29.1) captured inline via backlog notes (no formal RESEARCH.md authored); PRODUCT (Subtask 29.2) intentionally skipped per S251 ratification (refactor, no behavioural change); TECH (Subtask 29.3) authored + ratified S251 at `docs/specs/tanstack-workspace-types/TECH.md` (commit 8581bf4f); PLAN (Subtask 29.4) is the next step — WP5 dispatch this session.

## Acceptance criteria (carried from S251 ratified TECH.md)

- DDL migration: ADD COLUMN `label_plural` + `description` on `application_types` + backfill 6 seed rows.
- `useApplicationTypes()` TanStack hook + `/api/application-types` route created.
- 3 UI consumer migration: `app/workspaces/workspaces-content.tsx`, `components/workspace/workspace-card.tsx`, `components/workspace/workspace-create-dialog.tsx`.
- Delete static `WORKSPACE_TYPE_REGISTRY` + `registerType()` + `getWorkspaceType()` + `formatTypeCount()`; update `__tests__/lib/workspace-types.test.ts`; add `__tests__/hooks/use-application-types.test.ts`.
- `APPLICATION_TYPE_KEYS` hardcoded tuple preserved for sync Zod enum (`lib/validation/schemas.ts:535`) — async TanStack cannot back module-load Zod construction.

## Dependencies

None blocking. Spec chain ratified S251. Implementation slices decomposed by Subtask 29.4 PLAN this session.

Subtask status `deferred` chosen because SubtaskStatus schema (`task-list-schema.ts:44-48`) excludes `cancelled` at Subtask level — only Task-level can be `cancelled`. Semantic equivalent here is 'deliberately not authored' rather than 'postponed' — but `deferred` is the closest schema-permitted status.

subtasks.title: DDL migration — add label_plural + description columns + backfill 6 application_types seed rows + regen DB types
subtasks.description: Author and apply the Option C hybrid metadata DDL migration: ADD COLUMN `label_plural` + `description` on `application_types`; backfill the 6 seed rows verbatim per TECH.md §6; regen `supabase/types/database.types.ts`.
subtasks.details:
**TECH.md ref:** §6 (Migration plan) — full SQL template in TECH.md L412-483.

**Migration filename:** `supabase/migrations/<timestamp>_id_21_application_types_metadata_columns.sql` (use `/opt/homebrew/bin/supabase migration new id_21_application_types_metadata_columns` to scaffold).

**SQL shape (copy verbatim from TECH.md §6):**
- `SET search_path = public, extensions;` at the top (CLAUDE.md PG-function gotcha — though this migration adds no functions, set anyway for explicit-search-path discipline).
- `ALTER TABLE public.application_types ADD COLUMN label_plural text NULL, ADD COLUMN description text NULL;`
- Backfill UPDATEs for the 3 currently-rendered types (`procurement`, `intelligence`, `sales_proposal`) with verbatim values from the static `WORKSPACE_TYPE_REGISTRY` in `lib/workspace-types.ts` L81-136. Also backfills `default_icon` + `default_colour` for these 3 rows (currently NULL in DB).
- Default-backfill UPDATEs for the 3 non-rendered seed rows (`product_guide`, `competitor_research`, `training_onboarding`) — `label_plural = label || 's'`, `description = label`, NULL-coalesce guard.
- Post-check `DO $$` block asserts the 3 currently-rendered types have no NULLs in `label_plural`, `description`, `default_icon`, `default_colour`.

**No REVOKE block required** — this migration adds no `CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.*` (the anonymous `DO $$` block does not register as a callable function). CLAUDE.md S250 W1b anon-EXECUTE gotcha applies only to function creation.

**RLS / grants** — `application_types` table already has RLS enabled + `application_types_select_all FOR SELECT USING (true)` policy from T2 (migration `20260520120828` §1.1). New columns inherit the policy without modification.

**CLI apply discipline (CLAUDE.md gotchas — load-bearing):**
1. `cat supabase/.temp/project-ref` before each push (drift can silently push to wrong env).
2. Staging-apply first: `/opt/homebrew/bin/supabase db push --linked turayklvaunphgbgscat`.
3. Liam ratification gate.
4. Prod-apply: `/opt/homebrew/bin/supabase db push --linked rovrymhhffssilaftdwd`.
5. Regen types: `/opt/homebrew/bin/supabase gen types typescript --project-id rovrymhhffssilaftdwd --schema public > supabase/types/database.types.ts`.
6. Commit the regenerated `database.types.ts` in the same commit as the migration.

**Sandbox discipline:** ALL `supabase` CLI calls (migration new, db push, gen types) MUST use `dangerouslyDisableSandbox: true`. `POSTGRES_PASSWORD` must be set as a shell env var. Without sandbox-disable, the bun process hangs on HTTP 204 returns (CLAUDE.md gotcha).

**Post-apply verification (manual + scripted):**
- `SELECT key, label, label_plural, description, default_icon, default_colour FROM application_types ORDER BY key;` shows 6 rows.
- The 3 currently-rendered types (`procurement`, `intelligence`, `sales_proposal`) have non-NULL values for all 4 metadata fields.
- `supabase/types/database.types.ts` line ~17-52 `application_types` Row/Insert/Update now includes `label_plural: string | null` + `description: string | null`.

**Files touched:**
- `supabase/migrations/<timestamp>_id_21_application_types_metadata_columns.sql` (new)
- `supabase/types/database.types.ts` (regenerated; ~30-line diff on the application_types block)

**Verification gate after this Subtask:** staging + prod both clean; `application_types` row inspection shows 6 backfilled rows; database.types.ts regenerated and committed.

<info added on 2026-05-21T20:40:49.000Z>

Adds `label_plural text NULL` + `description text NULL` to `public.application_types`; backfills all 6 seed rows; post-check DO $$ block passed (0 NULL rows in rendered-type set). `supabase/types/database.types.ts` regenerated: `application_types` Row/Insert/Update now includes both new column triples.

**Files touched:** supabase/migrations/20260521203659_id_21_application_types_metadata_columns.sql, supabase/migrations/20260521203414_t8_op_id_propagation.sql (parallel T8 ID-28.5 stub — needed for CLI migration history consistency), supabase/types/database.types.ts, docs/reference/task-list.json

**Deviations:** (1) No `IF NOT EXISTS` guards on ADD COLUMN — TECH.md §6 verbatim does not include them (brief mentions idempotency discipline but TECH.md SQL is the authoritative source per brief). (2) `default_icon` for `intelligence` set to `'newspaper'` per TECH.md §6 — differs from `lib/workspace-types.ts` L130 which has `defaultIcon: 'globe'`; TECH.md verbatim takes precedence per brief. (3) Parallel T8 migration (20260521203414) was already applied to staging/prod by ID-28.5 executor; created local stub to repair CLI history; this stub will need to be reconciled at cherry-pick time (Orchestrator handles merge order per brief). (4) Supabase CLI appended update-notice text to types output — stripped before commit.

subtasks.title: Author useApplicationTypes() TanStack hook + /api/application-types route + queryKey namespace + hook tests
subtasks.description: Create the `useApplicationTypes()` TanStack Query hook + accompanying GET-only authenticated API route + queryKey namespace + the 6-case hook test suite per TECH.md §3 P-1, P-2, and P-5.
subtasks.details:
**TECH.md refs:** §3 P-1 (hook signature + cache key + fetcher), §3 P-2 (hook return shape + 3 sync helpers), §3 P-5 (test alignment for the new hook tests), §4 Option C (icon-name → LucideIcon static map + static client config for route/available/hasCustomCreation/features).

**Files to create:**

### 1. `hooks/workspaces/use-application-types.ts` (new)

Mirror the shape of `hooks/intelligence/use-company-profiles.ts` (single-fetch TanStack hook pattern, L33-47). Exports:
- `interface ApplicationTypeRow` — DB row shape (key, label, labelPlural, description, defaultIcon, defaultColour).
- `interface WorkspaceTypeConfig extends ApplicationTypeRow` — adds resolved `icon: LucideIcon`, `route: string | null`, `available: boolean`, `hasCustomCreation: boolean`, `features: { hasStatus, hasContentAssignment, hasDomainMetadata }`.
- `useApplicationTypes()` — `useQuery({ queryKey: queryKeys.applicationTypes.list, queryFn: () => fetchJson<ApplicationTypeRow[]>('/api/application-types'), select: (rows) => rows.map(toWorkspaceTypeConfig), staleTime: 5 * 60_000 })`.
- `useWorkspaceType(type: string)` — wraps `useApplicationTypes()` and `.find(c => c.key === type)`.
- `useLauncherTypes()` — wraps `useApplicationTypes()` and filters per current `getLauncherTypes()` semantics (`route !== null || !available`).
- `formatTypeCount(config: WorkspaceTypeConfig | undefined, n: number)` — pure-function sync utility, no hook.
- Internal static client config keyed by `application_types.key`: `route`, `available`, `hasCustomCreation`, `features.*` for the 3 currently-rendered types (procurement, intelligence, sales_proposal). Plus an icon-name → LucideIcon map for the `defaultIcon` strings (briefcase → Briefcase, newspaper → Newspaper, file-signature → FileSignature; default fallback → Folder).
- Internal `toWorkspaceTypeConfig(row: ApplicationTypeRow): WorkspaceTypeConfig` selector — joins DB row with static client config + resolves Lucide icon. Unknown keys (not in static map) get a permissive default (route=null, available=false, hasCustomCreation=false, features all false, icon=Folder) so the hook never returns an undefined-shape row.

**CLAUDE.md stable-empty-array discipline (R-6):** hoist module-level `const EMPTY_TYPES: WorkspaceTypeConfig[] = [];` and use it as the default when `data` is undefined. Do NOT inline `data ?? []` at consumer call sites — that breaks downstream React memoisation deps.

### 2. `app/api/application-types/route.ts` (new)

Mirror the shape of `app/api/intelligence/profiles/route.ts` GET handler. Single `GET` export:
- `getAuthorisedClient(['admin', 'editor', 'viewer'])` — authenticated, any role (TECH.md Q-3 ratification S251).
- Uses `authFailureResponse(auth)` for failure routing (CLAUDE.md auth pattern gotcha — discriminated union `{ success: boolean }`, never `{ authorised }`).
- `supabase.from('application_types').select('key, label, label_plural, description, default_icon, default_colour').order('label')`.
- Returns `NextResponse.json(data ?? [])`.
- Errors via `safeErrorMessage` per the route precedent.
- Camel-case mapping: the DB returns snake_case (`label_plural`, `default_icon`, `default_colour`); the route can either pass-through snake_case (then the hook's `toWorkspaceTypeConfig` selector maps snake→camel) OR map at the route layer. Recommended: route returns the snake_case shape verbatim from `database.types.ts`; the hook's `select:` selector handles the camel-case normalisation. This keeps the route a thin pass-through.

**NOTE on `ApplicationTypeRow` interface:** TECH.md §3 P-1 declares the interface with camelCase field names (`labelPlural`, `defaultIcon`, `defaultColour`). Decision: the interface is the *post-selector* shape; the route returns snake_case (matching `database.types.ts` Row), and the `select:` selector normalises to camelCase before consumers see it. Document this in the hook module header comment.

### 3. `lib/query/query-keys.ts` (edit — 1 namespace append)

Append to the exported `queryKeys` object (after the `workspaces` namespace at L135-140):

```ts
applicationTypes: {
  all: ['application-types'] as const,
  list: ['application-types', 'list'] as const,
},
```

### 4. `__tests__/hooks/workspaces/use-application-types.test.ts` (new)

Mirror existing TanStack hook test patterns (e.g. `__tests__/hooks/intelligence/use-workspace-flags.test.tsx` for QueryClient setup). Mock strategy: `createMockSupabaseClient()` from `__tests__/helpers/mock-supabase.ts`, returning the 6 seed rows verbatim with the Option C metadata grafted on. Coverage (6 cases per TECH.md §3 P-5):
1. Hook returns 6 rows when `application_types` table is mocked with 6 rows.
2. `useWorkspaceType('procurement')` resolves to a config with `label === 'Procurement'`, `route === '/procurement'`, `available === true`, `hasCustomCreation === true`, `icon === Briefcase` (resolved from string).
3. `useWorkspaceType('unknown_key')` resolves to `undefined` (preserves the current `getWorkspaceType()` contract).
4. `useLauncherTypes()` filters out types where `route === null && available === true` (preserves current `getLauncherTypes()` semantics).
5. `formatTypeCount(undefined, 0)` falls back to `'0 active workspaces'` (preserves current undefined-config branch).
6. `formatTypeCount(intelligenceConfig, 3)` returns `'3 active intelligence streams'` (preserves singular/plural logic).

**Coexistence note:** at this commit the hook + route + queryKey + tests all land but NO UI consumer uses them. The static `WORKSPACE_TYPE_REGISTRY` in `lib/workspace-types.ts` remains fully intact and still serves the 3 UI consumers. This is the intentional intermediate state per TECH.md §5 step 3 — gives the verification gate room to confirm the hook works in isolation before consumer migration.

**Files touched:**
- `hooks/workspaces/use-application-types.ts` (new)
- `app/api/application-types/route.ts` (new)
- `lib/query/query-keys.ts` (1 namespace append)
- `__tests__/hooks/workspaces/use-application-types.test.ts` (new)

**Verification gate after this Subtask:** `bun run test -- use-application-types` passes (6 cases). `bun lint` clean. `/workspaces` launcher still renders identically (no consumer change yet — the static registry still serves).

<info added on 2026-05-21T23:00:00.000Z>
**Wave 1 close — orchestrator promote to done.** Checker verdict on commit `371f07c0` (cherry-picked as `0c370954`) = **PASS_WITH_NOTES**. All 9 acceptance criteria pass. 3 in-scope nits curated to code-simplification pass at Task ID-29 close (per workflow quality-review gate): (1) useLauncherTypes exclusion-branch test coverage gap; (2) vi.stubGlobal vs createMockSupabaseClient mock-strategy clarification — architecturally correct (hook = HTTP boundary), spec amend candidate; (3) `use client` directive at L28 vs convention L1 — cosmetic. Test suite 12,736 pass / 7 fail (baseline carry, zero net-new) / 28 skip / 762 files post-cherry-pick.
</info added on 2026-05-21T23:00:00.000Z>

subtasks.title: Migrate 3 UI consumers to useApplicationTypes() hook (workspaces-content, workspace-card, workspace-create-dialog)
subtasks.description: Replace static `@/lib/workspace-types` imports with `@/hooks/workspaces/use-application-types` hook calls in the 3 UI consumer files per TECH.md §3 P-3a/3b/3c. Each migration preserves rendered behaviour byte-for-byte.
subtasks.details:
**TECH.md ref:** §3 P-3 (UI consumer migration patterns) — explicit before/after blocks for each of the 3 files.

### 1. `app/workspaces/workspaces-content.tsx` (edit; TECH.md §3 P-3a)

**Before (L4, L11, L28):**
```ts
import { getLauncherTypes, formatTypeCount } from '@/lib/workspace-types';
// L11:
const launcherTypes = getLauncherTypes();
// L28:
const countText = formatTypeCount(wt.type, count);
```

**After:**
```ts
import { useLauncherTypes, formatTypeCount } from '@/hooks/workspaces/use-application-types';
// L11:
const { data: launcherTypes = [], isLoading: _isLoading } = useLauncherTypes();
// L28:
const countText = formatTypeCount(wt, count);  // pass the config directly, not the type key
```

**Loading-state handling (TECH.md R-1):** the page is a server component (`app/workspaces/page.tsx`) that fetches counts on the server. The hook adds a render-time async fetch (max 1 round-trip on first paint, served from cache afterwards). On first paint, the existing empty-grid container renders (`launcherTypes = []` returns nothing from `.map()` — the surrounding `<div className="mt-8 grid …">` shows empty until hook resolves ~50ms). Acceptable per AC-3a.

### 2. `components/workspace/workspace-card.tsx` (edit; TECH.md §3 P-3b)

**Before (L18, L46):**
```ts
import { getWorkspaceType } from '@/lib/workspace-types';
// L46:
const typeConfig = workspace.type ? getWorkspaceType(workspace.type) : null;
```

**After:**
```ts
import { useWorkspaceType } from '@/hooks/workspaces/use-application-types';
// L46:
const { data: typeConfig = null } = useWorkspaceType(workspace.type ?? '');
```

The existing null-coalesce + `typeConfig &&` JSX guards at L75 and L84 handle the loading frame (`typeConfig === undefined`) the same way they handle the 'joined-row-absent' case today.

### 3. `components/workspace/workspace-create-dialog.tsx` (edit; TECH.md §3 P-3c)

**Before (L19, L50, L54):**
```ts
import { getWorkspaceType } from '@/lib/workspace-types';
// L50:
const typeConfig = getWorkspaceType(type);
// L54 (in useEffect):
if (open && typeConfig?.hasCustomCreation) { … }
```

**After:**
```ts
import { useWorkspaceType } from '@/hooks/workspaces/use-application-types';
// L50:
const { data: typeConfig } = useWorkspaceType(type);
// L54: useEffect deps unchanged — typeConfig identity changes when hook resolves, triggers effect.
if (open && typeConfig?.hasCustomCreation) { … }
```

**Hook caveat (TECH.md):** dialog opens via controlled `open` prop. On first open it may render once with `typeConfig === undefined` before hook resolves. The existing fallback at L126 (`typeConfig?.label ?? 'Workspace'`) and L128 (`typeConfig?.description ?? 'Create a new workspace.'`) cover this. Custom-creation `useEffect` fires correctly after data resolves (effect deps already include `typeConfig`).

### Post-migration verification (carry into 29.8 Subtask's zero-callers gate)

After all 3 files migrated, `grep -rn '@/lib/workspace-types' app/ components/ hooks/ contexts/` should return ONLY:
- `lib/validation/schemas.ts:3` (the sync constraint import — preserved per TECH.md §1 out-of-scope)
- `__tests__/lib/workspace-types.test.ts:14` (test file, to be pruned in 29.8)

Zero imports in `app/`, `components/`, `hooks/`, `contexts/`.

**No raw Tailwind colours discipline (CLAUDE.md):** none of these 3 files introduce new colour classes; they only swap imports and hook calls. No semantic-token work required.

**Files touched:**
- `app/workspaces/workspaces-content.tsx` (~5-line diff)
- `components/workspace/workspace-card.tsx` (~5-line diff)
- `components/workspace/workspace-create-dialog.tsx` (~5-line diff)

**Verification gate after this Subtask:** `/workspaces` launcher renders identically (counts + cards). Workspace card renders identically on browse page (type badge + label + arrow-up-right indicator). Workspace create dialog opens identically; if `type='procurement'` (default), custom-creation flow triggers as before. `bun run test` clean. `bun lint` clean. `bun run build` clean.

<info added on 2026-05-21T22:52:03.128Z>
**Shipped (this commit):** ID-29.7 — migrated 3 UI consumers from static `@/lib/workspace-types` to `useApplicationTypes` TanStack hook. Source files: `app/workspaces/workspaces-content.tsx` (getLauncherTypes→useLauncherTypes; `wt.type`→`wt.key` for counts/key/aria; formatTypeCount(wt,n) now passes resolved config object), `components/workspace/workspace-card.tsx` (getWorkspaceType→useWorkspaceType destructure-default-null), `components/workspace/workspace-create-dialog.tsx` (getWorkspaceType→useWorkspaceType; useEffect deps unchanged — typeConfig identity changes when hook resolves, triggers effect for custom-creation delegation).

**Acceptance vs testStrategy:**
- ✓ All 3 UI consumer files import from `@/hooks/workspaces/use-application-types` (zero references to `@/lib/workspace-types` in app/components/hooks/contexts).
- ✓ `grep -rn '@/lib/workspace-types' app/ components/ hooks/ contexts/` returns ZERO matches (verified post-migration).

**Deviations from brief (in-scope necessities for `testStrategy` compliance):**
1. **Test-file wrap with `QueryClientProvider` + `fetch` stub** — the 3 component tests (`__tests__/components/workspace-card.test.tsx`, `__tests__/components/workspace-create-dialog.test.tsx`, `__tests__/app/workspaces/workspaces-content.test.tsx`) and the closely-coupled `__tests__/app/workspaces/workspaces-launcher.test.tsx` previously relied on the static module-init registry resolving synchronously. Once the consumers use `useQuery`, jsdom renders throw `No QueryClient set, use QueryClientProvider to set one`. Added a local `createWrapper()` helper + `beforeEach`/`afterEach` `vi.stubGlobal('fetch', ...)` returning the 6 application_types seed rows verbatim (snake_case) — mirroring the pattern in `__tests__/hooks/workspaces/use-application-types.test.ts`. 2. **Test fixture key swap `proposal` → `sales_proposal`** — pre-migration, the static registry exposed `proposal` (label "Sales Proposal"); the DB seed uses `sales_proposal`. Three pre-existing test cases referenced `type: 'proposal'` (workspace-card "does not show arrow icon for types without a route"; 3× workspace-create-dialog "sales_proposal type — no custom creation"). Updated those fixtures to `type: 'sales_proposal'` so the hook resolves to the DB row. Visual rendered output is identical (label "Sales Proposal", description matches DB backfill, route null, available false, hasCustomCreation false from CLIENT_CONFIG).
3. **Field rename `wt.type` → `wt.key` in `workspaces-content.tsx` (3 sites)** — the hook's `WorkspaceTypeConfig` uses `key` (matching DB column), not `type` (the pre-refactor field name). The brief's "After" block at L28 — `formatTypeCount(wt, count)` — only works against `WorkspaceTypeConfig` shape so this rename was implied. Sites updated: counts lookup (L27), React key on `<Link>` (L33), React key on coming-soon `<div>` (L60).

**Out-of-scope observations (for Curator triage):**

Verified pre-existing via `git stash && bunx tsc --noEmit` on bare track branch. NOT introduced by this Subtask; my migrated files have zero TS errors. Likely a generated-types staleness or a regen artifact from a prior unrelated commit. Should be triaged to a regen-types Subtask.
- **`__tests__/lib/date-extraction-integration.test.ts` vi.hoisted/vi.mock warnings** — emitted by `bun run test --changed`. Pre-existing, unrelated to this Subtask.
- **Static registry deletion is ID-29.8 scope** (per TECH.md §5 step 5 + brief). The static `WORKSPACE_TYPE_REGISTRY`, inline `registerType({…})` calls, `getWorkspaceType()`, `getAllWorkspaceTypes()`, `getLauncherTypes()`, `formatTypeCount()`, and the `WorkspaceTypeConfig` interface in `lib/workspace-types.ts` are now orphaned UI metadata — only `APPLICATION_TYPE_KEYS` and `getValidTypeValues()` are still consumed (by `lib/validation/schemas.ts:3`). Ready for the next Subtask's pruning pass.

- `bun run test __tests__/components/workspace-card.test.tsx __tests__/components/workspace-create-dialog.test.tsx __tests__/app/workspaces/workspaces-content.test.tsx __tests__/app/workspaces/workspaces-launcher.test.tsx` → 41/41 PASS.

**TECH.md refs:** §3 P-4 (static registry deletion), §3 P-5 (test alignment for the legacy test file).

### Zero-callers verification gate (run BEFORE any delete)

For each symbol about to be deleted, confirm zero remaining call sites via `grep -rn` across `app/`, `components/`, `lib/`, `hooks/`, `contexts/`, `__tests__/`. Optional: `bun scripts/ast-dataflow-cli.ts callers <symbol>` for type-checker-resolved verification.

Expected: every deleted symbol returns ZERO matches outside `lib/workspace-types.ts` itself. If any match exists, halt and surface — the migration in 29.7 missed a site (this should not occur given 29.7's full inventory was confirmed pre-decomposition).

The ONLY surviving `@/lib/workspace-types` import in the codebase should be `lib/validation/schemas.ts:3` (`import { getValidTypeValues } from '@/lib/workspace-types'`).

### Deletions from `lib/workspace-types.ts`

Per TECH.md §3 P-4 deletion table:

| Symbol                              | Lines (current) | Deletion gate                              |
| ----------------------------------- | --------------- | ------------------------------------------ |
| `WorkspaceTypeConfig` interface     | L18-61          | Moved into `use-application-types.ts` in 29.6 |
| `WORKSPACE_TYPE_REGISTRY` const     | L64             | Zero remaining importers                   |
| `registerType()`                    | L67-73          | Zero remaining callers (all 3 inline)      |
| Inline `registerType({…})` ×3       | L81-136         | Zero remaining callers                     |
| `getWorkspaceType()`                | L141-145        | Replaced by `useWorkspaceType()` hook      |
| `getAllWorkspaceTypes()`            | L148-150        | Subsumed by `useApplicationTypes().data`   |
| `getLauncherTypes()`                | L153-157        | Replaced by `useLauncherTypes()` hook      |
| `formatTypeCount()`                 | L186-192        | Re-exported from `use-application-types.ts` in 29.6 |
| `logger` import                     | L3              | Deleted with `registerType()`              |
| Lucide icon imports + `LucideIcon`  | L1-2            | Moved into icon-name map in hook module in 29.6 |

### Retained verbatim in `lib/workspace-types.ts`

- `APPLICATION_TYPE_KEYS` tuple at L166-173 — DO NOT TOUCH.
- `getValidTypeValues()` function at L181-183 — DO NOT TOUCH.
- Top-of-file comment block (L1-11) — UPDATE to reflect Path c completion. Replace the existing comment with a header stating: 'Post-ID-29 (S252+): sync source of truth for `application_types.key` values, paired with the DB seed in `application_types`. Used by `lib/validation/schemas.ts` to construct Zod enum at module-load. The UI metadata surface (label, labelPlural, description, icon, route, available, hasCustomCreation, features, defaultColour, defaultIcon) lives in `hooks/workspaces/use-application-types.ts` as a TanStack Query hook over `application_types` DB rows joined with a static client config (Option C hybrid per docs/specs/tanstack-workspace-types/TECH.md). Add or retire a seed key: update both `APPLICATION_TYPE_KEYS` AND `application_types` table seed in lockstep.'

Post-deletion, `lib/workspace-types.ts` is ~25 lines.

### Prune `__tests__/lib/workspace-types.test.ts` (TECH.md §3 P-5)

Delete the following `describe()` blocks:
- `describe('getWorkspaceType')`
- `describe('getAllWorkspaceTypes')`
- `describe('getLauncherTypes')`
- `describe('formatTypeCount')`

Retain ONLY:
- `describe('getValidTypeValues')` block (tuple shape + 6-key invariant)

Update top-of-file imports: remove `getWorkspaceType, getAllWorkspaceTypes, getLauncherTypes, formatTypeCount` from the import statement at L7-14; retain only `getValidTypeValues`.

The new hook-test coverage lives in `__tests__/hooks/workspaces/use-application-types.test.ts` (authored in 29.6).

### Final regression sweep

After deletes + test prune:
1. `bun run test` — full suite passes.
2. `bun lint` — clean.
3. `bun run build` — clean (no TS errors from missing exports).
4. `bun run knip` — no new unused-export findings (the 5 deleted symbols + `logger` import + Lucide imports should fully drop out).
5. `grep -rn '@/lib/workspace-types' .` — returns ONLY `lib/validation/schemas.ts:3` and `__tests__/lib/workspace-types.test.ts:` (test file — narrowed to `getValidTypeValues` import).
6. Manual smoke: `/workspaces` launcher renders identically; workspace-card renders identically; workspace-create-dialog opens identically.

- `lib/workspace-types.ts` (delete ~168 lines, retain ~25 lines, update header comment)
- `__tests__/lib/workspace-types.test.ts` (delete ~4 describe blocks, retain `getValidTypeValues` coverage)

tasks.title: Task-list description discipline + content audit
tasks.description:
Audit current 22 Tasks for description/details length + propose per-field discipline. Carryover from S62 W3 Liam concern: task-list Task descriptions trending too long (ID-30 + ID-31 descriptions ~3000+ chars each).

Guiding principle: documentation is canonical reference (`docs/specs/`, `docs/research/`, `docs/reference/`); task-list shows implementation/test steps + traceability context only. Per-field discipline TBD — distinguish what belongs in `description` (compact what+why) vs `status_note` (acute carryover state) vs `details` (Subtask-level dispatch brief slice) vs `testStrategy` (one-line acceptance) vs `cross_doc_links` (canonical-ref anchors).

## Acceptance criteria

- Audit of 22 Tasks with length distribution per field.
- Per-field discipline guidelines codified in `docs/reference/task-list-discipline.md` (or extension of `docs/reference/taskmaster-schema-reference.md`).
- workflow-orchestration / write-product-spec / write-tech-spec / planning-and-task-breakdown skills updated to honour discipline.
- One retroactive sweep tightens existing Task descriptions per new discipline.

## Dependencies

None (audit-first; can run independently of ID-30 / ID-31 spec chains).

tasks.title: Ledger mutation CLI — thin wrapper over task-view patch primitives
tasks.description: Replace inline-Python `/tmp/claude/*.py` scripts the Orchestrator currently writes per ledger mutation (open Task / flip status / append journal / Promote) with a deterministic CLI invocation. S64 investigation (ii) recommended HYBRID approach: thin `bun scripts/ledger-cli.ts` wrapper over the already-shipped task-view `applyPatches` + `atomicWriteFile` primitives (ID-20.8 WP5b PASS). Surface area: 10 subcommands (open-task, flip-task, flip-subtask, add-subtask, append-journal, promote, create-backlog, update-backlog, delete-backlog, show). 4 of 10 subcommands (open-task, create-backlog, delete-backlog, promote) require NEW record-level primitives gated on ID-20.15 (patch-surface extension) per S62E sub-o 2 audit §2 (docs/research/id-35-crossover-audit.md). REJECTED alternatives: (A) build CLI from scratch — duplicates ID-20.8 work; (B) adopt Taskmaster — fit-gap too wide (KH bare-digit IDs / sibling-only deps / MoSCoW+Ranked+Trigger priority / Promote primitive / <info added on…> blocks / 25-Subtask ceiling — none match TM). Sequence: AFTER ID-34 (CLI must honour field discipline) and AFTER ID-20.15 (record-level patch primitives shipped). ID-22 cross-ref sweep is INDEPENDENT of this CLI (102 files repo-wide per S62E sub-o 2 audit §4); CLI's LEDGER_DIR config helps post-relocation runtime consumers only. First dogfood: open this Task itself via the CLI's Promote primitive when implemented (self-bootstrapping).

subtasks.title: S250 W1b followup — anon EXECUTE leak via PUBLIC inheritance fix
subtasks.description: Discovered during S250 W1 acceptance audit: REVOKE EXECUTE FROM anon does not block anon access because Postgres PUBLIC inherits EXECUTE on functions by default. Fix REVOKEs EXECUTE FROM PUBLIC before re-granting to specific roles. Applied to BOTH staging + prod via CLI db push.
